{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_dc826cfaf5f042c586efd054","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t// Package api provides REST API handlers for Spaxel notification channels.\n2\tpackage api\n3\t\n4\timport (\n5\t\t\"database/sql\"\n6\t\t\"encoding/json\"\n7\t\t\"log\"\n8\t\t\"net/http\"\n9\t\t\"os\"\n10\t\t\"path/filepath\"\n11\t\t\"sync\"\n12\t\t\"time\"\n13\t\n14\t\t\"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5\"\n15\t\t_ \"modernc.org/sqlite\"\n16\t)\n17\t\n18\t// NotificationsHandler manages notification delivery channels.\n19\t// Supported channel types: ntfy, pushover, gotify, webhook, mqtt.\n20\ttype NotificationsHandler struct {\n21\t\tmu            sync.RWMutex\n22\t\tdb            *sql.DB\n23\t\tchannels      map[string]*NotificationChannel\n24\t\tnotifyService NotifySender\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t// NotificationChannel represents a notification delivery channel configuration.\n28\t//\n29\t// Channel types and their config schemas:\n30\t//\n31\t//\tntfy:    {\"url\":\"https://ntfy.sh/my-topic\", \"token\":\"tk_...\"} (token optional)\n32\t//\tpushover: {\"app_token\":\"aXXXXXX...\",\"user_key\":\"uXXXXXX...\"}\n33\t//\tgotify:  {\"url\":\"https://gotify.example.com\",\"token\":\"Aq7mXXXX\"}\n34\t//\twebhook: {\"url\":\"https://example.com/hook\",\"method\":\"POST\",\"headers\":{\"X-Secret\":\"abc\"}}\n35\t//\tmqtt:    {} (uses global MQTT connection; no config needed)\n36\ttype NotificationChannel struct {\n37\t\tType    string      `json:\"type\"`             // ntfy, pushover, gotify, webhook, mqtt\n38\t\tEnabled bool        `json:\"enabled\"`          // true if channel is active\n39\t\tConfig  interface{} `json:\"config,omitempty\"` // channel-specific configuration\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\t// NotifySender is the interface for sending test notifications.\n43\ttype NotifySender interface {\n44\t\tSend(title, body string, data map[string]interface{}) error\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\t// NewNotificationsHandler creates a new notifications handler.\n48\tfunc NewNotificationsHandler(dbPath string) (*NotificationsHandler, error) {\n49\t\tif err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0755); err != nil {\n50\t\t\treturn nil, err\n51\t\t}\n52\t\n53\t\tdb, err := sql.Open(\"sqlite\", dbPath)\n54\t\tif err != nil {\n55\t\t\treturn nil, err\n56\t\t}\n57\t\tdb.SetMaxOpenConns(1)\n58\t\n59\t\tn := &NotificationsHandler{\n60\t\t\tdb:       db,\n61\t\t\tchannels: make(map[string]*NotificationChannel),\n62\t\t}\n63\t\n64\t\tif err := n.migrate(); err != nil {\n65\t\t\tdb.Close()\n66\t\t\treturn nil, err\n67\t\t}\n68\t\n69\t\tif err := n.load(); err != nil {\n70\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to load notification channels: %v\", err)\n71\t\t}\n72\t\n73\t\treturn n, nil\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) migrate() error {\n77\t\t_, err := n.db.Exec(`\n78\t\t\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_channels (\n79\t\t\t\ttype        TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n80\t\t\t\tenabled     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n81\t\t\t\tconfig_json TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',\n82\t\t\t\tupdated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch() * 1000)\n83\t\t\t);\n84\t\t`)\n85\t\treturn err\n86\t}\n87\t\n88\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) load() error {\n89\t\trows, err := n.db.Query(`SELECT type, enabled, config_json FROM notification_channels`)\n90\t\tif err != nil {\n91\t\t\treturn err\n92\t\t}\n93\t\tdefer rows.Close()\n94\t\n95\t\tfor rows.Next() {\n96\t\t\tvar nc NotificationChannel\n97\t\t\tvar enabled int\n98\t\t\tvar configJSON string\n99\t\n100\t\t\tif err := rows.Scan(&nc.Type, &enabled, &configJSON); err != nil {\n101\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to scan notification channel: %v\", err)\n102\t\t\t\tcontinue\n103\t\t\t}\n104\t\n105\t\t\tnc.Enabled = enabled != 0\n106\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(configJSON), &nc.Config); err != nil {\n107\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to unmarshal config for %s: %v\", nc.Type, err)\n108\t\t\t\t// Keep as raw JSON string if unmarshaling fails\n109\t\t\t\tnc.Config = configJSON\n110\t\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\t\tn.channels[nc.Type] = &nc\n113\t\t}\n114\t\n115\t\treturn nil\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t// Close closes the database.\n119\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) Close() error {\n120\t\treturn n.db.Close()\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\t// SetNotifyService sets the notification sender for test notifications.\n124\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) SetNotifyService(ns NotifySender) {\n125\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n126\t\tdefer n.mu.Unlock()\n127\t\tn.notifyService = ns\n128\t}\n129\t\n130\t// GetChannels returns a copy of all notification channels.\n131\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) GetChannels() map[string]*NotificationChannel {\n132\t\tn.mu.RLock()\n133\t\tdefer n.mu.RUnlock()\n134\t\n135\t\tresult := make(map[string]*NotificationChannel, len(n.channels))\n136\t\tfor k, v := range n.channels {\n137\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n138\t\t}\n139\t\treturn result\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t// GetChannel returns a single channel by type.\n143\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) GetChannel(channelType string) (*NotificationChannel, bool) {\n144\t\tn.mu.RLock()\n145\t\tdefer n.mu.RUnlock()\n146\t\tch, ok := n.channels[channelType]\n147\t\treturn ch, ok\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\t// SetChannel updates or creates a notification channel.\n151\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) SetChannel(channelType string, enabled bool, config interface{}) error {\n152\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n153\t\tdefer n.mu.Unlock()\n154\t\n155\t\t// Validate config based on channel type\n156\t\tif err := validateChannelConfig(channelType, config); err != nil {\n157\t\t\treturn err\n158\t\t}\n159\t\n160\t\tconfigJSON, err := json.Marshal(config)\n161\t\tif err != nil {\n162\t\t\treturn err\n163\t\t}\n164\t\n165\t\tenabledInt := 0\n166\t\tif enabled {\n167\t\t\tenabledInt = 1\n168\t\t}\n169\t\n170\t\tnow := time.Now().UnixMilli()\n171\t\t_, err = n.db.Exec(`\n172\t\t\tINSERT INTO notification_channels (type, enabled, config_json, updated_at)\n173\t\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\n174\t\t\tON CONFLICT(type) DO UPDATE SET enabled = ?, config_json = ?, updated_at = ?\n175\t\t`, channelType, enabledInt, string(configJSON), now,\n176\t\t\tenabledInt, string(configJSON), now)\n177\t\tif err != nil {\n178\t\t\treturn err\n179\t\t}\n180\t\n181\t\tn.channels[channelType] = &NotificationChannel{\n182\t\t\tType:    channelType,\n183\t\t\tEnabled: enabled,\n184\t\t\tConfig:  config,\n185\t\t}\n186\t\n187\t\treturn nil\n188\t}\n189\t\n190\t// validateChannelConfig validates the configuration for a specific channel type.\n191\tfunc validateChannelConfig(channelType string, config interface{}) error {\n192\t\tif config == nil {\n193\t\t\t// Nil config is valid for mqtt, optional for others\n194\t\t\treturn nil\n195\t\t}\n196\t\n197\t\tconfigMap, ok := config.(map[string]interface{})\n198\t\tif !ok {\n199\t\t\t// Try to unmarshal as JSON\n200\t\t\tjsonBytes, err := json.Marshal(config)\n201\t\t\tif err != nil {\n202\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Reason: \"config must be a JSON object\"}\n203\t\t\t}\n204\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(jsonBytes, &configMap); err != nil {\n205\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Reason: \"config must be a JSON object\"}\n206\t\t\t}\n207\t\t}\n208\t\n209\t\tswitch channelType {\n210\t\tcase \"ntfy\":\n211\t\t\t// url is required, token is optional\n212\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n213\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n214\t\t\t}\n215\t\tcase \"pushover\":\n216\t\t\t// app_token and user_key are required\n217\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"app_token\"]; !ok {\n218\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"app_token\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n219\t\t\t}\n220\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"user_key\"]; !ok {\n221\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"user_key\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n222\t\t\t}\n223\t\tcase \"gotify\":\n224\t\t\t// url and token are required\n225\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n226\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n227\t\t\t}\n228\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"token\"]; !ok {\n229\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"token\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n230\t\t\t}\n231\t\tcase \"webhook\":\n232\t\t\t// url is required, method and headers are optional\n233\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n234\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\t\t// Validate method if provided\n237\t\t\tif method, ok := configMap[\"method\"].(string); ok {\n238\t\t\t\tif method != \"GET\" && method != \"POST\" {\n239\t\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"method\", Reason: \"must be GET or POST\"}\n240\t\t\t\t}\n241\t\t\t}\n242\t\tcase \"mqtt\":\n243\t\t\t// No config required for mqtt (uses global connection)\n244\t\tdefault:\n245\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Reason: \"unknown channel type\"}\n246\t\t}\n247\t\n248\t\treturn nil\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ChannelValidationError represents a configuration validation error.\n252\ttype ChannelValidationError struct {\n253\t\tType   string\n254\t\tField  string\n255\t\tReason string\n256\t}\n257\t\n258\tfunc (e *ChannelValidationError) Error() string {\n259\t\tif e.Field != \"\" {\n260\t\t\treturn e.Type + \".\" + e.Field + \": \" + e.Reason\n261\t\t}\n262\t\treturn e.Type + \": \" + e.Reason\n263\t}\n264\t\n265\t// RegisterRoutes registers notification endpoints.\n266\t//\n267\t// Notification Channels Endpoints:\n268\t//\n269\t// The notification channels API manages delivery channels for alerts and notifications.\n270\t// Supported channel types: ntfy, pushover, gotify, webhook, mqtt.\n271\t//\n272\t// GET /api/notifications/config\n273\t//\n274\t//\t@Summary\t\tGet notification channel configuration\n275\t//\t@Description\tReturns all notification channel configurations including enabled status and channel-specific settings.\n276\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tnotifications\n277\t//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n278\t//\t@Success\t\t200\t{object}\tnotificationConfigResponse\t\"Channel configurations\"\n279\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/notifications/config [get]\n280\t//\n281\t// POST /api/notifications/config\n282\t//\n283\t//\t@Summary\t\tUpdate notification channel configuration\n284\t//\t@Description\tUpdates one or more notification channel configurations. Each channel has type-specific required fields.\n285\t//\t@Description\t<br>ntfy: requires \"url\", optional \"token\"<br>\n286\t//\t@Description\tpushover: requires \"app_token\", \"user_key\"<br>\n287\t//\t@Description\tgotify: requires \"url\", \"token\"<br>\n288\t//\t@Description\twebhook: requires \"url\", optional \"method\" (GET/POST), optional \"headers\"<br>\n289\t//\t@Description\tmqtt: no config required (uses global MQTT connection)\n290\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tnotifications\n291\t//\t@Accept\t\t\tjson\n292\t//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n293\t//\t@Param\t\t\trequest\tbody\t\tsetNotificationConfigRequest\ttrue\t\"Channel configurations to update\"\n294\t//\t@Success\t\t200\t{object}\tnotificationConfigResponse\t\"Updated channel configurations\"\n295\t//\t@Failure\t\t400\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Invalid request body or validation error\"\n296\t//\t@Failure\t\t500\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Failed to save configuration\"\n297\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/notifications/config [post]\n298\t//\n299\t// POST /api/notifications/test\n300\t//\n301\t//\t@Summary\t\tSend a test notification\n302\t//\t@Description\tSends a test notification via the specified channel type. The channel must be enabled.\n303\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tnotifications\n304\t//\t@Accept\t\t\tjson\n305\t//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n306\t//\t@Param\t\t\trequest\tbody\t\ttestNotificationRequest\ttrue\t\"Test notification parameters\"\n307\t//\t@Success\t\t200\t{object}\tmap[string]interface{}\t\"Test result\"\n308\t//\t@Failure\t\t400\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Invalid request or no enabled channel\"\n309\t//\t@Failure\t\t500\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Failed to send notification\"\n310\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/notifications/test [post]\n311\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n312\t\tr.Get(\"/api/notifications/config\", n.handleGetConfig)\n313\t\tr.Post(\"/api/notifications/config\", n.handleSetConfig)\n314\t\tr.Post(\"/api/notifications/test\", n.handleSendTest)\n315\t}\n316\t\n317\t// notificationConfigResponse is the response for channel configuration requests.\n318\ttype notificationConfigResponse struct {\n319\t\tChannels map[string]*NotificationChannel `json:\"channels\"`\n320\t}\n321\t\n322\t// handleGetConfig handles GET /api/notifications/config requests.\n323\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) handleGetConfig(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n324\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, notificationConfigResponse{\n325\t\t\tChannels: n.GetChannels(),\n326\t\t})\n327\t}\n328\t\n329\t// setNotificationConfigRequest is the request body for setting channel configuration.\n330\ttype setNotificationConfigRequest struct {\n331\t\tChannels map[string]struct {\n332\t\t\tType    string      `json:\"type\"`\n333\t\t\tEnabled bool        `json:\"enabled\"`\n334\t\t\tConfig  interface{} `json:\"config,omitempty\"`\n335\t\t} `json:\"channels\"`\n336\t}\n337\t\n338\t// handleSetConfig handles POST /api/notifications/config requests.\n339\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) handleSetConfig(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n340\t\tvar req setNotificationConfigRequest\n341\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n342\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid request body: \"+err.Error())\n343\t\t\treturn\n344\t\t}\n345\t\n346\t\t// Validate and update each channel\n347\t\tfor channelType, ch := range req.Channels {\n348\t\t\tif ch.Type == \"\" {\n349\t\t\t\tch.Type = channelType // Use map key as type if not specified\n350\t\t\t}\n351\t\t\tif ch.Type != channelType {\n352\t\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"channel type mismatch: key is \"+channelType+\" but body specifies \"+ch.Type)\n353\t\t\t\treturn\n354\t\t\t}\n355\t\n356\t\t\tif err := n.SetChannel(ch.Type, ch.Enabled, ch.Config); err != nil {\n357\t\t\t\tif ce, ok := err.(*ChannelValidationError); ok {\n358\t\t\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, ce.Error())\n359\t\t\t\t} else {\n360\t\t\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to save configuration: \"+err.Error())\n361\t\t\t\t}\n362\t\t\t\treturn\n363\t\t\t}\n364\t\t}\n365\t\n366\t\tn.handleGetConfig(w, r)\n367\t}\n368\t\n369\t// testNotificationRequest is the request body for sending a test notification.\n370\ttype testNotificationRequest struct {\n371\t\tChannelType string                 `json:\"channel_type\"` // ntfy, pushover, gotify, webhook, mqtt\n372\t\tTitle       string                 `json:\"title\"`        // Custom title (optional)\n373\t\tBody        string                 `json:\"body\"`         // Custom body (optional)\n374\t\tData        map[string]interface{} `json:\"data,omitempty\"` // Additional data (optional)\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t// testNotificationResponse is the response for a test notification.\n378\ttype testNotificationResponse struct {\n379\t\tStatus  string `json:\"status\"`  // \"sent\" or \"simulated\"\n380\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"` // Human-readable result\n381\t}\n382\t\n383\t// handleSendTest handles POST /api/notifications/test requests.\n384\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) handleSendTest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n385\t\tvar req testNotificationRequest\n386\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n387\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid request body: \"+err.Error())\n388\t\t\treturn\n389\t\t}\n390\t\n391\t\t// Set defaults\n392\t\tif req.Title == \"\" {\n393\t\t\treq.Title = \"Spaxel Test Notification\"\n394\t\t}\n395\t\tif req.Body == \"\" {\n396\t\t\treq.Body = \"This is a test notification from Spaxel.\"\n397\t\t}\n398\t\tif req.Data == nil {\n399\t\t\treq.Data = make(map[string]interface{})\n400\t\t}\n401\t\treq.Data[\"test\"] = true\n402\t\n403\t\t// Check if channel type exists and is enabled\n404\t\tch, ok := n.GetChannel(req.ChannelType)\n405\t\tif !ok {\n406\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"unknown channel type: \"+req.ChannelType)\n407\t\t\treturn\n408\t\t}\n409\t\tif !ch.Enabled {\n410\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"channel is not enabled: \"+req.ChannelType)\n411\t\t\treturn\n412\t\t}\n413\t\n414\t\t// Send test notification\n415\t\tn.mu.RLock()\n416\t\tsender := n.notifyService\n417\t\tn.mu.RUnlock()\n418\t\n419\t\tif sender == nil {\n420\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, testNotificationResponse{\n421\t\t\t\tStatus:  \"simulated\",\n422\t\t\t\tMessage: \"Test notification simulated (no sender attached)\",\n423\t\t\t})\n424\t\t\treturn\n425\t\t}\n426\t\n427\t\tif err := sender.Send(req.Title, req.Body, req.Data); err != nil {\n428\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to send notification: \"+err.Error())\n429\t\t\treturn\n430\t\t}\n431\t\n432\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, testNotificationResponse{\n433\t\t\tStatus:  \"sent\",\n434\t\t\tMessage: \"Test notification sent successfully\",\n435\t\t})\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\t// ── Notification sending (called by automation engine) ────────────────────────────\n439\t\n440\t// SendNotification sends a notification via all enabled channels.\n441\tfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) SendNotification(title, body string, data map[string]interface{}) error {\n442\t\tn.mu.RLock()\n443\t\tsender := n.notifyService\n444\t\tchannels := make([]NotificationChannel, 0, len(n.channels))\n445\t\tfor _, ch := range n.channels {\n446\t\t\tif ch.Enabled {\n447\t\t\t\tchannels = append(channels, *ch)\n448\t\t\t}\n449\t\t}\n450\t\tn.mu.RUnlock()\n451\t\n452\t\tif len(channels) == 0 {\n453\t\t\treturn nil\n454\t\t}\n455\t\n456\t\tif sender == nil {\n457\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] No notification sender attached, skipping: %s\", title)\n458\t\t\treturn nil\n459\t\t}\n460\t\n461\t\treturn sender.Send(title, body, data)\n462\t}\n463\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_751c7b698c8b4faeb9495bc9","session_id":"e969917b-904e-4516-80e4-c0016537e6d7","uuid":"a7449c63-4fc5-43a2-b1f6-b4f939e1612b","timestamp":"2026-04-25T12:46:15.287Z"}

Data directory: verify /data is writable; acquire flock() lock\n23\t//   2. SQLite: open database with WAL mode, busy_timeout=5000\n24\t//   3. Schema migration: apply pending migrations with backup\n25\t//   4. Config & secrets: load/generate install secret\n26\t//\n27\t// The parentCtx should be the startup timeout context from main so that all\n28\t// phases share the same 30-second deadline. If parentCtx is nil, a fresh\n29\t// context with TotalTimeout is created.\n30\t//\n31\t// If any phase fails, the function returns an error and the caller should\n32\t// exit without serving traffic.\n33\tfunc OpenDB(parentCtx context.Context, dataDir, dbName string) (*sql.DB, error) {\n34\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n35\t\tctx := parentCtx\n36\t\tif ctx == nil {\n37\t\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), startup.TotalTimeout)\n38\t\t\tdefer cancel()\n39\t\t}\n40\t\n41\t\t// Phase 1: Data directory + flock\n42\t\tstartup.CheckTimeout(ctx)\n43\t\tdone := startup.Phase(1, \"Data directory\")\n44\t\tdbPath := filepath.Join(dataDir, dbName)\n45\t\tif err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0755); err != nil {\n46\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"create data dir: %w\", err)\n47\t\t}\n48\t\n49\t\t// Create floorplan storage directory\n50\t\tfloorplanDir := filepath.Join(dataDir, \"floorplan\")\n51\t\tif err := os.MkdirAll(floorplanDir, 0755); err != nil {\n52\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"create floorplan dir: %w\", err)\n53\t\t}\n54\t\n55\t\t// Acquire exclusive flock to prevent duplicate instances\n56\t\tlockPath := filepath.Join(dataDir, \".lock\")\n57\t\tlockFile, err := os.OpenFile(lockPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)\n58\t\tif err != nil {\n59\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"create lock file: %w\", err)\n60\t\t}\n61\t\tif err := syscall.Flock(int(lockFile.Fd()), syscall.LOCK_EX|syscall.LOCK_NB); err != nil {\n62\t\t\tlockFile.Close()\n63\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"acquire flock on %s (another instance running?): %w\", lockPath, err)\n64\t\t}\n65\t\tdone()\n66\t\n67\t\t// Phase 2: SQLite open\n68\t\tstartup.CheckTimeout(ctx)\n69\t\tdone = startup.Phase(2, \"SQLite\")\n70\t\tdb, err := sql.Open(\"sqlite\", dbPath+\"?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=synchronous(NORMAL)&_pragma=foreign_keys(ON)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)\")\n71\t\tif err != nil {\n72\t\t\tlockFile.Close()\n73\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"open sqlite: %w\", err)\n74\t\t}\n75\t\tdb.SetMaxOpenConns(1) // SQLite is single-writer\n76\t\n77\t\tif err := db.PingContext(ctx); err != nil {\n78\t\t\tdb.Close()\n79\t\t\tlockFile.Close()\n80\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ping database: %w\", err)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\t// Integrity check\n84\t\tvar integrityResult string\n85\t\terr = db.QueryRowContext(ctx, \"PRAGMA integrity_check\").Scan(&integrityResult)\n86\t\tif err != nil || integrityResult != \"ok\" {\n87\t\t\tcorruptPath := dbPath + \".corrupt.\" + time.Now().Format(\"20060102-150405\")\n88\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] SQLite integrity check failed (%s), moving to %s and starting fresh\", integrityResult, corruptPath)\n89\t\t\tdb.Close()\n90\t\t\tif renameErr := os.Rename(dbPath, corruptPath); renameErr != nil {\n91\t\t\t\tlockFile.Close()\n92\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"move corrupt database: %w\", renameErr)\n93\t\t\t}\n94\t\t\tdb, err = sql.Open(\"sqlite\", dbPath+\"?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=synchronous(NORMAL)&_pragma=foreign_keys(ON)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)\")\n95\t\t\tif err != nil {\n96\t\t\t\tlockFile.Close()\n97\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"open fresh sqlite: %w\", err)\n98\t\t\t}\n99\t\t\tdb.SetMaxOpenConns(1)\n100\t\t}\n101\t\tdone()\n102\t\n103\t\t// Phase 3: Schema migration\n104\t\tstartup.CheckTimeout(ctx)\n105\t\tdone = startup.Phase(3, \"Schema migrations\")\n106\t\tmigrator, err := NewMigrator(dbPath, Config{\n107\t\t\tDataDir:         dataDir,\n108\t\t\tBackupRetention: 90 * 24 * time.Hour,\n109\t\t})\n110\t\tif err != nil {\n111\t\t\tdb.Close()\n112\t\t\tlockFile.Close()\n113\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"create migrator: %w\", err)\n114\t\t}\n115\t\tmigrator.Register(AllMigrations()...)\n116\t\n117\t\tcurrent, err := migrator.CurrentVersion(ctx)\n118\t\tif err != nil {\n119\t\t\tdb.Close()\n120\t\t\tlockFile.Close()\n121\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"get current version: %w\", err)\n122\t\t}\n123\t\n124\t\tif current == 0 {\n125\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Initializing new database\")\n126\t\t} else {\n127\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Current schema version %d\", current)\n128\t\t}\n129\t\n130\t\tif err := migrator.Migrate(ctx); err != nil {\n150\t\t\tdb.Close()\n151\t\t\tlockFile.Close()\n152\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ensure install secret: %w\", err)\n153\t\t}\n154\t\tdone()\n155\t\n156\t\treturn db, nil\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t// ensureInstallSecret ensures the install secret exists, generating one if needed.\n160\tfunc ensureInstallSecret(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error {\n161\t\tvar existingSecret []byte\n162\t\terr := db.QueryRowContext(ctx, \"SELECT install_secret FROM auth WHERE id = 1\").Scan(&existingSecret)\n163\t\tif err == nil && len(existingSecret) == 32 {\n164\t\t\treturn nil\n165\t\t}\n166\t\n167\t\tsecret := make([]byte, 32)\n168\t\tif _, err := rand.Read(secret); This handles cases where the FTS index\n207\t// gets out of sync (e.g., after a DB restore or migration re-creation).\n208\tfunc ensureFTSSync(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error {\n209\t\tvar ftsCount, eventsCount int\n210\t\tif err := db.QueryRowContext(ctx, \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events_fts\").Scan(&ftsCount); err != nil {\n211\t\t\t// FTS table might not exist yet (migration 011 hasn't run)\n212\t\t\treturn nil\n213\t\t}\n214\t\tif err := db.QueryRowContext(ctx, \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events\").Scan(&eventsCount); err != nil {\n215\t\t\treturn err\n216\t\t}\n217\t\tif eventsCount > 0 && ftsCount == 0 {\n218\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Rebuilding events_fts index (%d rows to index)\", eventsCount)\n219\t\t\t_, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, \"INSERT INTO events_fts(events_fts) VALUES ('rebuild')\")\n220\t\t\treturn err\n221\t\t}\n222\t\treturn nil\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\t// CurrentVersion returns the current schema version of the database.\n226\tfunc CurrentVersion(dataDir, dbName string) (int, error) {\n227\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)\n228\t\tdefer cancel()\n229\t\n230\t\tdbPath := filepath.Join(dataDir, dbName)\n231\t\n232\t\tmigrator, err := NewMigrator(dbPath, Config{\n233\t\t\tDataDir: dataDir,\n234\t\t})\n235\t\tif err != nil {\n236\t\t\treturn 0, err\n237\t\t}\n238\t\tdefer migrator.Close()\n239\t\n240\t\treturn migrator.CurrentVersion(ctx)\n241\t}\n242\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_751c7b698c8b4faeb9495bc9","session_id":"e969917b-904e-4516-80e4-c0016537e6d7","uuid":"a38f79e8-a552-4de7-86e8-0666110bf218","timestamp":"2026-04-25T12:46:15.293Z"}

s.mu.Unlock()\n94\t\n95\t\trows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT key, value_json FROM settings`)\n96\t\tif err != nil {\n97\t\t\treturn err\n98\t\t}\n99\t\tdefer rows.Close()\n100\t\n101\t\t// Clear existing cache\n102\t\ts.cache = make(map[string]interface{})\n103\t\n104\t\tfor rows.Next() {\n105\t\t\tvar key, valueJSON string\n106\t\t\tif err := rows.Scan(&key, &valueJSON); err != nil {\n107\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to scan setting key=%s: %v\", key, err)\n108\t\t\t\tcontinue\n109\t\t\t}\n110\t\n111\t\t\tvar value interface{}\n112\t\t\tif err := err)\n115\t\t\t\ts.cache[key] = valueJSON\n116\t\t\t} else {\n117\t\t\t\ts.cache[key] = value\n118\t\t\t}\n119\t\t}\n120\t\n121\t\treturn rows.Err()\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t// Get returns all settings as false\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\t// Set updates a single setting value.\n166\t// The value is marshaled to JSON and stored in the database.\n167\tfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Set(key string, value interface{}) value\n192\t\treturn nil\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// Update merges a partial settings map with existing settings.\n196\t// Only the keys provided in the updates map are modified.\n197\tfunc (s h.setSpeed)\n133\t\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/set-state\", h.setState)\n134\t\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/jump-to-time\", h.jumpToTime)\n135\t\n136\t\t// Session state endpoint for polling\n137\t\tr.Get(\"/api/replay/session/{id}\", h.getSessionState)\n138\t}\n139\t\n140\t// replayInfo represents the response from GET /api/replay/sessions.\n141\ttype replayInfo struct {\n142\t\tHasData   bool             `json:\"has_data\"`\n143\t\tFileSize  int64            `json:\"file_size_mb\"`\n144\t\tWritePos  int64            `json:\"write_pos\"`\n145\t\tOldestPos int64            `json:\"oldest_pos\"`\n146\t\tOldestTS  int64            `json:\"oldest_timestamp_ms\"`\n147\t\tNewestTS  int64            `json:\"newest_timestamp_ms\"`\n148\t\tSessions  []*_replaySession `json:\"sessions\"`\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// listSessions handles GET /api/replay/sessions.\n152\t// Returns replay store statistics and all active sessions.\n153\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) listSessions(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n154\t\tstats := h.worker.GetStoreStats()\n155\t\n156\t\th.mu.RLock()\n157\t\tsessions := make([]*_replaySession, 0, len(h.sessions))\n158\t\tfor _, s := range h.sessions {\n159\t\t\tsessions = append(sessions, s)\n160\t\t}\n161\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n162\t\n163\t\t// Get oldest and newest timestamps\n164\t\toldestTS := int64(0)\n165\t\tnewestTS := int64(0)\n166\t\tif stats.HasData {\n167\t\t\t// Scan to find timestamps\n168\t\t\th.scanTimestamps(&oldestTS, &newestTS)\n169\t\t}\n170\t\n171\t\tinfo := replayInfo{\n172\t\t\tHasData:   stats.HasData,\n173\t\t\tFileSize:  stats.FileSize / (1024 * 1024),\n174\t\t\tWritePos:  stats.WritePos,\n175\t\t\tOldestPos: stats.OldestPos,\n176\t\t\tOldestTS:  oldestTS,\n177\t\t\tNewestTS:  newestTS,\n178\t\t\tSessions:  sessions,\n179\t\t}\n180\t\n181\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, info)\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// scanTimestamps scans the replay store to find oldest and newest timestamps.\n185\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) scanTimestamps(oldest, newest *int64) {\n186\t\tstats := h.worker.GetStoreStats()\n187\t\tif !stats.HasData {\n188\t\t\treturn\n189\t\t}\n190\t\n191\t\t// Scan for oldest and newest\n192\t\tstore := h.worker.GetStore()\n193\t\tstore.Scan(func(recvTimeNS int64, frame []byte) bool {\n194\t\t\trecvMS := recvTimeNS / 1e6\n195\t\t\tif *oldest == 0 || recvMS < *oldest {\n196\t\t\t\t*oldest = recvMS\n197\t\t\t}\n198\t\t\t*newest = recvMS\n199\t\t\treturn true // continue to find newest\n200\t\t})\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\t// startSessionRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/start.\n204\ttype startSessionRequest struct {\n205\t\t// FromISO8601 is the start timestamp in ISO8601 format (e.g., \"2024-03-15T14:30:00Z\")\n206\t\tFromISO8601 string `json:\"from_iso8601\"`\n207\t\t// ToISO8601 is the end timestamp in ISO8601 format. If empty, defaults to now.\n208\t\tToISO8601 string `json:\"to_iso8601\"`\n209\t\t// Speed is the playback speed multiplier: 1, 2, or 5. Defaults to 1.\n210\t\tSpeed int `json:\"speed,omitempty\"`\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\t// startSession handles POST /api/replay/start.\n214\t// Creates a new replay session for the specified time range.\n215\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) startSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n216\t\tvar req startSessionRequest\n217\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n218\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n219\t\t\treturn\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tfromMS, err := parseISO8601(req.FromISO8601)\n223\t\tif err != nil {\n224\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid from_iso8601: \" + err.Error()})\n225\t\t\treturn\n226\t\t}\n227\t\n228\t\ttoMS := timeNowMillis()\n229\t\tif req.ToISO8601 != \"\" {\n230\t\t\ttoMS, err = parseISO8601(req.ToISO8601)\n231\t\t\tif err != nil {\n232\t\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid to_iso8601: \" + err.Error()})\n233\t\t\t\treturn\n234\t\t\t}\n235\t\t}\n236\t\n237\t\tif toMS < fromMS {\n238\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"to_iso8601 must be after from_iso8601\"})\n239\t\t\treturn\n240\t\t}\n241\t\n242\t\tspeed := req.Speed\n243\t\tif speed == 0 {\n244\t\t\tspeed = 1\n245\t\t}\n246\t\tif speed != 1 && speed != 2 && speed != 5 {\n247\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"speed must be 1, 2, or 5\"})\n248\t\t\treturn\n249\t\t}\n250\t\n251\t\t// Start session via worker\n252\t\tsessionID, err := h.worker.StartSession(fromMS, toMS, speed)\n253\t\tif err != nil {\n254\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n255\t\t\treturn\n256\t\t}\n257\t\n258\t\t// Track session in API layer\n259\t\th.mu.Lock()\n260\t\tsession := &_replaySession{\n261\t\t\tID:        sessionID,\n262\t\t\tFromMS:    fromMS,\n263\t\t\tToMS:      toMS,\n264\t\t\tCurrentMS: fromMS,\n265\t\t\tSpeed:     speed,\n266\t\t\tState:     \"paused\",\n267\t\t\tParams:    make(map[string]interface{}),\n268\t\t\tCreatedAt: formatTimestamp(fromMS),\n269\t\t}\n270\t\th.sessions[sessionID] = session\n271\t\th.nextID++\n272\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n273\t\n274\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session started: %s (from %d to %d, speed %dx)\",\n275\t\t\tsessionID, fromMS, toMS, speed)\n276\t\n277\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n278\t\t\t\"session_id\": sessionID,\n279\t\t\t\"from_ms\":    fromMS,\n280\t\t\t\"to_ms\":      toMS,\n281\t\t\t\"speed\":      speed,\n282\t\t\t\"state\":      \"paused\",\n283\t\t})\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t// stopSessionRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/stop.\n287\ttype stopSessionRequest struct {\n288\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to stop.\n289\t\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t// stopSession handles POST /api/replay/stop.\n293\t// Stops the specified replay session and deletes it.\n294\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) stopSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n295\t\tvar req stopSessionRequest\n296\t\tif err := `json:\"session_id\"`\n326\t\t// TimestampISO8601 is the target timestamp in ISO8601 format.\n327\t\tTimestampISO8601 string `json:\"timestamp_iso8601\"`\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t// seek handles POST /api/replay/seek.\n331\t// Seeks to the specified timestamp within the session.\n332\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) seek(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n333\t\tvar req seekRequest\n334\t\tif err fromMS || targetMS > toMS {\n359\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp outside session range\"})\n360\t\t\treturn\n361\t\t}\n362\t\n363\t\tif err := err.Error()})\n369\t\t\treturn\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\t// Update API layer session state\n373\t\th.mu.Lock()\n374\t\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n375\t\t\ts.CurrentMS = DeltaRMSThreshold is the motion detection threshold (0.001-1.0).\n394\t\tDeltaRMSThreshold *float64 `json:\"delta_rms_threshold,omitempty\"`\n395\t\t// TauS is the EMA baseline time constant in handles GET /api/replay/session/{id}.\n550\t// Returns the current state of a replay session including blobs.\n551\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) getSessionState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n552\t\tsessionID := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n553\t\n554\t\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(sessionID)\n555\t\tif err != nil {\n556\t\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n557\t\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n558\t\t\t\treturn\n559\t\t\t}\n560\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n561\t\t\treturn\n562\t\t}\n563\t\n564\t\t// Update API layer session state\n565\t\th.mu.Lock()\n566\t\tif s, exists := h.sessions[sessionID]; exists {\n567\t\t\ts.CurrentMS = session.CurrentMS\n568\t\t\ts.State = session.State\n569\t\t}\n570\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n571\t\n572\t\t// Calculate progress\n573\t\tduration := session.ToMS - session.FromMS\n574\t\tprogress := 0.0\n575\t\tif duration > 0 {\n576\t\t\tprogress = float64(session.CurrentMS-session.FromMS) / float64(duration)\n577\t\t}\n578\t\n579\t\t// Convert replay blobs to API format\n580\t\tblobs := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(session.LastBlobs))\n581\t\tfor _, b := range session.LastBlobs {\n582\t\t\tblob := map[string]interface{}{\n583\t\t\t\t\"id\":                  b.ID,\n584\t\t\t\t\"x\":                   b.X,\n585\t\t\t\t\"y\":                   b.Y,\n586\t\t\t\t\"z\":                   b.Z,\n587\t\t\t\t\"vx\":                  b.VX,\n588\t\t\t\t\"vy\":                  b.VY,\n589\t\t\t\t\"vz\":                  b.VZ,\n590\t\t\t\t\"weight\":              b.Weight,\n591\t\t\t\t\"posture\":             b.Posture,\n592\t\t\t\t\"person_id\":           b.PersonID,\n593\t\t\t\t\"person_label\":        b.PersonLabel,\n594\t\t\t\t\"person_color\":        b.PersonColor,\n595\t\t\t\t\"identity_confidence\": b.IdentityConfidence,\n596\t\t\t\t\"identity_source\":     b.IdentitySource,\n597\t\t\t\t\"trail\":               b.Trail,\n598\t\t\t}\n599\t\t\tblobs = append(blobs, blob)\n600\t\t}\n601\t\n602\t\t// Build response with session state and blobs\n603\t\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n604\t\t\t\"session_id\":  sessionID,\n605\t\t\t\"current_ms\":  session.CurrentMS,\n606\t\t\t\"from_ms\":     session.FromMS,\n607\t\t\t\"to_ms\":       session.ToMS,\n608\t\t\t\"state\":       session.State,\n609\t\t\t\"speed\":       session.Speed,\n610\t\t\t\"progress\":    progress,\n611\t\t\t\"params\":      session.Params,\n612\t\t\t\"blobs\":       blobs,\n613\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": session.LastBlobTime,\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// jumpToTimeRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\n620\ttype jumpToTimeRequest struct {\n621\t\t// TimestampMS is the target timestamp in epoch milliseconds.\n622\t\tTimestampMS int64 `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n623\t\t// WindowMS is the time window around the timestamp (default 5000ms).\n624\t\tWindowMS int64 `json:\"window_ms,omitempty\"`\n625\t}\n626\t\n627\t// jumpToTime handles POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\n628\t// Creates a replay session centered on the given timestamp, seeks to it,\n629\t// and {\n634\t\tvar tableExists bool\n635\t\tif err := tx.QueryRow(\n636\t\t\t`SELECT COUNT(*) > 0 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='events'`,\n637\t\t).Scan(&tableExists); err != nil {\n638\t\t\treturn err\n639\t\t}\n640\t\tif !tableExists {\n641\t\t\treturn nil\n642\t\t}\n643\t\n644\t\tschema := `\n645\t\t-- FTS5 index for natural-language search across event detail\n646\t\tCREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events_fts USING fts5(\n647\t\t\ttype, zone, person, detail_json,\n648\t\t\tcontent='events', content_rowid='id'\n649\t\t);\n650\t\n651\t\t-- Triggers to keep events_fts in sync with the events table\n652\t\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON events BEGIN\n653\t\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n654\t\t\tVALUES (new.id, new.type, new.zone, new.person, new.detail_json);\n655\t\tEND;\n656\t\n657\t\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON events AUTOINCREMENT,\n134\t\t\ttimestamp_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n135\t\t\ttype        TEXT    NOT NULL,\n136\t\t\tzone        TEXT,\n137\t\t\tperson      TEXT,\n138\t\t\tblob_id     INTEGER,\n139\t\t\tdetail_json TEXT,\n140\t\t\tseverity    TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'\n141\t\t);\n142\t\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_time ON events(timestamp_ms DESC);\n143\t\tCREATE detail_json)\n162\t\t\tVALUES (new.id, new.type, new.zone, new.person, new.detail_json);\n163\t\tEND;\n164\t\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON events BEGIN\n165\t\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(events_fts, rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n166\t\t\tVALUES ('delete', old.id, old.type, old.zone, old.person, old.detail_json);\n167\t\tEND;\n168\t\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON events BEGIN\n169\t\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(events_fts, rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n170\t\t\tVALUES ('delete', old.id, old.type, old.zone, old.person, old.detail_json);\n171\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n172\t\tVALUES (new.id, new.type, new.zone, new.person, new.detail_json);\n173\t\tEND;\n174\t\t`\n175\t\t_, err := db.Exec(schema)\n176\t\treturn err\n177\t}\n178\t\n179\t// isValidEventType checks whether the event type string is a known type.\n180\tfunc isValidEventType(t string) bool {\n181\t\tswitch t {\n182\t\tcase \"detection\", \"zone_entry\", \"zone_exit\", \"portal_crossing\",\n183\t\t\t\"trigger_fired\", \"fall_alert\", \"FallDetected\", \"anomaly\", \"AnomalyDetected\", \"security_alert\",\n184\t\t\t\"node_online\", \"node_offline\", \"ota_update\", \"baseline_changed\",\n185\t\t\t\"system\", \"learning_milestone\", \"sleep_session_end\", \"ZoneTransition\",\n186\t\t\t\"presence_transition\", \"stationary_detected\", \"anomaly_learned\", \"sleep_session_start\":\n187\t\t\treturn true\n188\t\t}\n189\t\treturn false\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t// RegisterRoutes registers events endpoints.\n193\t//\n194\t// GET /api/events — paginated event list with FTS5 search and keyset cursor pagination.\n195\t//\n196\t//\tQuery params: limit (default 50, max 500), before (timestamp_ms cursor),\n197\t//\tsince (ISO8601), until (ISO8601), type (single type filter),\n198\t//\ttypes (comma-separated type list for category filtering),\n199\t//\tzone, zone_id, person, person_id, q (FTS5 query), mode (expert|simple).\n200\t//\n201\t// GET /api/events/{id} — single event by `json:\"has_more\"`\n215\t\tTotalFiltered int      `json:\"total_filtered\"`\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// prepareFTSQuery appends a trailing * for prefix matching if the query\n219\t// doesn't already end with a FTS5 operator character. This enables\n220\t// partial word matching (e.g., \"kit\" matches \"kitchen\").\n221\tfunc prepareFTSQuery(q string) string {\n222\t\tq = strings.TrimSpace(q)\n223\t\tif q == \"\" {\n224\t\t\treturn q\n225\t\t}\n226\t\t// If the query already ends with a FTS5 special character or operator, leave it alone.\n227\t\tlast := q[len(q)-1]\n228\t\tif last == '*' || last == '\"' || last == ')' {\n229\t\t\treturn q\n230\t\t}\n231\t\t// For simple terms (no operators), append * for prefix matching.\n232\t\t// If the query contains FTS5 operators (AND, OR, NOT, NEAR), append * to each\n233\t\t// simple token instead.\n234\t\tif strings.Contains(q, \" AND \") || strings.Contains(q, \" OR \") ||\n235\t\t\tstrings.Contains(q, \" NOT \") || strings.Contains(q, \" NEAR \") {\n236\t\t\t// Has operators — append * to each token that isn't an operator or quoted phrase.\n237\t\t\tparts := strings.Fields(q)\n238\t\t\tfor i, p := range parts {\n239\t\t\t\tif p == \"AND\" || p == \"OR\" || p == \"NOT\" || p == \"NEAR\" {\n240\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n241\t\t\t\t}\n242\t\t\t\tif (strings.HasPrefix(p, `\"`) && strings.HasSuffix(p, `\"`)) || p == \"(\" || p == \")\" {\n243\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n244\t\t\t\t}\n245\t\t\t\tparts[i] = p + \"*\"\n246\t\t\t}\n247\t\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \" \")\n248\t\t}\n249\t\t// Simple single-term query — just append * for prefix matching.\n250\t\treturn q + \"*\"\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\tfunc (e *EventsHandler) listEvents(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n254\t\t// Parse limit\n255\t\tlimit :=     string  `json:\"title\"`\n31\t\tMessage   string  `json:\"message\"`\n32\t\tZone      string  `json:\"zone,omitempty\"`\n33\t\tPerson    string  `json:\"person,omitempty\"`\n34\t\tTimestamp int64  `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n35\t\tData      any     `json:\"data,omitempty\"` // Type-specific data\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t// ActiveAlertsResponse is the response for GET /api/alerts/active.\n39\ttype ActiveAlertsResponse struct {\n40\t\tAlerts []Alert `json:\"alerts\"`\n41\t\tCount  int      `json:\"count\"`\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\t// NewAlertsHandler creates a new alerts handler.\n45\tfunc NewAlertsHandler() *AlertsHandler {\n46\t\treturn &AlertsHandler{}\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t// SetFallDetector sets the fall detection module.\n50\tfunc (h *AlertsHandler) SetFallDetector(detector *falldetect.Detector) {\n51\t\th.mu.Lock()\n52\t\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n53\t\th.fallDetector = detector\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t// SetAnomalyDetector sets the anomaly detection module.\n57\tfunc (h handleGetActiveAlerts returns all active alerts from all sources.\n82\t// GET /api/alerts/active\n83\t//\n84\t// Response:\n85\t//\n86\t//\t{\n87\t//\t  \"alerts\": [\n88\t//\t    {\n89\t//\t      \"id\": \"fall-123\",\n90\t//\t      \"type\": \"fall\",\n91\t//\t      \"severity\": \"critical\",\n92\t//\t      \"title\": \"Possible fall detected\",\n93\t//\t      \"message\": \"Alice in ],\n100\t//\t  \"count\": 2\n101\t//\t}\n102\tfunc (h *AlertsHandler) handleGetActiveAlerts(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n103\t\th.mu.RLock()\n104\t\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n105\t\n106\t\tvar alerts []Alert\n107\t\n108\t\t// Add active fall alerts\n109\t\tif h.fallDetector != nil {\n110\t\t\tfalls := h.fallDetector.GetActiveFalls()\n111\t\t\tfor _, fall := range falls {\n112\t\t\t\talert := Alert{\n113\t\t\t\t\tID:        \"fall-\" + fall.ID,\n114\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"fall\",\n115\t\t\t\t\tSeverity:  \"critical\",\n116\t\t\t\t\tTitle:     \"Possible fall detected\",\n117\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   h.formatFallMessage(fall),\n118\t\t\t\t\tZone:      fall.ZoneName,\n119\t\t\t\t\tPerson:    fall.Identity,\n120\t\t\t\t\tTimestamp: fall.Timestamp.Unix() * 1000,\n121\t\t\t\t\tData:      fall,\n122\t\t\t\t}\n123\t\t\t\talerts = append(alerts, alert)\n124\t\t\t}\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Add active anomaly alerts\n128\t\tif h.anomalyDetector != nil {\n129\t\t\tanomalies := h.anomalyDetector.GetActiveAnomalies()\n130\t\t\tfor _, anomaly := range anomalies {\n131\t\t\t\talert := Alert{\n132\t\t\t\t\tID:        \"anomaly-\" + anomaly.ID,\n133\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"anomaly\",\n134\t\t\t\t\tSeverity:  \"warning\",\n135\t\t\t\t\tTitle:     \"Unusual activity detected\",\n136\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   h.formatAnomalyMessage(anomaly),\n137\t\t\t\t\tTimestamp: anomaly.Timestamp.Unix() * 1000,\n138\t\t\t\t\tData:      anomaly,\n139\t\t\t\t}\n140\t\t\t\talerts = append(alerts, alert)\n141\t\t\t}\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Add offline node alerts\n145\t\tif h.fleetRegistry != nil {\n146\t\t\tnodes, err := h.fleetRegistry.GetAllNodes()\n147\t\t\tif err == nil {\n148\t\t\t\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n149\t\t\t\t\tif !node.WentOfflineAt.IsZero() {\n150\t\t\t\t\t\talert := Alert{\n151\t\t\t\t\t\t\tID:        \"node-\" + node.MAC,\n152\t\t\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"node_offline\",\n153\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSeverity:  \"warning\",\n154\t\t\t\t\t\t\tTitle:     \"Node offline\",\n155\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Node \" + node.Name + \" went offline\",\n156\t\t\t\t\t\t\tTimestamp: node.WentOfflineAt.Unix() * 1000,\n157\t\t\t\t\t\t\tData: map[string]interface{}{\n158\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"mac\":          node.MAC,\n159\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"name\":         node.Name,\n160\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"status\":       \"offline\",\n161\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"last_seen_at\": node.LastSeenAt,\n162\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n163\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n164\t\t\t\t\t\talerts = append(alerts, alert)\n165\t\t\t\t\t}\n166\t\t\t\t}\n167\t\t\t}\n168\t\t}\n169\t\n170\t\t// Sort by severity (critical > warning > info) and timestamp\n171\t\th.sortAlerts(alerts)\n172\t\n173\t\tresponse := ActiveAlertsResponse{\n174\t\t\tAlerts: alerts,\n175\t\t\tCount:  len(alerts),\n176\t\t}\n177\t\n178\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t// handleAcknowledgeAlert acknowledges an alert by ID.\n182\t// POST /api/alerts/{id}/acknowledge\n183\t//\n184\t// The ID is prefixed by the alert type: \"fall-123\", \"anomaly-456\", \"node-AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\".\n185\tfunc (h *AlertsHandler) handleAcknowledgeAlert(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n186\t\talertID := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n187\t\tif alertID == \"\" {\n188\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"alert ID required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n189\t\t\treturn\n190\t\t}\n191\t\n192\t\t// Parse the alert type and ID\n193\t\tvar alertType, id string\n194\t\tif len(alertID) > 5 && alertID[4] == '-' {\n195\t\t\talertType = alertID[:4]\n196\t\t\tid = alertID[5:]\n197\t\t} else {\n198\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid alert ID format\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n199\t\t\treturn\n200\t\t}\n201\t\n202\t\tvar err error\n203\t\tswitch alertType {\n204\t\tcase \"fall\":\n205\t\t\tif h.fallDetector != nil {\n206\t\t\t\terr = h.fallDetector.AcknowledgeFall(id, \"acknowledged\")\n207\t\t\t} else {\n208\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Fall detector not available for acknowledgment\")\n209\t\t\t}\n210\t\tcase \"anomaly\":\n211\t\t\tif h.anomalyDetector != nil {\n212\t\t\t\terr = h.anomalyDetector.AcknowledgeAnomaly(id, \"\", \"\")\n213\t\t\t} else {\n214\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Anomaly detector not available for acknowledgment\")\n215\t\t\t}\n216\t\tcase \"node\":\n217\t\t\t// Node alerts don't require acknowledgment - they auto-clear when node comes back online\n218\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Node offline alert acknowledged: %s\", id)\n219\t\tdefault:\n220\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"unknown alert type: \"+alertType, http.StatusBadRequest)\n221\t\t\treturn\n222\t\t}\n223\t\n224\t\tif err != nil {\n225\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to acknowledge alert %s: %v\", alertID, err)\n226\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to acknowledge alert\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n227\t\t\treturn\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{\"status\": \"acknowledged\", \"id\": alertID})\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// sortAlerts sorts alerts by severity and timestamp.\n234\tfunc (h *AlertsHandler) sortAlerts(alerts []Alert) {\n235\t\t// Simple bubble sort (small slice size expected)\n236\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(alerts)-1; i++ {\n237\t\t\tfor j := 0; j < len(alerts)-1-i; j++ {\n238\t\t\t\tif h.compareAlerts(alerts[j], alerts[j+1]) > 0 {\n239\t\t\t\t\talerts[j], alerts[j+1] = alerts[j+1], alerts[j]\n240\t\t\t\t}\n241\t\t\t}\n242\t\t}\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t// compareAlerts returns negative if a < b (a should come first).\n246\tfunc (h *AlertsHandler) compareAlerts(a, b Alert) int {\n247\t\t// First compare by severity\n248\t\tseverityOrder := map[string]int{\n249\t\t\t\"critical\": 0,\n250\t\t\t\"warning\":  1,\n251\t\t\t\"info\":     2,\n252\t\t}\n253\t\n254\t\tif severityOrder[a.Severity] != severityOrder[b.Severity] {\n255\t\t\treturn severityOrder[a.Severity] - severityOrder[b.Severity]\n256\t\t}\n257\t\n258\t\t// Same severity, compare by timestamp (newer first)\n259\t\tif a.Timestamp > b.Timestamp {\n260\t\t\treturn -1\n261\t\t}\n262\t\tif a.Timestamp < b.Timestamp {\n263\t\t\treturn 1\n264\t\t}\n265\t\treturn 0\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t// formatFallMessage formats a fall event into a human-readable message.\n269\tfunc (h *AlertsHandler) formatFallMessage(fall falldetect.FallEvent) string {\n270\t\tif fall.Identity != \"\" {\n271\t\t\treturn fall.Identity + \" in \" + fall.ZoneName\n272\t\t}\n273\t\treturn \"Someone in \" + fall.ZoneName\n274\t}\n275\t\n276\t// formatAnomalyMessage formats an anomaly into a \"Unusual activity detected\"\n280\t}\n281\t\n282\t// handleAcknowledgeFall acknowledges a fall alert.\n283\t// POST /api/fall/{id}/acknowledge\n284\t//\n285\t// This is a convenience route that (must be 00-23)\"}\n506\t\t}\n507\t\tif minuteVal > 59 {\n508\t\t\treturn &NotificationValidationError{Reason: \"invalid minute (must be 00-59)\"}\n509\t\t}\n510\t\treturn nil\n511\t}\n512\t\n513\t// {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_f60b318421424da283b24368","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t// Package api provides the backup streaming endpoint.\n2\tpackage api\n3\t\n4\timport (\n5\t\t\"archive/zip\"\n6\t\t\"context\"\n7\t\t\"database/sql\"\n8\t\t\"fmt\"\n9\t\t\"io/fs\"\n10\t\t\"log\"\n11\t\t\"net/http\"\n12\t\t\"os\"\n13\t\t\"path/filepath\"\n14\t\t\"sort\"\n15\t\t\"strings\"\n16\t\t\"time\"\n17\t\n18\t\t\"modernc.org/sqlite\"\n19\t)\n20\t\n21\t// BackupHandler handles GET /api/backup — streams a zip of all databases\n22\t// and supporting files directly to the HTTP response without temp files.\n23\ttype BackupHandler struct {\n24\t\tdataDir string\n25\t\tversion string\n26\t}\n27\t\n28\t// NewBackupHandler creates a backup handler that will archive every .db\n29\t// file found inside dataDir, plus optional floor_plan/ and a VERSION file.\n30\tfunc NewBackupHandler(dataDir, version string) *BackupHandler {\n31\t\treturn &BackupHandler{dataDir: dataDir, version: version}\n32\t}\n33\t\n34\t// HandleBackup streams a zip archive to w.\n35\t//\n36\t// Zip layout:\n37\t//\n38\t//\tspaxel-backup-<timestamp>.zip\n39\t//\t├── *.db               — one entry per database file found in dataDir\n40\t//\t├── floor_plan/        — if the directory exists\n41\t//\t│   └── ...\n42\t//\t└── VERSION            — mothership version string\n43\tfunc (h *BackupHandler) HandleBackup(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n44\t\tstart := time.Now()\n45\t\ttimestamp := start.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02\")\n46\t\n47\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/zip\")\n48\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\",\n49\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(`attachment; filename=\"spaxel-backup-%s.zip\"`, timestamp))\n50\t\n51\t\t// We write directly into the response — no temp file on disk.\n52\t\tzw := zip.NewWriter(w)\n53\t\tdefer zw.Close()\n54\t\n55\t\t// 1. Back up every .db file found in dataDir using the Online Backup API.\n56\t\tif err := h.backupDatabases(zw); err != nil {\n57\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] backup: database backup failed: %v\", err)\n58\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"backup failed\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n59\t\t\treturn\n60\t\t}\n61\t\n62\t\t// 2. Include floor_plan/ directory if it exists.\n63\t\tif err := h.backupDirectory(zw, \"floor_plan\"); err != nil {\n64\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] backup: floor_plan backup skipped: %v\", err)\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\t// 3. Include VERSION file.\n68\t\tif fw, err := zw.Create(\"VERSION\"); err == nil {\n69\t\t\tfw.Write([]byte(h.version + \"\\n\"))\n70\t\t}\n71\t\n72\t\tif err := zw.Close(); err != nil {\n73\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] backup: zip close failed: %v\", err)\n74\t\t\treturn\n75\t\t}\n76\t\n77\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] backup completed in %s\", time.Since(start))\n78\t}\n79\t\n80\t// backupDatabases finds all .db files in dataDir, uses the SQLite Online\n81\t// Backup API to create a consistent snapshot of each, and adds the snapshot\n82\t// to the zip.\n83\tfunc (h *BackupHandler) backupDatabases(zw *zip.Writer) error {\n84\t\tentries, err := os.ReadDir(h.dataDir)\n85\t\tif err != nil {\n86\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"read data dir: %w\", err)\n87\t\t}\n88\t\n89\t\t// Collect .db files and sort for deterministic zip ordering.\n90\t\tvar dbFiles []string\n91\t\tfor _, e := range entries {\n92\t\t\tif !e.Type().IsRegular() {\n93\t\t\t\tcontinue\n94\t\t\t}\n95\t\t\tif strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), \".db\") {\n96\t\t\t\tdbFiles = append(dbFiles, e.Name())\n97\t\t\t}\n98\t\t}\n99\t\tsort.Strings(dbFiles)\n100\t\n101\t\tfor _, name := range dbFiles {\n102\t\t\tdbPath := filepath.Join(h.dataDir, name)\n103\t\t\tif err := h.backupOneDB(zw, dbPath, name); err != nil {\n104\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] backup: skipping %s: %v\", name, err)\n105\t\t\t\tcontinue\n106\t\t\t}\n107\t\t}\n108\t\n109\t\treturn nil\n110\t}\n111\t\n112\t// backupOneDB uses the SQLite Online Backup API to produce a consistent\n113\t// snapshot of the database at dbPath, then writes the serialized bytes into\n114\t// the zip entry named zipName.\n115\t//\n116\t// The Online Backup API copies page-by-page; readers and writers continue\n117\t// uninterrupted. No temp file is written — the backup is serialized from\n118\t// an in-memory copy directly to the zip stream.\n119\tfunc (h *BackupHandler) backupOneDB(zw *zip.Writer, dbPath, zipName string) error {\n120\t\tdsn := dbPath + \"?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=synchronous(NORMAL)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)\"\n121\t\tdb, err := sql.Open(\"sqlite\", dsn)\n122\t\tif err != nil {\n123\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"open: %w\", err)\n124\t\t}\n125\t\tdefer db.Close()\n126\t\n127\t\tconn, err := db.Conn(context.Background())\n128\t\tif err != nil {\n129\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"conn: %w\", err)\n130\t\t}\n131\t\tdefer conn.Close()\n132\t\n133\t\tvar backupBytes []byte\n134\t\n135\t\terr = conn.Raw(func(driverConn any) error {\n136\t\t\t// Assert the backuper interface to access the Online Backup API.\n137\t\t\tbp, ok := driverConn.(interface {\n138\t\t\t\tNewBackup(dstUri string) (*sqlite.Backup, error)\n139\t\t\t})\n140\t\t\tif !ok {\n141\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"driver does not support online backup\")\n142\t\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t\t// Create an in-memory destination and initialise the backup.\n145\t\t\tbck, err := bp.NewBackup(\":memory:\")\n146\t\t\tif err != nil {\n147\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"backup init: %w\", err)\n148\t\t\t}\n149\t\n150\t\t\t// Copy pages 100 at a time until done.\n151\t\t\tconst pagesPerStep = 100\n152\t\t\tfor {\n153\t\t\t\tmore, err := bck.Step(pagesPerStep)\n154\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n155\t\t\t\t\tbck.Finish()\n156\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"backup step: %w\", err)\n157\t\t\t\t}\n158\t\t\t\tif !more {\n159\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n160\t\t\t\t}\n161\t\t\t}\n162\t\n163\t\t\t// Finish the backup but keep the destination connection open.\n164\t\t\tdstConn, err := bck.Commit()\n165\t\t\tif err != nil {\n166\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"backup commit: %w\", err)\n167\t\t\t}\n168\t\t\tdefer dstConn.Close()\n169\t\n170\t\t\t// Serialize the in-memory database to bytes.\n171\t\t\tser, ok := dstConn.(interface {\n172\t\t\t\tSerialize() ([]byte, error)\n173\t\t\t})\n174\t\t\tif !ok {\n175\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"driver does not support serialize\")\n176\t\t\t}\n177\t\n178\t\t\tbackupBytes, err = ser.Serialize()\n179\t\t\treturn err\n180\t\t})\n181\t\tif err != nil {\n182\t\t\treturn err\n183\t\t}\n184\t\n185\t\tif len(backupBytes) == 0 {\n186\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"empty database backup\")\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\tfw, err := zw.Create(zipName)\n190\t\tif err != nil {\n191\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"zip create: %w\", err)\n192\t\t}\n193\t\tif _, err := fw.Write(backupBytes); err != nil {\n194\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"zip write: %w\", err)\n195\t\t}\n196\t\n197\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] backup: %s (%d bytes)\", zipName, len(backupBytes))\n198\t\treturn nil\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t// backupDirectory adds every file under dirName (relative to dataDir) into\n202\t// the zip, preserving directory structure.  Silently skips if the directory\n203\t// does not exist.\n204\tfunc (h *BackupHandler) backupDirectory(zw *zip.Writer, dirName string) error {\n205\t\tdirPath := filepath.Join(h.dataDir, dirName)\n206\t\tinfo, err := os.Stat(dirPath)\n207\t\tif err != nil || !info.IsDir() {\n208\t\t\treturn nil // not present — skip silently\n209\t\t}\n210\t\n211\t\treturn filepath.WalkDir(dirPath, func(path string, d fs.DirEntry, err error) error {\n212\t\t\tif err != nil {\n213\t\t\t\treturn err\n214\t\t\t}\n215\t\t\tif d.IsDir() {\n216\t\t\t\treturn nil\n217\t\t\t}\n218\t\n219\t\t\trel, err := filepath.Rel(h.dataDir, path)\n220\t\t\tif err != nil {\n221\t\t\t\treturn err\n222\t\t\t}\n223\t\t\t// zip paths must use forward slashes.\n224\t\t\trel = filepath.ToSlash(rel)\n225\t\n226\t\t\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(path)\n227\t\t\tif err != nil {\n228\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"read %s: %w\", rel, err)\n229\t\t\t}\n230\t\n231\t\t\tfw, err := zw.Create(rel)\n232\t\t\tif err != nil {\n233\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"zip create %s: %w\", rel, err)\n234\t\t\t}\n235\t\t\tif _, err := fw.Write(data); err != nil {\n236\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"zip write %s: %w\", rel, err)\n237\t\t\t}\n238\t\t\treturn nil\n239\t\t})\n240\t}\n241\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_751c7b698c8b4faeb9495bc9","session_id":"e969917b-904e-4516-80e4-c0016537e6d7","uuid":"e01b27ed-c973-4963-be46-ee6751c572d9","timestamp":"2026-04-25T12:46:40.203Z"}

Enabled defaults to true.\n349\t//\n350\t// Request body (application/json):\n351\t//\n352\t//\t{\n353\t//\t  \"name\": \"Couch Dwell\",\n354\t//\t  \"shape\": {\"type\": \"box\", \"x\": 1, \"y\": 2, \"06:00\"},\n358\t//\t  \"actions\": [{\"type\": \"webhook\", \"url\": \"http://example.com/hook\"}],\n359\t//\t  \"enabled\": true\n360\t//\t}\n361\t//\n362\t// Response 201 (application/json): the created trigger object.\n363\t// Response 400: missing required fields, invalid shape geometry, or invalid condition value.\n364\t// Response 500: database error.\n365\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) createTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n366\t\tvar req volumeCreateTriggerRequest\n367\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n368\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n369\t\t\treturn\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\tif req.Name == \"\" {\n373\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"name is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n380\t\t\treturn\n381\t\t}\n382\t\n383\t\t// Validate condition\n384\t\tvalidConditions := map[string]bool{\n385\t\t\t\"enter\":  true,\n386\t\t\t\"leave\":  true,\n387\t\t\t\"dwell\":  true,\n388\t\t\t\"vacant\": true,\n389\t\t\t\"count\":  true,\n390\t\t}\n391\t\tif !validConditions[req.Condition] {\n392\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"condition must be one of: enter, leave, dwell, vacant, count\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n393\t\t\treturn\n394\t\t}\n395\t\n396\t\t// Set default enabled if not specified\n397\t\tenabled := true\n398\t\tif req.Enabled != nil {\n399\t\t\tenabled = *req.Enabled\n400\t\t}\n401\t\n402\t\ttrigger := &volume.Trigger{\n403\t\t\tName:            req.Name,\n404\t\t\tShape:           req.Shape,\n405\t\t\tCondition:       req.Condition,\n406\t\t\tConditionParams: req.ConditionParams,\n407\t\t\tTimeConstraint:  req.TimeConstraint,\n408\t\t\tActions:         req.Actions,\n409\t\t\tEnabled:         enabled,\n410\t\t}\n411\t\n412\t\tid, err := h.store.Create(trigger)\n413\t\tif err != nil {\n414\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to create trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n415\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to create trigger: %v\", err)\n416\t\t\treturn\n417\t\t}\n418\t\n419\t\t// Fetch the created trigger to get the full object\n420\t\tcreated, err := h.store.Get(id)\n421\t\tif err != nil {\n422\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to retrieve created trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n423\t\t\treturn\n424\t\t}\n425\t\n426\t\tresp := h.toResponse(created, time.Now())\n427\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, resp)\n428\t}\n429\t\n430\t// volumeUpdateTriggerRequest is the request body for PUT /api/triggers/{id}.\n431\t// Only non-nil fields are updated.\n432\ttype volumeUpdateTriggerRequest struct {\n433\t\tName            *string                  `json:\"name,omitempty\"`\n434\t\tShape           *volume.ShapeJSON        `json:\"shape,omitempty\"`\n435\t\tCondition       *string                  `json:\"condition,omitempty\"`\n436\t\tConditionParams *volume.ConditionParams `json:\"condition_params,omitempty\"`\n437\t\tTimeConstraint  *volume.TimeConstraint   `json:\"time_constraint,omitempty\"`\n438\t\tActions         *[]volume.Action         `json:\"actions,omitempty\"`\n439\t\tEnabled         *bool                    `json:\"enabled,omitempty\"`\n440\t}\n441\t\n442\t// updateTrigger handles PUT /api/triggers/{id}.\n443\t//\n444\t// Updates an existing trigger. Only fields present in the request body are\n445\t// modified; omitted fields retain their current values. Shape geometry is\n446\t// validated on update.\n447\t//\n448\t// Request body (application/json): partial trigger object with fields to update.\n449\t//\n450\t// Response 200 (application/json): the updated trigger object.\n451\t// Response 400: invalid request body or invalid shape geometry.\n452\t// Response 404: trigger not found.\n453\t// Response 500: database error.\n454\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) updateTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n455\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n456\t\n457\t\ttrigger, err := h.store.Get(id)\n458\t\tif err != nil {\n459\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"trigger not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n460\t\t\treturn\n461\t\t}\n462\t\n463\t\tvar req volumeUpdateTriggerRequest\n464\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n465\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n466\t\t\treturn\n467\t\t}\n468\t\n469\t\t// Apply updates\n470\t\tif req.Name != nil {\n471\t\t\ttrigger.Name = *req.Name\n472\t\t}\n473\t\tif req.Shape != nil {\n474\t\t\tif !h.isValidShape(req.Shape) {\n475\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid shape geometry\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n476\t\t\t\treturn\n477\t\t\t}\n478\t\t\ttrigger.Shape = *req.Shape\n479\t\t}\n480\t\tif req.Condition != nil {\n481\t\t\ttrigger.Condition = *req.Condition\n482\t\t}\n483\t\tif req.ConditionParams != nil {\n484\t\t\ttrigger.ConditionParams = *req.ConditionParams\n485\t\t}\n486\t\tif req.TimeConstraint != nil {\n487\t\t\ttrigger.TimeConstraint = req.TimeConstraint\n488\t\t}\n489\t\tif req.Actions != nil {\n490\t\t\ttrigger.Actions = *req.Actions\n491\t\t}\n492\t\tif req.Enabled != nil {\n493\t\t\ttrigger.Enabled = *req.Enabled\n494\t\t}\n495\t\n496\t\tif err := h.store.Update(trigger); err != nil {\n497\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to update trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n498\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to update trigger: %v\", err)\n499\t\t\treturn\n500\t\t}\n501\t\n502\t\tresp := h.toResponse(trigger, time.Now())\n503\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, resp)\n504\t}\n505\t\n506\t// deleteTrigger handles DELETE /api/triggers/{id}.\n507\t//\n508\t// Removes a trigger by ID and all associated state (trigger state, webhook log entries).\n509\t//\n510\t// Response 204: trigger deleted.\n511\t// Response 500: database error.\n512\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) deleteTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n513\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n514\t\n515\t\tif err := h.store.Delete(id); err != nil {\n516\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to delete trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n517\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to delete trigger: %v\", err)\n518\t\t\treturn\n519\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n522\t}\n523\t\n524\t// testTrigger handles POST /api/triggers/{id}/test.\n525\t//\n526\t// Fires the trigger's actions once with a synthetic event payload for testing.\n527\t// Webhook actions are executed immediately; MQTT and notification actions are\n528\t// reported as simulated (not executed). Test firings do NOT update last_fired,\n529\t// do NOT increment error counts, and do NOT disable the trigger on 4xx responses.\n530\t//\n531\t// Response 200 (application/json):\n532\t//\n533\t//\t{\n534\t//\t  \"status\": \"ok\",\n535\t//\t  \"response_ms\": 42,\n536\t//\t  \"actions\": [{\n537\t//\t    \"type\": \"webhook\",\n538\t//\t    \"url\": \"http://example.com/hook\",\n539\t//\t    \"status\": 200,\n540\t//\t    \"response_ms\": 42\n541\t//\t  }]\n542\t//\t}\n543\t//\n544\t// Response 404: trigger not found.\n545\t// Response 500: failed to marshal test payload.\n546\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) testTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n547\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n548\t\n549\t\ttrigger, err := h.store.Get(id)\n550\t\tif err != nil {\n551\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"trigger not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n552\t\t\treturn\n553\t\t}\n554\t\n555\t\tnow := time.Now()\n556\t\n557\t\t// Build synthetic payload per spec:\n558\t\t// {trigger_id, trigger_name, condition, blob_id, person, position:{x,y,z}, zone, dwell_s, timestamp_ms}\n559\t\tpayload := map[string]interface{}{\n560\t\t\t\"trigger_id\":   trigger.ID,\n561\t\t\t\"trigger_name\": trigger.Name,\n562\t\t\t\"condition\":    trigger.Condition,\n563\t\t\t\"blob_id\":      0,\n564\t\t\t\"person\":       nil,\n565\t\t\t\"position\": map[string]float64{\"x\": 0, \"y\": 0, \"z\": 0},\n566\t\t\t\"zone\":         nil,\n567\t\t\t\"dwell_s\":      0,\n568\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\":  now.UnixMilli(),\n569\t\t}\n570\t\n571\t\tdata, err := json.Marshal(payload)\n572\t\tif err != nil {\n573\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to marshal test payload\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n574\t\t\treturn\n575\t\t}\n576\t\n577\t\t// Execute each action and collect results\n578\t\tvar results []ActionResult\n579\t\ttotalStart := time.Now()\n580\t\n581\t\tfor _, action := range trigger.Actions {\n582\t\t\tresult := ActionResult{Type: action.Type}\n583\t\n584\t\t\tswitch action.Type {\n585\t\t\tcase \"webhook\":\n586\t\t\t\turl, ok := action.Params[\"url\"].(string)\n587\t\t\t\tif ok && url != \"\" {\n588\t\t\t\t\tresult.URL = url\n589\t\t\t\t\tstatusCode, latencyMs, err := h.doWebhookPost(url, data, action.Params)\n590\t\t\t\t\tresult.Status = statusCode\n591\t\t\t\t\tresult.ResponseMs = latencyMs\n592\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n593\t\t\t\t\t\tresult.Error = err.Error()\n594\t\t\t\t\t}\n595\t\t\t\t} else {\n596\t\t\t\t\tresult.Error = \"missing url\"\n597\t\t\t\t}\n598\t\n599\t\t\tcase \"mqtt\":\n600\t\t\t\tresult.URL, _ = action.Params[\"topic\"].(string)\n601\t\t\t\tif result.URL == \"\" {\n602\t\t\t\t\tresult.URL = \"(no topic)\"\n603\t\t\t\t}\n604\t\t\t\tresult.Error = \"test mode — mqtt not executed\"\n605\t\n606\t\t\tdefault:\n607\t\t\t\tresult.URL = \"(n/a)\"\n608\t\t\t\tresult.Error = \"test mode — action type not executable\"\n609\t\t\t}\n610\t\n611\t\t\tresults = append(results, result)\n612\t\t}\n613\t\n614\t\ttotalMs := time.Since(totalStart).Milliseconds()\n615\t\n616\t\tresp := WebhookTestResult{\n617\t\t\tStatus:    \"ok\",\n618\t\t\tResponseMs: totalMs,\n619\t\t\tActions:   results,\n620\t\t}\n621\t\n622\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, resp)\n623\t}\n624\t\n625\t// enableTrigger handles POST /api/triggers/{id}/enable.\n626\t//\n627\t// Clears the error state (error_message and error_count) and re-enables the trigger.\n628\t//\n629\t// Response 200 (application/json): {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n630\t// Response 404: trigger not found.\n631\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) enableTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n632\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n633\t\n634\t\tif err := h.store.EnableTrigger(id); err != nil {\n635\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusNotFound)\n636\t\t\treturn\n637\t\t}\n638\t\n639\t\t// Broadcast updated trigger state to dashboard\n640\t\th.broadcastTriggerState(id)\n641\t\n642\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\"status\": \"ok\"})\n643\t}\n644\t\n645\t// disableTrigger handles POST /api/triggers/{id}/disable.\n646\t//\n647\t// Disables a trigger. The trigger will no longer be evaluated until re-enabled.\n648\t//\n649\t// Response 200 (application/json): {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n650\t// Response 404: trigger not found.\n651\t// Response 500: database error.\n652\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) disableTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n653\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n654\t\n655\t\ttrigger, err := h.store.Get(id)\n656\t\tif err != nil {\n657\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"trigger not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n658\t\t\treturn\n659\t\t}\n660\t\n661\t\ttrigger.Enabled = false\n662\t\tif err := h.store.Update(trigger); err != nil {\n663\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to update trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n664\t\t\treturn\n665\t\t}\n666\t\n667\t\t// Broadcast updated trigger state to dashboard\n668\t\th.broadcastTriggerState(id)\n669\t\n670\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\"status\": \"ok\"})\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\t// getWebhookLog handles GET /api/triggers/{id}/webhook-log.\n674\t//\n675\t// Returns the most recent webhook firing log entries for a specific trigger.\n676\t// Entries include URL, timestamp, HTTP status code, latency, and any error message.\n677\t//\n678\t// Query parameters:\n679\t//   - limit: maximum entries to return (default 20, max 100)\n680\t//\n681\t// Response 200 (application/json): array of webhook log entries.\n682\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) getWebhookLog(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n683\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n684\t\n685\t\tlimitStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\")\n686\t\tlimit := 20\n687\t\tif limitStr != \"\" {\n688\t\t\tif l, err := strconv.Atoi(limitStr); err == nil && l > 0 && l <= 100 {\n689\t\t\t\tlimit = l\n690\t\t\t}\n691\t\t}\n692\t\n693\t\tentries := h.store.GetWebhookLog(id, limit)\n694\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, entries)\n695\t}\n696\t\n697\t// getTriggerLog handles GET /api/triggers/log.\n698\t//\n699\t// Returns the most recent trigger firing events across all triggers.\n700\t//\n701\t// Query parameters:\n702\t//   - limit: maximum entries to return (default 10, max 100)\n703\t//\n704\t// Response 200 (application/json): array of firing records.\n705\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) getTriggerLog(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n706\t\t// Get limit from query param (default 10, max 100)\n707\t\tlimitStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\")\n708\t\tlimit := 10\n709\t\tif limitStr != \"\" {\n710\t\t\tif l, err := strconv.Atoi(limitStr); err == nil && l > 0 && l <= 100 {\n711\t\t\t\tlimit = l\n712\t\t\t}\n713\t\t}\n714\t\n715\t\trecords := h.store.GetRecentFirings(limit)\n716\t\n717\t\t// Convert to response format\n718\t\tresponse := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(records))\n719\t\tfor _, r := range records {\n720\t\t\tresponse = append(response, map[string]interface{}{\n721\t\t\t\t\"trigger_id\":   r.TriggerID,\n722\t\t\t\t\"trigger_name\": r.TriggerName,\n723\t\t\t\t\"condition\":    r.Condition,\n724\t\t\t\t\"fired_at\":     r.FiredAt.Format(time.RFC3339),\n725\t\t\t})\n726\t\t}\n727\t\n728\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n729\t}\n730\t\n731\t// isValidShape validates that a shape has all required fields.\n732\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) isValidShape(shape *volume.ShapeJSON) bool {\n733\t\tswitch shape.Type {\n734\t\tcase volume.ShapeBox:\n735\t\t\treturn shape.X != nil && shape.Y != nil && shape.Z != nil &&\n736\t\t\t\tshape.W != nil && shape.D != nil && shape.H != nil &&\n737\t\t\t\t*shape.W > 0 && *shape.D > 0 && *shape.H > 0\n738\t\tcase volume.ShapeCylinder:\n739\t\t\treturn shape.CX != nil && shape.CY != nil && shape.Z != nil &&\n740\t\t\t\tshape.R != nil && shape.H != nil &&\n741\t\t\t\t*shape.R > 0 && *shape.H > 0\n742\t\tdefault:\n743\t\t\treturn false\n744\t\t}\n745\t}\n746\t\n747\t// toResponse converts a trigger to the API response format.\n748\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) toResponse(t *volume.Trigger, now time.Time) *TriggerResponse {\n749\t\tresp := &TriggerResponse{\n750\t\t\tID:             t.ID,\n751\t\t\tName:           t.Name,\n752\t\t\tShape:          t.Shape,\n753\t\t\tCondition:      t.Condition,\n754\t\t\tConditionParams: t.ConditionParams,\n755\t\t\tTimeConstraint: t.TimeConstraint,\n756\t\t\tActions:        t.Actions,\n757\t\t\tEnabled:        t.Enabled,\n758\t\t\tErrorMessage:  t.ErrorMessage,\n759\t\t\tErrorCount:     t.ErrorCount,\n760\t\t\tLastFired:      t.LastFired,\n761\t\t\tCreatedAt:      t.CreatedAt,\n762\t\t\tUpdatedAt:      t.UpdatedAt,\n763\t\t}\n764\t\n765\t\tif t.LastFired != nil {\n766\t\t\tresp.Elapsed = int(now.Sub(*t.LastFired).Seconds())\n767\t\t}\n768\t\n769\t\treturn resp\n770\t}\n771\t\n772\t// EvaluateTriggers is called by the fusion engine to evaluate triggers.\n773\t// Returns a list of trigger IDs that fired.\n774\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) EvaluateTriggers(blobs []volume.BlobPos) []string {\n775\t\treturn h.store.Evaluate(blobs, time.Now())\n776\t}\n777\t\n778\t// IsInVolume checks if a point is inside a trigger volume.\n779\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) IsInVolume(triggerID string, x, y, z float64) bool {\n780\t\treturn h.store.IsInVolume(triggerID, x, y, z)\n781\t}\n782\t\n783\t// onTriggerFired is called by the volume store when a trigger fires.\n784\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) onTriggerFired(event volume.FiredEvent) {\n785\t\tt := h.store.GetTrigger(event.TriggerID)\n786\t\tif t == nil {\n787\t\t\treturn\n788\t\t}\n789\t\n790\t\t// Execute all actions\n791\t\tfor _, action := range t.Actions {\n792\t\t\th.executeAction(action, event)\n793\t\t}\n794\t\n795\t\t// Broadcast trigger state to dashboard\n796\t\th.mu.RLock()\n797\t\tbroadcaster := h.wsBroadcaster\n798\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n799\t\tif broadcaster != nil {\n800\t\t\tbroadcaster.BroadcastTriggerState(t.ID, t.Name, event.Timestamp, t.Enabled)\n801\t\t}\n802\t\n803\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Trigger fired: %s (%s, %d blob(s))\", t.Name, t.Condition, len(event.BlobIDs))\n804\t}\n805\t\n806\t// executeAction executes a single trigger action.\n807\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) executeAction(action volume.Action, event volume.FiredEvent) {\n808\t\tswitch action.Type {\n809\t\tcase \"webhook\":\n810\t\t\th.executeWebhook(action, event)\n811\t\tcase \"mqtt\":\n812\t\t\th.executeMQTT(action, event)\n813\t\tcase \"ntfy\", \"pushover\":\n814\t\t\th.executeNotification(action, event)\n815\t\t}\n816\t}\n817\t\n818\t// doWebhookPost sends an HTTP POST and returns status code, latency, error.\n819\t// This is the low-level webhook call shared by normal firing and test.\n820\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) doWebhookPost(url string, data []byte, params map[string]interface{}) (statusCode int, latencyMs int64, err error) {\n821\t\treq, err := http.NewRequest(\"POST\", url, bytes.NewReader(data))\n822\t\tif err != nil {\n823\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"create request: %w\", err)\n824\t\t}\n825\t\n826\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n827\t\n828\t\t// Add custom headers from action params\n829\t\tfor k, v := range params {\n830\t\t\tif k != \"url\" {\n831\t\t\t\tif headerStr, ok := v.(string); ok {\n832\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(k, headerStr)\n833\t\t\t\t}\n834\t\t\t}\n835\t\t}\n836\t\n837\t\tstart := time.Now()\n838\t\tresp, err := h.httpClient.Do(req)\n839\t\tlatencyMs = time.Since(start).Milliseconds()\n840\t\n841\t\tif err != nil {\n842\t\t\treturn 0, latencyMs, fmt.Errorf(\"request failed: %w\", err)\n843\t\t}\n844\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n845\t\t// Drain body to allow connection reuse\n846\t\tio.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)\n847\t\n848\t\treturn resp.StatusCode, latencyMs, nil\n849\t}\n850\t\n851\t// executeWebhook sends an HTTP POST to a webhook URL with fault tolerance.\n852\t// - 4xx: disable trigger, set error_message, push WS alert, log webhook_log\n853\t// - 5xx / timeout: log warning, increment error_count, do NOT disable\n854\t// - 2xx: reset error_count, log webhook_log\n855\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) executeWebhook(action volume.Action, event volume.FiredEvent) {\n856\t\turl, ok := action.Params[\"url\"].(string)\n857\t\tif !ok || url == \"\" {\n858\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Webhook action missing URL\")\n859\t\t\treturn\n860\t\t}\n861\t\n862\t\t// Build payload per spec:\n863\t\t// {trigger_id, trigger_name, condition, blob_id, person, position:{x,y,z}, zone, dwell_s, timestamp_ms}\n864\t\tt := h.store.GetTrigger(event.TriggerID)\n865\t\tpayload := map[string]interface{}{\n866\t\t\t\"trigger_id\":   event.TriggerID,\n867\t\t\t\"trigger_name\":  t.Name,\n868\t\t\t\"condition\":    t.Condition,\n869\t\t\t\"blob_id\":      0,\n870\t\t\t\"person\":       nil,\n871\t\t\t\"position\":     map[string]float64{\"x\": 0, \"y\": 0, \"z\": 0},\n872\t\t\t\"zone\":         nil,\n873\t\t\t\"dwell_s\":      0,\n874\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\":  event.Timestamp.UnixMilli(),\n875\t\t}\n876\t\n877\t\tdata, err := json.Marshal(payload)\n878\t\tif err != nil {\n879\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to marshal webhook payload: %v\", err)\n880\t\t\treturn\n881\t\t}\n882\t\n883\t\tstatusCode, latencyMs, reqErr := h.doWebhookPost(url, data, action.Params)\n884\t\tfiredAtMs := event.Timestamp.UnixMilli()\n885\t\n886\t\t// Log the attempt\n887\t\tlogErr := \"\"\n888\t\tif reqErr != nil {\n889\t\t\tlogErr = reqErr.Error()\n890\t\t}\n891\t\th.store.WriteWebhookLog(event.TriggerID, url, firedAtMs, statusCode, latencyMs, logErr)\n892\t\n893\t\t// Error handling based on response class\n894\t\tif reqErr != nil {\n895\t\t\t// Timeout or network error — treat as 5xx-equivalent\n896\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Webhook request failed for trigger %q: %v\", t.Name, reqErr)\n897\t\t\th.store.IncrementErrorCount(event.TriggerID)\n898\t\t\treturn\n899\t\t}\n900\t\n901\t\tif statusCode >= 400 && statusCode < 500 {\n902\t\t\t// 4xx — client error, likely misconfigured URL\n903\t\t\terrMsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Webhook returned HTTP %d — trigger disabled. Fix the URL and re-enable.\", statusCode)\n904\t\t\th.store.DisableTriggerWithError(event.TriggerID, errMsg)\n905\t\n906\t\t\t// Push WS alert to dashboard\n907\t\t\th.mu.RLock()\n908\t\t\tbroadcaster := h.wsBroadcaster\n909\t\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n910\t\t\tif broadcaster != nil {\n911\t\t\t\tbroadcaster.BroadcastAlert(\"trigger_disabled\", time.Now(), \"warning\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Trigger %q disabled: webhook returned HTTP %d\", t.Name, statusCode), false)\n912\t\t\t}\n913\t\t\treturn\n914\t\t}\n915\t\n916\t\tif statusCode >= 500 {\n917\t\t\t// 5xx — server error, transient\n918\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Webhook returned HTTP %d for trigger %q (server error, not disabling)\", statusCode, t.Name)\n919\t\t\th.store.IncrementErrorCount(event.TriggerID)\n920\t\t\treturn\n921\t\t}\n922\t\n923\t\t// 2xx — success, reset error
