{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_5e92a0ba49074405b6ca13b5","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t// Package replay provides a disk-backed circular buffer for raw CSI frames.\n2\t//\n3\t// File layout:\n4\t//\n5\t//\tHeader (32 bytes):\n6\t//\t  magic[8]     \"SPAXLREP\"\n7\t//\t  writePos[8]  uint64 LE — absolute file offset of next write\n8\t//\t  oldestPos[8] uint64 LE — absolute file offset of oldest valid record (0 = empty)\n9\t//\t  wrapPos[8]   uint64 LE — writePos at last wrap point (0 = no pending wrap)\n10\t//\n11\t//\tRecord (10 + frameLen bytes):\n12\t//\t  recvTimeNS[8] int64 LE — Unix nanosecond receive timestamp\n13\t//\t  frameLen[2]   uint16 LE — length of following frame bytes\n14\t//\t  frameData[N]  raw CSI frame bytes\n15\t//\n16\t// Eviction: when the oldest record's successor would reach wrapPos, oldest wraps\n17\t// to headerSize. New writes evict oldest records as needed to make room.\n18\tpackage replay\n19\t\n20\timport (\n21\t\t\"encoding/binary\"\n22\t\t\"errors\"\n23\t\t\"os\"\n24\t\t\"sync\"\n25\t)\n26\t\n27\tconst (\n28\t\tfileMagic      = \"SPAXLREP\"\n29\t\theaderSize     = int64(32)  // magic(8) + writePos(8) + oldestPos(8) + wrapPos(8)\n30\t\trecordOverhead = int64(10)  // recvTimeNS(8) + frameLen(2)\n31\t\tmaxFrameBytes  = int64(280) // per plan: max CSI frame = 24 + 128*2\n32\t\n33\t\t// DefaultMaxMB is the default recording buffer capacity in megabytes (~48h at 20 Hz, 20 links).\n34\t\tDefaultMaxMB = 360\n35\t)\n36\t\n37\t// RecordingStore is a disk-backed circular buffer for raw CSI frames.\n38\t// It is safe for concurrent use.\n39\ttype RecordingStore struct {\n40\t\tmu       sync.Mutex\n41\t\tf        *os.File\n42\t\tfileSize int64 // total file size including header\n43\t\twritePos int64 // absolute file offset of next write\n44\t\toldestPos int64 // absolute file offset of oldest valid record (0 = empty)\n45\t\twrapPos  int64 // writePos at time of last wrap (0 = no pending wrap)\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\t// NewRecordingStore opens or creates a recording store at path.\n49\t// maxMB is the data capacity; pass 0 to use DefaultMaxMB.\n50\tfunc NewRecordingStore(path string, maxMB int) (*RecordingStore, error) {\n51\t\tif maxMB <= 0 {\n52\t\t\tmaxMB = DefaultMaxMB\n53\t\t}\n54\t\tfileSize := headerSize + int64(maxMB)*1024*1024\n55\t\n56\t\tif fileSize-headerSize < maxFrameBytes+recordOverhead {\n57\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"replay: maxMB too small for a single record\")\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\tf, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_RDWR|os.O_CREATE, 0644)"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_751c7b698c8b4faeb9495bc9","session_id":"e969917b-904e-4516-80e4-c0016537e6d7","uuid":"dbc250a0-67df-4811-adae-0100e72b3a0e","timestamp":"2026-04-25T12:47:20.199Z"}

entry.\n134\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tble\n135\t//\t@Accept\t\t\tjson\n136\t//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n137\t//\t@Param\t\t\trequest\tbody\tsplitDeviceRequest\ttrue\t\"Canonical and alias addresses\"\n138\t//\t@Success\t\t200\t\t{object}\tmap[string]interface{}\t\"Split devices\"\n139\t//\t@Failure\t\t400\t\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Invalid request\"\n140\t//\t@Failure\t\t404\t\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Device not found\"\n141\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/ble/split [post]\n142\t//\n143\t// GET /api/people\n144\t//\n145\t//\t@Summary\t\tList people\n146\t//\t@Description\tReturns all people with their associated device counts and devices.\n147\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tpeople\n148\t//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n149\t//\t@Success\t\t200\t{array}\t\tmap[string]interface{}\t\"List of people with devices\"\n150\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/people [get]\n151\t//\n152\t// POST /api/people\n153\t//\n154\t//\t@Summary\t\tCreate person\n155\t//\t@Description\tCreates a new person for BLE device assignment.\n156\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tpeople\n157\t//\t@Accept\t\t\tjson\n158\t//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n159\t//\t@Param\t\t\trequest\tbody\tcreatePersonRequest\ttrue\t\"Person name and optional color\"\n160\t//\t@Success\t\t201\t\t{object}\tPerson\t\"Created person\"\n161\t//\t@Failure\t\t400\t\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Invalid request\"\n162\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/people [post]\n163\t//\n164\t// GET /api/people/{id}\n165\t//\n166\t//\t@Summary\t\tGet person\n167\t//\t@Description\tReturns a single person with their associated devices.\n168\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tpeople\n169\t//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n170\t//\t@Param\t\t\tid\t\tpath\t\tstring\ttrue\t\"Person UUID\"\n171\t//\t@Success\t\t200\t\t{object}\tmap[string]interface{}\t\"Person with devices\"\n172\t//\t@Failure\t\t404\t\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Person not found\"\n173\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/people/{id} [get]\n174\t//\n175\t// PUT /api/people/{id}\n176\t//\n177\t//\t@Summary\t\tUpdate person\n178\t//\t@Description\tUpdates a person's name and/or color.\n179\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tpeople\n180\t//\t@Accept\t\t\tjson\n181\t//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n182\t//\t@Param\t\t\tid\t\tpath\t\tstring\t\t\t\ttrue\t\"Person UUID\"\n183\t//\t@Param\t\t\trequest\tbody\tupdatePersonRequest\ttrue\t\"Person update fields\"\n184\t//\t@Success\t\t200\t\t{object}\tPerson\t\"Updated person\"\n185\t//\t@Failure\t\t400\t\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Invalid request\"\n186\t//\t@Failure\t\t404\t\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Person not found\"\n187\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/people/{id} [put]\n188\t//\n189\t// DELETE /api/people/{id}\n190\t//\n191\t//\t@Summary\t\tDelete person\n192\t//\t@Description\tDeletes a person and unassigns all their devices.\n193\t//\t@Tags\t\t\tpeople\n194\t//\t@Param\t\t\tid\t\tpath\t\tstring\ttrue\t\"Person UUID\"\n195\t//\t@Success\t\t204\t\t\"No content\"\n196\t//\t@Failure\t\t404\t\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Person not found\"\n197\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/people/{id} [delete]\n198\tfunc (h *Handler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n199\t\t// Device endpoints\n200\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices\", h.listDevices)\n201\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.getDevice)\n202\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}/history\", h.getDeviceHistory)\n203\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}/aliases\", h.getDeviceAliases)\n204\t\tr.Put(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.updateDevice)\n205\t\tr.Delete(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.archiveDevice)\n206\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/devices/preregister\", h.preregisterDevice)\n207\t\n208\t\t// Duplicate detection\n209\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/duplicates\", h.listDuplicates)\n210\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/merge\", h.mergeDevices)\n211\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/split\", h.splitDevice)\n212\t\n213\t\t// People endpoints\n214\t\tr.Get(\"/api/people\", h.listPeople)\n215\t\tr.Post(\"/api/people\", h.createPerson)\n216\t\tr.Get(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.getPerson)\n217\t\tr.Put(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.updatePerson)\n218\t\tr.Delete(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.deletePerson)\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t// ── Device endpoints ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n222\t\n223\t// listDevices handles GET /api/ble/devices.\n224\t//\n225\t// Returns a list of all BLE devices seen by the system. Devices can be filtered\n226\t// by registration status (registered/discovered), time window (hours parameter),\n227\t// and archival status.\n228\t//\n229\t// Query parameters:\n230\t//   - registered: \"true\" to return only devices assigned to a person\n231\t//   - discovered: \"true\" to return only unassigned devices\n232\t//   - archived: \"true\" to include archived (soft-deleted) devices\n233\t//   - hours: time window in hours (default: 24)\n234\t//\n235\t// Response: JSON object with \"devices\" array and \"privacy_notice\" string.\n236\t// Each device includes: mac, name, label, manufacturer, device_type, device_name,\n237\t// person_id, person_name, rssi_min, rssi_max, rssi_avg, first_seen_at, last_seen_at,\n238\t// last_seen_node, is_archived, is_wearable, enabled, last_location.\n239\t//\n240\t// Status codes:\n241\t//   - 200: Success\n242\t//   - 500: Internal error\n243\tfunc (h *Handler) listDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n244\t\tincludeArchived := r.URL.Query().Get(\"archived\") == \"true\"\n245\t\tregistered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"registered\")\n246\t\tdiscovered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"discovered\")\n247\t\n248\t\t// Parse hours parameter (default: 24 hours)\n249\t\thoursStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"hours\")\n250\t\thours := 24 // Default to last 24 hours\n251\t\tif hoursStr != \"\" {\n252\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(hoursStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n253\t\t\t\thours = n\n254\t\t\t}\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\tvar devices []DeviceRecord\n258\t\tvar err error\n259\t\n260\t\t// Filter by registration status and time window\n261\t\tif registered == \"true\" {\n262\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n263\t\t\t// Filter to only registered devices (has person_id)\n264\t\t\tvar registeredDevices []DeviceRecord\n265\t\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n266\t\t\t\tif d.PersonID != \"\" {\n267\t\t\t\t\tregisteredDevices = append(registeredDevices, d)\n268\t\t\t\t}\n269\t\t\t}\n270\t\t\tdevices = registeredDevices\n271\t\t} else if discovered == \"true\" {\n272\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n273\t\t\t// Filter to only unregistered devices (no person_id)\n274\t\t\tvar unregisteredDevices []DeviceRecord\n275\t\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n276\t\t\t\tif d.PersonID == \"\" {\n277\t\t\t\t\tunregisteredDevices = append(unregisteredDevices, d)\n278\t\t\t\t}\n279\t\t\t}\n280\t\t\tdevices = unregisteredDevices\n281\t\t} else {\n282\t\t\t// Get all devices seen in the time window\n283\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n284\t\t}\n285\t\n286\t\tif err != nil {\n287\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n288\t\t\treturn\n289\t\t}\n290\t\tif devices == nil {\n291\t\t\tdevices = []DeviceRecord{}\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\t// Add privacy notice in response header\n295\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Privacy-Notice\", \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\")\n296\t\n297\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n298\t\t\t\"devices\":       devices,\n299\t\t\t\"privacy_notice\": \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\",\n300\t\t})\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\t// getDevice handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n304\t//\n305\t// Returns detailed information about a single BLE device by its MAC address.\n306\t// The MAC address should be in uppercase colon-separated hex format (e.g., \"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\").\n307\t//\n308\t// URL parameters:\n309\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n310\t//\n311\t// Response: JSON device object with all fields including location history.\n312\t//\n313\t// Status codes:\n314\t//   - 200: Success, device found\n315\t//   - 404: Device not found\n316\t//   - 500: Internal error\n317\tfunc (h *Handler) getDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n318\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n319\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n320\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n321\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n322\t\t\treturn\n323\t\t}\n324\t\tif err != nil {\n325\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n326\t\t\treturn\n327\t\t}\n328\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// getDeviceHistory handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}/history.\n332\t//\n333\t// Returns the sighting history for a specific BLE device. This includes\n334\t// RSSI observations from nodes that have detected this device over time.\n335\t//\n336\t// URL parameters:\n337\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n338\t//\n339\t// Query parameters:\n340\t//   - limit: maximum number of history entries to return (default: 100, max: 1000)\n341\t//\n342\t// Response: JSON object with \"mac\", \"history\" (array of sighting entries),\n343\t// and \"limit\" fields. Each history entry includes timestamp, rssi_dbm, and node_mac.\n344\t//\n345\t// Status codes:\n346\t//   - 200: Success\n347\t//   - 404: Device not found\n348\t//   - 500: Internal error\n349\tfunc (h *Handler) getDeviceHistory(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n350\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n351\t\n352\t\t// Parse limit parameter\n353\t\tlimitStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\")\n354\t\tlimit := 100\n355\t\tif limitStr != \"\" {\n356\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(limitStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n357\t\t\t\tlimit = n\n358\t\t\t}\n359\t\t}\n360\t\n361\t\t// Check that the device exists first\n362\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); err != nil {\n363\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n364\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n365\t\t\t\treturn\n366\t\t\t}\n367\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n368\t\t\treturn\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\thistory, err := h.registry.GetDeviceSightingHistory(mac, limit)\n372\t\tif err != nil {\n373\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n374\t\t\treturn\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n378\t\t\t\"mac\":     mac,\n379\t\t\t\"history\": history,\n380\t\t\t\"limit\":   limit,\n381\t\t})\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\ttype updateDeviceRequest struct {\n385\t\tLabel      string `json:\"label\"`       // User-assigned display label\n386\t\tDeviceType string `json:\"device_type\"` // Device type (apple_phone, apple_watch, tile, etc.)\n387\t\tPersonID   string `json:\"person_id\"`   // Person ID to assign device to\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// updateDevice handles PUT /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n391\t//\n392\t// Updates a BLE device's properties. This endpoint is used to set a human-readable\n393\t// label for a device and/or assign it to a person for identity tracking.\n394\t//\n395\t// URL parameters:\n396\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address (uppercase colon-separated hex)\n397\t//\n398\t// Request body: JSON object with optional fields:\n399\t//   - label: User-assigned display label (e.g., \"Alice's iPhone\")\n400\t//   - device_type: Device type identifier (e.g., \"apple_phone\", \"tile\", \"fitbit\")\n401\t//   - person_id: UUID of person to assign this device to (must exist)\n402\t//\n403\t// Response: Updated device object as JSON.\n404\t//\n405\t// Status codes:\n406\t//   - 200: Success, device updated\n407\t//   - 400: Invalid request body or person_id not found\n408\t//   - 404: Device not found\n409\t//   - 500: Internal error\n410\tfunc (h *Handler) updateDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n411\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n412\t\n413\t\t// Verify device exists\n414\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n415\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n416\t\t\treturn\n417\t\t} else if err != nil {\n418\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n419\t\t\treturn\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\tvar req updateDeviceRequest\n423\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n424\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n425\t\t\treturn\n426\t\t}\n427\t\n428\t\tupdates := make(map[string]interface{})\n429\t\n430\t\tif req.Label != \"\" {\n431\t\t\tupdates[\"label\"] = req.Label\n432\t\t}\n433\t\tif req.DeviceType != \"\" {\n434\t\t\tupdates[\"device_type\"] = req.DeviceType\n435\t\t}\n436\t\tif req.PersonID != \"\" {\n437\t\t\t// Verify person exists\n438\t\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(req.PersonID); err != nil {\n439\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n440\t\t\t\treturn\n441\t\t\t}\n442\t\t\tupdates[\"person_id\"] = req.PersonID\n443\t\t}\n444\t\n445\t\tif len(updates) > 0 {\n446\t\t\tif err := h.registry.UpdateDevice(mac, updates); err != nil {\n447\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n448\t\t\t\treturn\n449\t\t\t}\n450\t\t}\n451\t\n452\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n453\t\tif err != nil {\n454\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n455\t\t\treturn\n456\t\t}\n457\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\tfunc (h *Handler) archiveDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n461\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n462\t\n463\t\t// Verify device exists\n464\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n465\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n466\t\t\treturn\n467\t\t} else if err != nil {\n468\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n469\t\t\treturn\n470\t\t}\n471\t\n472\t\tif err := h.registry.ArchiveDevice(mac); err != nil {\n473\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n474\t\t\treturn\n475\t\t}\n476\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n477\t}\n478\t\n479\t// ── Duplicate detection endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n480\t\n481\tfunc (h *Handler) listDuplicates(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n482\t\tduplicates, err := h.registry.DetectPossibleDuplicates()\n483\t\tif err != nil {\n484\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n485\t\t\treturn\n486\t\t}\n487\t\tif duplicates == nil {\n488\t\t\tduplicates = []PossibleDuplicate{}\n489\t\t}\n490\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n491\t\t\t\"duplicates\": duplicates,\n492\t\t\t\"message\":    \"These devices may be the same device with a rotated MAC address. Review and merge if appropriate.\",\n493\t\t})\n494\t}\n495\t\n496\ttype mergeDevicesRequest struct {\n497\t\tMAC1 string `json:\"mac1\"`\n498\t\tMAC2 string `json:\"mac2\"`\n499\t}\n500\t\n501\tfunc (h *Handler) mergeDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n502\t\tvar req mergeDevicesRequest\n503\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.MAC1 == \"\" || req.MAC2 == \"\" {\n504\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n505\t\t\treturn\n506\t\t}\n507\t\n508\t\tif req.MAC1 == req.MAC2 {\n509\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"cannot merge device with itself\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n510\t\t\treturn\n511\t\t}\n512\t\n513\t\t// Verify both devices exist\n514\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC1); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n515\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device 1 not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n516\t\t\treturn\n517\t\t}\n518\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC2); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n519\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device 2 not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n520\t\t\treturn\n521\t\t}\n522\t\n523\t\tif err := h.registry.MergeDevices(req.MAC1, req.MAC2); err != nil {\n524\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n525\t\t\treturn\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC1)\n529\t\tif err != nil {\n530\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n531\t\t\treturn\n532\t\t}\n533\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n534\t\t\t\"merged\": device,\n535\t\t\t\"message\": \"Devices merged successfully. \" + req.MAC2 + \" has been removed.\",\n536\t\t})\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\t// ── People endpoints ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n540\t\n541\tfunc (h *Handler) listPeople(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n542\t\tpeople, err := h.registry.GetPeopleWithDevices()\n543\t\tif err != nil {\n544\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n545\t\t\treturn\n546\t\t}\n547\t\tif people == nil {\n548\t\t\tpeople = []map[string]interface{}{}\n549\t\t}\n550\t\twriteJSON(w, people)\n551\t}\n552\t\n553\ttype createPersonRequest struct {\n554\t\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n555\t\tColor string `json:\"color\"`\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\tfunc (h *Handler) createPerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n559\t\tvar req createPersonRequest\n560\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.Name == \"\" {\n561\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n562\t\t\treturn\n563\t\t}\n564\t\n565\t\t// Default color if not provided\n566\t\tif req.Color == \"\" {\n567\t\t\treq.Color = \"#3b82f6\"\n568\t\t}\n569\t\n570\t\tperson, err := h.registry.CreatePerson(req.Name, req.Color)\n571\t\tif err != nil {\n572\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n573\t\t\treturn\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)\n577\t\twriteJSON(w, person)\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\tfunc (h *Handler) getPerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n581\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n582\t\n583\t\tperson, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id)\n584\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n585\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n586\t\t\treturn\n587\t\t}\n588\t\tif err != nil {\n589\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n590\t\t\treturn\n591\t\t}\n592\t\n593\t\t// Get devices for this person\n594\t\tdevices, err := h.registry.GetPersonDevices(id)\n595\t\tif err != nil {\n596\t\t\tdevices = nil\n597\t\t}\n598\t\n599\t\t// Find most recent last_seen among devices\n600\t\tvar lastSeen time.Time\n601\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n602\t\t\tif d.LastSeenAt.After(lastSeen) {\n603\t\t\t\tlastSeen = d.LastSeenAt\n604\t\t\t}\n605\t\t}\n606\t\n607\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n608\t\t\t\"id\":           person.ID,\n609\t\t\t\"name\":         person.Name,\n610\t\t\t\"color\":        person.Color,\n611\t\t\t\"created_at\":   person.CreatedAt,\n612\t\t\t\"device_count\": len(devices),\n613\t\t\t\"devices\":      devices,\n614\t\t\t\"last_seen\":    lastSeen,\n615\t\t})\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\ttype updatePersonRequest struct {\n619\t\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n620\t\tColor string `json:\"color\"`\n621\t}\n622\t\n623\tfunc (h *Handler) updatePerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n624\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n625\t\n626\t\t// Verify person exists\n627\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n628\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n629\t\t\treturn\n630\t\t} else if err != nil {\n631\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n632\t\t\treturn\n633\t\t}\n634\t\n635\t\tvar req updatePersonRequest\n636\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n637\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n638\t\t\treturn\n639\t\t}\n640\t\n641\t\tif req.Name == \"\" && req.Color == \"\" {\n642\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"no updates provided\", HTTP Route Registration Pattern\n\nRoutes are registered in `/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go` in a single massive function. The pattern is:\n\n**A) Handler classes with `RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router)` method:**\nEach API handler type has a `RegisterRoutes` method that receives a `chi.Router` and mounts its endpoints directly. Examples:\n\n```go\nsettingsHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts GET/POST/PATCH /api/settings\nzonesHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                 // mounts GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/zones, /api/portals\nvolumeTriggersHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)        // mounts full triggers CRUD\nsecurityHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts /api/security/* and /api/mode\nbleHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                   // mounts /api/ble/* and /api/people/*\neventsHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                // mounts /api/events/*\nbriefingHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts /api/briefings/*\nanalyticsHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)             // mounts /api/analytics/*\nlearningHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts /api/learning/*\nfeedbackHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts /api/feedback/*\nguidedHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                // mounts /api/guided/*\nexplainabilityHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)        // mounts /api/explainability/*\nsleepHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                 // mounts /api/sleep/*\ntracksHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                // mounts /api/tracks/*\ndiurnalHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)               // mounts /api/diurnal/*\nsimulatorHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)             // mounts /simulator/*\n```\n\n**B) Inline anonymous handlers in main.go:**\nMany endpoints are registered as inline closures directly in `main.go`, especially for:\n- `/api/automations/*` (full CRUD for automations/volumes/events)\n- `/api/notifications/channels`, `/api/notifications/config`, `/api/notifications/history`, `/api/notifications/quiet-hours`, `/api/notifications/test`\n- `/api/weather/*`, `/api/coverage/*`, `/api/healing/*`, `/api/health/system`\n- `/api/diurnal/*` (also has a separate handler)\n- `/api/links/*`, `/api/diagnostics/*`\n- `/api/predictions/*` (extensive inline registration)\n- `/api/localization/*`\n- `/api/accuracy/*`\n- `/api/security`, `/api/security/acknowledge-all`\n- `/api/blobs`\n- `/api/firmware/*`, `/api/provision`\n- `/api/mode`\n- `/ambient`, `/fleet`, `/live`, `/setup` (static file serving)\n\n---\n\n### 5. API Handler Pattern\n\nAll handlers follow a consistent pattern:\n\n```go\ntype XxxHandler struct {\n    mu   sync.RWMutex\n    db   *sql.DB           // or *zones.Manager, *ble.Registry, etc.\n    // dependencies...\n}\n\nfunc NewXxxHandler(...) *XxxHandler { ... }\nfunc (h *XxxHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) { ... }\nfunc (h *XxxHandler) handleXxx(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { ... }\n```\n\n**Common patterns:**\n- JSON request decoding with `json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req)`\n- JSON response via `writeJSON(w, status, data)` from `utils.go`\n- Error responses via `writeJSONError(w, status, \"message\")` -- returns `{\"error\": \"message\"}`\n- URL parameters via `chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")`\n- Query parameters via `r.URL.Query().Get(\"key\")`\n- Concurrency protection with `sync.RWMutex`\n- Methods like `Set*()` for dependency injection after construction\n\n**Note:** The `ble` package has its own `writeJSON` helper (same signature), while all other API handlers use the shared one in `api/utils.go`.\n\n---\n\n### 6. SQLite Database Layer\n\n**Database initialization** (`/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/db/db.go`):\n- `OpenDB(ctx, dataDir, dbName)` -- 4-phase startup: data dir + flock, SQLite open (WAL mode, `busy_timeout=5000`), schema migration, config/secrets\n- Uses `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure Go, no CGO)\n- `db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)` -- single-writer for SQLite\n- Auto-heals corrupt databases by renaming and starting fresh\n- Auto-generates 32-byte install secret on first run\n\n**Migration framework** (`/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/db/migrate.go`):\n- Versioned migrations with `Migrator` struct\n- Each migration: `func(*sql.Tx) error`\n- Pre-migration backup via `VACUUM INTO`\n- Backup pruning (90-day retention)\n- `schema_migrations` table tracks applied versions\n\n**15 migrations** (`/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/db/migrations.go`):\n\n| # HTTP Route Registration Pattern\n\nRoutes are registered in `/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go` in a single massive function. The pattern is:\n\n**A) Handler classes with `RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router)` method:**\nEach API handler type has a `RegisterRoutes` method that receives a `chi.Router` and mounts its endpoints directly. Examples:\n\n```go\nsettingsHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts GET/POST/PATCH /api/settings\nzonesHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                 // mounts GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/zones, /api/portals\nvolumeTriggersHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)        // mounts full triggers CRUD\nsecurityHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts /api/security/* and /api/mode\nbleHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                   // mounts /api/ble/* and /api/people/*\neventsHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                // mounts /api/events/*\nbriefingHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts /api/briefings/*\nanalyticsHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)             // mounts /api/analytics/*\nlearningHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts /api/learning/*\nfeedbackHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)              // mounts /api/feedback/*\nguidedHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                // mounts /api/guided/*\nexplainabilityHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)        // mounts /api/explainability/*\nsleepHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                 // mounts /api/sleep/*\ntracksHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)                // mounts /api/tracks/*\ndiurnalHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)               // mounts /api/diurnal/*\nsimulatorHandler.RegisterRoutes(r)             // mounts /simulator/*\n```\n\n**B) Inline anonymous handlers in main.go:**\nMany endpoints are registered as inline closures directly in `main.go`, especially for:\n- `/api/automations/*` (full CRUD for automations/volumes/events)\n- `/api/notifications/channels`, `/api/notifications/config`, `/api/notifications/history`, `/api/notifications/quiet-hours`, `/api/notifications/test`\n- `/api/weather/*`, `/api/coverage/*`, `/api/healing/*`, `/api/health/system`\n- `/api/diurnal/*` (also has a separate handler)\n- `/api/links/*`, `/api/diagnostics/*`\n- `/api/predictions/*` (extensive inline registration)\n- `/api/localization/*`\n- `/api/accuracy/*`\n- `/api/security`, `/api/security/acknowledge-all`\n- `/api/blobs`\n- `/api/firmware/*`, `/api/provision`\n- `/api/mode`\n- `/ambient`, `/fleet`, `/live`, `/setup` (static file serving)\n\n---\n\n### 5. API Handler Pattern\n\nAll handlers follow a consistent pattern:\n\n```go\ntype XxxHandler struct {\n    mu   sync.RWMutex\n    db   *sql.DB           // or *zones.Manager, *ble.Registry, etc.\n    // dependencies...\n}\n\nfunc NewXxxHandler(...) *XxxHandler { ... }\nfunc (h *XxxHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) { ... }\nfunc (h *XxxHandler) handleXxx(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { ... }\n```\n\n**Common patterns:**\n- JSON request decoding with `json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req)`\n- JSON response via `writeJSON(w, status, data)` from `utils.go`\n- Error responses via `writeJSONError(w, status, \"message\")` -- returns `{\"error\": \"message\"}`\n- URL parameters via `chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")`\n- Query parameters via `r.URL.Query().Get(\"key\")`\n- Concurrency protection with `sync.RWMutex`\n- Methods like `Set*()` for dependency injection after construction\n\n**Note:** The `ble` package has its own `writeJSON` helper (same signature), while all other API handlers use the shared one in `api/utils.go`.\n\n---\n\n### 6. h.mgr.Close()\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// RegisterRoutes registers zones and portals endpoints.\n159\t//\n160\t// Zones:\n161\t//\n162\t//\tGET /api/zones\n163\t//\n164\t//\t@Summary\t\tList all from the database.\n234\tfunc (h *NotificationSettingsHandler) getSettings() (*notificationSettingsResponse, error) {\n235\t\th.mu.RLock()\n236\t\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n237\t\n238\t\tresponse := &notificationSettingsResponse{\n239\t\t\t// Set defaults\n240\t\t\tChannelType:            \"none\",\n241\t\t\tChannelConfig:          make(map[string]interface{}),\n242\t\t\tQuietHoursDays:         0x7F, // All days\n243\t\t\tMorningDigestTime:      \"07:00\",\n244\t\t\tSmartBatchingEnabled:   true,\n245\t\t\tSmartBatchingWindow:    30,\n246\t\t\tEventTypes: key = 'notification_morning_digest_enabled'`).Scan(&morningDigest)\n301\t\tif err == nil {\n302\t\t\tresponse.MorningDigestEnabled = morningDigest != 0\n303\t\t} else {\n304\t\t\tresponse.MorningDigestEnabled = true // Default on\n305\t\t}\n306\t\n307\t\t// Get morning digest time\n308\t\tvar digestTimeJSON string\n309\t\terr = h.db.QueryRow(`SELECT value_json FROM settings WHERE key = 'notification_morning_digest_time'`).Scan(&digestTimeJSON)\n310\t\tif err == nil {\n311\t\t\tjson.Unmarshal([]byte(digestTimeJSON), &response.MorningDigestTime) //nolint:errcheck\n312\t\t}\n313\t\n314\t\t// Get smart batching enabled\n315\t\tvar smartBatching int\n316\t\terr = h.db.QueryRow(`SELECT value_json FROM settings WHERE key = 'notification_smart_batching_enabled'`).Scan(&smartBatching)\n317\t\tif err == nil {\n318\t\t\tresponse.SmartBatchingEnabled = smartBatching != 0\n319\t\t}\n320\t\n321\t\t// Get smart batching {\n325\t\t\tresponse.SmartBatchingWindow = batchWindow\n326\t\t}\n327\t\n328\t\t// Get event type preferences\n329\t\tvar eventTypesJSON string\n330\t\terr = h.db.QueryRow(`SELECT value_json FROM err != nil {\n205\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Reason: \"config must be a JSON object\"}\n206\t\t\t}\n207\t\t}\n208\t\n209\t\tswitch channelType {\n210\t\tcase \"ntfy\":\n211\t\t\t// url is required, token is optional\n212\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n213\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n214\t\t\t}\n215\t\tcase \"pushover\":\n216\t\t\t// app_token and user_key are required\n217\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"app_token\"]; !ok {\n218\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"app_token\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n219\t\t\t}\n220\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"user_key\"]; !ok {\n221\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"user_key\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n222\t\t\t}\n223\t\tcase \"gotify\":\n224\t\t\t// url and token are required\n225\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n226\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n227\t\t\t}\n228\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"token\"]; !ok {\n229\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"token\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n230\t\t\t}\n231\t\tcase \"webhook\":\n232\t\t\t// url is required, method and headers are optional\n233\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n234\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\t\t// Validate method if provided\n237\t\t\tif method, ok := configMap[\"method\"].(string); {\n218\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n219\t\t\treturn\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tfromMS, err := parseISO8601(req.FromISO8601)\n223\t\tif err != nil {\n224\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid from_iso8601: \" + err.Error()})\n225\t\t\treturn\n226\t\t}\n227\t\n228\t\ttoMS := timeNowMillis()\n229\t\tif req.ToISO8601 != \"\" {\n230\t\t\ttoMS, err = parseISO8601(req.ToISO8601)\n231\t\t\tif err != nil {\n232\t\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid to_iso8601: \" + err.Error()})\n233\t\t\t\treturn\n234\t\t\t}\n235\t\t}\n236\t\n237\t\tif toMS < fromMS {\n238\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"to_iso8601 must be after from_iso8601\"})\n239\t\t\treturn\n240\t\t}\n241\t\n242\t\tspeed := req.Speed\n243\t\tif speed == 0 {\n244\t\t\tspeed = 1\n245\t\t}\n246\t\tif speed != 1 && speed != 2 && speed != 5 {\n247\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"speed must be 1, 2, or 5\"})\n248\t\t\treturn\n249\t\t}\n250\t\n251\t\t// Start session via worker\n252\t\tsessionID, err := h.worker.StartSession(fromMS, toMS, speed)\n253\t\tif err != nil {\n254\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n255\t\t\treturn\n256\t\t}\n257\t\n258\t\t// Track session in API layer\n259\t\th.mu.Lock()\n260\t\tsession := &_replaySession{\n261\t\t\tID:        sessionID,\n262\t\t\tFromMS:    fromMS,\n263\t\t\tToMS:      toMS,\n264\t\t\tCurrentMS: fromMS,\n265\t\t\tSpeed:     speed,\n266\t\t\tState:     \"paused\",\n267\t\t\tParams:    make(map[string]interface{}),\n268\t\t\tCreatedAt: formatTimestamp(fromMS),\n269\t\t}\n270\t\th.sessions[sessionID] = session\n271\t\th.nextID++\n272\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n273\t\n274\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session started: %s (from %d to %d, speed %dx)\",\n275\t\t\tsessionID, fromMS, toMS, speed)\n276\t\n277\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n278\t\t\t\"session_id\": sessionID,\n279\t\t\t\"from_ms\":    fromMS,\n280\t\t\t\"to_ms\":      toMS,\n281\t\t\t\"speed\":      speed,\n282\t\t\t\"state\":      \"paused\",\n283\t\t})\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t// stopSessionRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/stop.\n287\ttype stopSessionRequest struct {\n288\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to stop.\n289\t\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t// stopSession handles POST /api/replay/stop.\n293\t// Stops the specified seekRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/seek.\n323\ttype seekRequest struct {\n324\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to seek within.\n325\t\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n326\t\t// TimestampISO8601 is the target timestamp in ISO8601 format.\n327\t\tTimestampISO8601 string `json:\"timestamp_iso8601\"`\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t// seek handles POST /api/replay/seek.\n331\t// Seeks to the specified timestamp within the session.\n332\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) seek(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n333\t\tvar req seekRequest\n334\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n335\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n369\t\t\treturn\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\t// Update API layer session state\n373\t\th.mu.Lock()\n374\t\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n375\t\t\ts.CurrentMS = represents the request body for POST /api/replay/tune.\n390\ttype tuneRequest struct {\n391\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to tune.\n392\t\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n393\t\t// DeltaRMSThreshold is the motion detection threshold (0.001-1.0).\n394\t\tDeltaRMSThreshold *float64 `json:\"delta_rms_threshold,omitempty\"`\n395\t\t// TauS is the EMA baseline time constant in seconds (1-600).\n396\t\tTauS *float64 `json:\"tau_s,omitempty\"`\n397\t\t// FresnelDecay is the Fresnel zone weight decay rate (1.0-4.0).\n398\t\tFresnelDecay *float64 `json:\"fresnel_decay,omitempty\"`\n399\t\t// Subcarriers is the number of subcarriers for NBVI selection (8-47).\n400\t\tSubcarriers *int `json:\"n_subcarriers,omitempty\"`\n401\t\t// handles POST /api/replay/tune.\n406\t// Updates pipeline parameters for the replay session.\n407\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) tune(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n408\t\tvar req err.Error()})\n411\t\t\treturn\n412\t\t}\n413\t\n414\t\tif _, err := h.worker.GetSession(req.SessionID); err != nil {\n415\t\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n416\t\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n417\t\t\t\treturn\n418\t\t\t}\n419\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": req.SessionID, params)\n447\t\n448\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n449\t\t\t\"status\":  \"tuned\",\n450\t\t\t\"params\":  params,\n451\t\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n452\t\t})\n453\t}\n454\t\n455\t// setSpeedRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/set-speed.\n456\ttype setSpeedRequest struct {\n457\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the represents the request body for POST /api/replay/set-state.\n503\ttype setStateRequest struct {\n504\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to modify.\n505\t\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n506\t\t// State is the playback state: \"playing\" or \"paused\".\n507\t\tState string `json:\"state\"`\n508\t}\n509\t\n510\t// setState handles POST /api/replay/set-state.\n511\t// Changes the playback state for a replay session.\n512\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) setState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n513\t\tvar req setStateRequest\n514\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n515\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + {\n536\t\t\ts.State = req.State\n537\t\t}\n538\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n539\t\n540\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session state changed: %s to %s\", req.SessionID, req.State)\n541\t\n542\t\twriteJSON(w, handles GET /api/replay/session/{id}.\n550\t// Returns the current state of a replay session including blobs.\n551\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) getSessionState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n552\t\tsessionID := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n553\t\n554\t\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(sessionID)\n555\t\tif err != nil {\n556\t\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n557\t\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n558\t\t\t\treturn\n559\t\t\t}\n560\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n561\t\t\treturn\n562\t\t}\n563\t\n564\t\t// Update API layer session state\n565\t\th.mu.Lock()\n566\t\tif s, exists := h.sessions[sessionID]; exists {\n567\t\t\ts.CurrentMS = session.CurrentMS\n568\t\t\ts.State = session.State\n569\t\t}\n570\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n571\t\n572\t\t// Calculate progress\n573\t\tduration := session.ToMS - session.FromMS\n574\t\tprogress := 0.0\n575\t\tif duration > 0 {\n576\t\t\tprogress = float64(session.CurrentMS-session.FromMS) / float64(duration)\n577\t\t}\n578\t\n579\t\t// Convert replay blobs to API format\n580\t\tblobs := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(session.LastBlobs))\n581\t\tfor _, b := range session.LastBlobs {\n582\t\t\tblob := map[string]interface{}{\n583\t\t\t\t\"id\":                  b.ID,\n584\t\t\t\t\"x\":                   b.X,\n585\t\t\t\t\"y\":                   b.Y,\n586\t\t\t\t\"z\":                   b.Z,\n587\t\t\t\t\"vx\":                  b.VX,\n588\t\t\t\t\"vy\":                  b.VY,\n589\t\t\t\t\"vz\":                  b.VZ,\n590\t\t\t\t\"weight\":              b.Weight,\n591\t\t\t\t\"posture\":             b.Posture,\n592\t\t\t\t\"person_id\":           b.PersonID,\n593\t\t\t\t\"person_label\":        b.PersonLabel,\n594\t\t\t\t\"person_color\":        b.PersonColor,\n595\t\t\t\t\"identity_confidence\": b.IdentityConfidence,\n596\t\t\t\t\"identity_source\":     b.IdentitySource,\n597\t\t\t\t\"trail\":               b.Trail,\n598\t\t\t}\n599\t\t\tblobs = append(blobs, blob)\n600\t\t}\n601\t\n602\t\t// Build response with session state and blobs\n603\t\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n604\t\t\t\"session_id\":  sessionID,\n605\t\t\t\"current_ms\":  session.CurrentMS,\n606\t\t\t\"from_ms\":     session.FromMS,\n607\t\t\t\"to_ms\":       session.ToMS,\n608\t\t\t\"state\":       session.State,\n609\t\t\t\"speed\":       session.Speed,\n610\t\t\t\"progress\":    progress,\n611\t\t\t\"params\":      session.Params,\n612\t\t\t\"blobs\":       blobs,\n613\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": session.LastBlobTime,\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// jumpToTimeRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\n620\ttype jumpToTimeRequest struct {\n621\t\t// TimestampMS is the target timestamp in epoch milliseconds.\n622\t\tTimestampMS int64 `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n623\t\t// WindowMS is the time window around the timestamp (default 5000ms).\n624\t\tWindowMS int64 `json:\"window_ms,omitempty\"`\n625\t}\n626\t\n627\t// jumpToTime handles POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\n628\t// Creates a replay session centered on the given timestamp, seeks to it,\n629\t// and sets it as the active session. Stops any previous active session first.\n630\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) jumpToTime(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n631\t\tvar req jumpToTimeRequest\n632\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n633\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n634\t\t\treturn\n635\t\t}\n636\t\n637\t\tif req.TimestampMS <= 0 {\n638\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp_ms is required and must be positive\"})\n639\t\t\treturn\n640\t\t}\n641\t\n642\t\twindowMS := req.WindowMS\n643\t\tif windowMS <= 0 {\n644\t\t\twindowMS = 5000 // ±5 seconds default\n645\t\t}\n646\t\n647\t\tfromMS := req.TimestampMS - windowMS\n648\t\ttoMS := req.TimestampMS + windowMS\n649\t\n650\t\t// Stop previous active session if any\n651\t\th.mu.Lock()\n652\t\tprevID := h.activeSessionID\n653\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n654\t\n655\t\tif prevID != \"\" {\n656\t\t\tif err := h.worker.StopSession(prevID); err == nil {\n657\t\t\t\th.mu.Lock()\n658\t\t\t\tdelete(h.sessions, prevID)\n659\t\t\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n660\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Previous replay session stopped: %s\", prevID)\n661\t\t\t}\n662\t\t}\n663\t\n664\t\t// Start new session\n665\t\tsessionID, err := h.worker.StartSession(fromMS, toMS, 1)\n666\t\tif err != nil {\n667\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": \"failed to start session: \" + err.Error()})\n668\t\t\treturn\n669\t\t}\n670\t\n671\t\t// Seek to target timestamp\n672\t\tif err := h.worker.Seek(sessionID, req.TimestampMS); err != nil {\n673\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Jump-to-time seek failed: %v\", err)\n674\t\t}\n675\t\n676\t\t// Track in API layer\n677\t\tsession := &_replaySession{\n678\t\t\tID:        sessionID,\n679\t\t\tFromMS:    fromMS,\n680\t\t\tToMS:      toMS,\n681\t\t\tCurrentMS: req.TimestampMS,\n682\t\t\tSpeed:     1,\n683\t\t\tState:     \"paused\",\n684\t\t\tParams:    make(map[string]interface{}),\n685\t\t\tCreatedAt: formatTimestamp(fromMS),\n686\t\t}\n687\t\th.mu.Lock()\n688\t\th.sessions[sessionID] = session\n689\t\th.activeSessionID = sessionID\n690\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n691\t\n692\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Jump-to-time: session %s at %d (window ±%dms)\", sessionID, req.TimestampMS, windowMS)\n693\t\n694\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n695\t\t\t\"session_id\":   sessionID,\n696\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": req.TimestampMS,\n697\t\t\t\"from_ms\":      fromMS,\n698\t\t\t\"to_ms\":        toMS,\n699\t\t\t\"state\":        \"paused\",\n700\t\t})\n701\t}\n702\t\n703\t// Helper functions\n704\tfunc timeNowMillis() int64 {\n705\t\treturn time.Now().UnixNano() / 1e6\n706\t}\n707\t\n708\tfunc parseISO8601(s string) (int64, error) {\n709\t\tt, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s)\n710\t\tif err != nil {\n711\t\t\treturn 0, err\n712\t\t}\n713\t\treturn t.UnixNano() / 1e6, nil\n714\t}\n715\t\n716\tfunc formatTimestamp(ms int64) string content\"\n196\t//\t@Failure\t\t404\t\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Person not found\"\n197\t//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/people/{id} [delete]\n198\tfunc (h *Handler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n199\t\t// Device endpoints\n200\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices\", h.listDevices)\n201\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.getDevice)\n202\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}/history\", h.getDeviceHistory)\n203\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}/aliases\", h.getDeviceAliases)\n204\t\tr.Put(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.updateDevice)\n205\t\tr.Delete(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.archiveDevice)\n206\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/devices/preregister\", h.preregisterDevice)\n207\t\n208\t\t// Duplicate detection\n209\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/duplicates\", h.listDuplicates)\n210\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/merge\", h.mergeDevices)\n211\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/split\", h.splitDevice)\n212\t\n213\t\t// People endpoints\n214\t\tr.Get(\"/api/people\", h.listPeople)\n215\t\tr.Post(\"/api/people\", h.createPerson)\n216\t\tr.Get(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.getPerson)\n217\t\tr.Put(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.updatePerson)\n218\t\tr.Delete(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.deletePerson)\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t// ── Device endpoints ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n222\t\n223\t// listDevices handles GET /api/ble/devices.\n224\t//\n225\t// Returns a list of all BLE devices seen by the system. Devices can be filtered\n226\t// by registration status (registered/discovered), time window (hours parameter),\n227\t// and archival status.\n228\t//\n229\t// Query parameters:\n230\t//   - registered: \"true\" to return only devices assigned to a person\n231\t//   - discovered: \"true\" to return only unassigned devices\n232\t//   - archived: \"true\" to include archived (soft-deleted) devices\n233\t//   - hours: time window in hours (default: 24)\n234\t//\n235\t// Response: JSON object with \"devices\" array and \"privacy_notice\" string.\n236\t// Each device includes: mac, name, label, manufacturer, device_type, device_name,\n237\t// person_id, person_name, rssi_min, rssi_max, rssi_avg, first_seen_at, last_seen_at,\n238\t// last_seen_node, is_archived, is_wearable, enabled, last_location.\n239\t//\n240\t// Status codes:\n241\t//   - 200: Success\n242\t//   - 500: Internal error\n243\tfunc (h *Handler) listDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n244\t\tincludeArchived := r.URL.Query().Get(\"archived\") == \"true\"\n245\t\tregistered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"registered\")\n246\t\tdiscovered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"discovered\")\n247\t\n248\t\t// Parse hours parameter (default: 24 hours)\n249\t\thoursStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"hours\")\n250\t\thours := 24 // Default to last 24 hours\n251\t\tif hoursStr != \"\" {\n252\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(hoursStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n253\t\t\t\thours = n\n254\t\t\t}\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\tvar devices []DeviceRecord\n258\t\tvar err error\n259\t\n260\t\t// Filter by registration status and time window\n261\t\tif registered == \"true\" {\n262\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n263\t\t\t// Filter to only registered devices (has person_id)\n264\t\t\tvar registeredDevices []DeviceRecord\n265\t\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n266\t\t\t\tif d.PersonID != \"\" {\n267\t\t\t\t\tregisteredDevices = append(registeredDevices, d)\n268\t\t\t\t}\n269\t\t\t}\n270\t\t\tdevices = registeredDevices\n271\t\t} else if discovered == \"true\" {\n272\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n273\t\t\t// Filter to only unregistered devices (no person_id)\n274\t\t\tvar unregisteredDevices []DeviceRecord\n275\t\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n276\t\t\t\tif d.PersonID == \"\" {\n277\t\t\t\t\tunregisteredDevices = append(unregisteredDevices, d)\n278\t\t\t\t}\n279\t\t\t}\n280\t\t\tdevices = unregisteredDevices\n281\t\t} else {\n282\t\t\t// Get all devices seen in the time window\n283\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n284\t\t}\n285\t\n286\t\tif err != nil {\n287\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n288\t\t\treturn\n289\t\t}\n290\t\tif devices == nil {\n291\t\t\tdevices = []DeviceRecord{}\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\t// Add privacy notice in response header\n295\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Privacy-Notice\", \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\")\n296\t\n297\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n298\t\t\t\"devices\":       devices,\n299\t\t\t\"privacy_notice\": \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\",\n300\t\t})\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\t// getDevice handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n304\t//\n305\t// Returns detailed information about a single BLE device by its MAC address.\n306\t// The MAC address should be in uppercase colon-separated hex format (e.g., \"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\").\n307\t//\n308\t// URL parameters:\n309\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n310\t//\n311\t// Response: JSON device object with all fields including location history.\n312\t//\n313\t// Status codes:\n314\t//   - 200: Success, device found\n315\t//   - 404: Device not found\n316\t//   - 500: Internal error\n317\tfunc (h *Handler) getDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n318\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n319\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n320\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n321\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n322\t\t\treturn\n323\t\t}\n324\t\tif err != nil {\n325\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n326\t\t\treturn\n327\t\t}\n328\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// getDeviceHistory handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}/history.\n332\t//\n333\t// Returns the sighting history for a specific BLE device. This includes\n334\t// RSSI observations from nodes that have detected this device over time.\n335\t//\n336\t// URL parameters:\n337\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n338\t//\n339\t// Query parameters:\n340\t//   - limit: maximum number of history entries to return (default: 100, max: 1000)\n341\t//\n342\t// Response: JSON object with \"mac\", \"history\" (array of sighting entries),\n343\t// and \"limit\" fields. Each history entry includes timestamp, rssi_dbm, and node_mac.\n344\t//\n345\t// Status codes:\n346\t//   - 200: Success\n347\t//   - 404: Device not found\n348\t//   - 500: Internal error\n349\tfunc (h *Handler) getDeviceHistory(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n350\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n351\t\n352\t\t// Parse limit parameter\n353\t\tlimitStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\")\n354\t\tlimit := 100\n355\t\tif limitStr != \"\" {\n356\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(limitStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n357\t\t\t\tlimit = n\n358\t\t\t}\n359\t\t}\n360\t\n361\t\t// Check that the device exists first\n362\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); err != nil {\n363\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n364\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n365\t\t\t\treturn\n366\t\t\t}\n367\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n368\t\t\treturn\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\thistory, err := h.registry.GetDeviceSightingHistory(mac, limit)\n372\t\tif err != nil {\n373\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n374\t\t\treturn\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n378\t\t\t\"mac\":     mac,\n379\t\t\t\"history\": history,\n380\t\t\t\"limit\":   limit,\n381\t\t})\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\ttype updateDeviceRequest struct {\n385\t\tLabel      string `json:\"label\"`       // User-assigned display label\n386\t\tDeviceType string `json:\"device_type\"` // Device type (apple_phone, apple_watch, tile, etc.)\n387\t\tPersonID   string `json:\"person_id\"`   // Person ID to assign device to\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// updateDevice handles PUT /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n391\t//\n392\t// Updates a BLE device's properties. This endpoint is used to set a human-readable\n393\t// label for a device and/or assign it to a person for identity tracking.\n394\t//\n395\t// URL parameters:\n396\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address (uppercase colon-separated hex)\n397\t//\n398\t// Request body: JSON object with optional fields:\n399\t//   - label: User-assigned display label (e.g., \"Alice's iPhone\")\n400\t//   - device_type: Device type identifier (e.g., \"apple_phone\", \"tile\", \"fitbit\")\n401\t//   - person_id: UUID of person to assign this device to (must exist)\n402\t//\n403\t// Response: Updated device object as JSON.\n404\t//\n405\t// Status codes:\n406\t//   - 200: Success, device updated\n407\t//   - 400: Invalid request body or person_id not found\n408\t//   - 404: Device not found\n409\t//   - 500: Internal error\n410\tfunc (h *Handler) updateDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n411\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n412\t\n413\t\t// Verify device exists\n414\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n415\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n416\t\t\treturn\n417\t\t} else if err != nil {\n418\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n419\t\t\treturn\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\tvar req updateDeviceRequest\n423\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n424\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n425\t\t\treturn\n426\t\t}\n427\t\n428\t\tupdates := make(map[string]interface{})\n429\t\n430\t\tif req.Label != \"\" {\n431\t\t\tupdates[\"label\"] = req.Label\n432\t\t}\n433\t\tif req.DeviceType != \"\" {\n434\t\t\tupdates[\"device_type\"] = req.DeviceType\n435\t\t}\n436\t\tif req.PersonID != \"\" {\n437\t\t\t// Verify person exists\n438\t\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(req.PersonID); err != nil {\n439\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n440\t\t\t\treturn\n441\t\t\t}\n442\t\t\tupdates[\"person_id\"] = req.PersonID\n443\t\t}\n444\t\n445\t\tif len(updates) > 0 {\n446\t\t\tif err := h.registry.UpdateDevice(mac, updates); err != nil {\n447\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n448\t\t\t\treturn\n449\t\t\t}\n450\t\t}\n451\t\n452\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n453\t\tif err != nil {\n454\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n455\t\t\treturn\n456\t\t}\n457\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\tfunc (h *Handler) archiveDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n461\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n462\t\n463\t\t// Verify device exists\n464\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n465\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n466\t\t\treturn\n467\t\t} else if err != nil {\n468\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n469\t\t\treturn\n470\t\t}\n471\t\n472\t\tif err := h.registry.ArchiveDevice(mac); err != nil {\n473\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n474\t\t\treturn\n475\t\t}\n476\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n477\t}\n478\t\n479\t// ── Duplicate detection endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n480\t\n481\tfunc (h *Handler) listDuplicates(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n482\t\tduplicates, err := h.registry.DetectPossibleDuplicates()\n483\t\tif err != nil {\n484\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n485\t\t\treturn\n486\t\t}\n487\t\tif duplicates == nil {\n488\t\t\tduplicates = []PossibleDuplicate{}\n489\t\t}\n490\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n491\t\t\t\"duplicates\": duplicates,\n492\t\t\t\"message\":    \"These devices may be the same device with a rotated MAC address. Review and merge if appropriate.\",\n493\t\t})\n494\t}\n495\t\n496\ttype mergeDevicesRequest struct {\n497\t\tMAC1 string `json:\"mac1\"`\n498\t\tMAC2 string `json:\"mac2\"`\n499\t}\n500\t\n501\tfunc (h *Handler) mergeDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n502\t\tvar req mergeDevicesRequest\n503\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.MAC1 == \"\" || req.MAC2 == \"\" {\n504\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n505\t\t\treturn\n506\t\t}\n507\t\n508\t\tif req.MAC1 == req.MAC2 {\n509\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"cannot merge device with itself\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n510\t\t\treturn\n511\t\t}\n512\t\n513\t\t// Verify both devices exist\n514\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC1); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n515\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device 1 not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n516\t\t\treturn\n517\t\t}\n518\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC2); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n519\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device 2 not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n520\t\t\treturn\n521\t\t}\n522\t\n523\t\tif err := h.registry.MergeDevices(req.MAC1, req.MAC2); err != nil {\n524\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n525\t\t\treturn\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC1)\n529\t\tif err != nil {\n530\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n531\t\t\treturn\n532\t\t}\n533\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n534\t\t\t\"merged\": device,\n535\t\t\t\"message\": \"Devices merged successfully. \" + req.MAC2 + \" has been removed.\",\n536\t\t})\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\t// ── People endpoints ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n540\t\n541\tfunc (h *Handler) listPeople(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n542\t\tpeople, err := h.registry.GetPeopleWithDevices()\n543\t\tif err != nil {\n544\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n545\t\t\treturn\n546\t\t}\n547\t\tif people == nil {\n548\t\t\tpeople = []map[string]interface{}{}\n549\t\t}\n550\t\twriteJSON(w, people)\n551\t}\n552\t\n553\ttype createPersonRequest struct {\n554\t\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n555\t\tColor string `json:\"color\"`\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\tfunc (h *Handler) createPerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n559\t\tvar req createPersonRequest\n560\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.Name == \"\" {\n561\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n562\t\t\treturn\n563\t\t}\n564\t\n565\t\t// Default color if not provided\n566\t\tif req.Color == \"\" {\n567\t\t\treq.Color = \"#3b82f6\"\n568\t\t}\n569\t\n570\t\tperson, err := h.registry.CreatePerson(req.Name, req.Color)\n571\t\tif err != nil {\n572\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n573\t\t\treturn\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)\n577\t\twriteJSON(w, person)\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\tfunc (h *Handler) getPerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n581\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n582\t\n583\t\tperson, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id)\n584\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n585\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n586\t\t\treturn\n587\t\t}\n588\t\tif err != nil {\n589\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n590\t\t\treturn\n591\t\t}\n592\t\n593\t\t// Get devices for this person\n594\t\tdevices, err := h.registry.GetPersonDevices(id)\n595\t\tif err != nil {\n596\t\t\tdevices = nil\n597\t\t}\n598\t\n599\t\t// Find most recent last_seen among devices\n600\t\tvar lastSeen time.Time\n601\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n602\t\t\tif d.LastSeenAt.After(lastSeen) {\n603\t\t\t\tlastSeen = d.LastSeenAt\n604\t\t\t}\n605\t\t}\n606\t\n607\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n608\t\t\t\"id\":           person.ID,\n609\t\t\t\"name\":         person.Name,\n610\t\t\t\"color\":        person.Color,\n611\t\t\t\"created_at\":   person.CreatedAt,\n612\t\t\t\"device_count\": len(devices),\n613\t\t\t\"devices\":      devices,\n614\t\t\t\"last_seen\":    lastSeen,\n615\t\t})\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\ttype updatePersonRequest struct {\n619\t\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n620\t\tColor string `json:\"color\"`\n621\t}\n622\t\n623\tfunc (h *Handler) updatePerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n624\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n625\t\n626\t\t// Verify person exists\n627\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n628\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n629\t\t\treturn\n630\t\t} else if err != nil {\n631\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n632\t\t\treturn\n633\t\t}\n634\t\n635\t\tvar req updatePersonRequest\n636\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n637\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n638\t\t\treturn\n639\t\t}\n640\t\n641\t\tif req.Name == \"\" && req.Color == \"\" {\n642\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"no updates provided\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n643\t\t\treturn\n644\t\t}\n645\t\n646\t\tif err := h.registry.UpdatePerson(id, req.Name, req.Color); err != nil {\n647\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n648\t\t\treturn\n649\t\t}\n650\t\n651\t\tperson, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id)\n652\t\tif err != nil {\n653\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n654\t\t\treturn\n655\t\t}\n656\t\twriteJSON(w, person)\n657\t}\n658\t\n659\tfunc (h *Handler) deletePerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n660\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n661\t\n662\t\t// Verify person exists\n663\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n664\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n679\t\n680\ttype preregisterDeviceRequest struct {\n681\t\tMAC   string `json:\"mac\"`\n682\t\tLabel string `json:\"label\"`\n683\t}\n684\t\n685\t// preregisterDevice manually creates a device entry for a known MAC address.\n686\t// This is useful for pre-registering tracker tags that haven't been seen yet.\n687\tfunc (h *Handler) preregisterDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n688\t\tvar req preregisterDeviceRequest\n689\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.MAC == \"\" {\n690\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body: mac is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n691\t\t\treturn\n692\t\t}\n693\t\n694\t\t// Default label to MAC if not provided\n695\t\tif req.Label == \"\" {\n696\t\t\treq.Label = req.MAC\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.PreregisterDevice(req.MAC, req.Label)\n700\t\tif err != nil {\n701\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n702\t\t\treturn\n703\t\t}\n704\t\n705\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)\n706\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t// ── Alias endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n710\t\n711\t// getDeviceAliases returns the alias history for a device.\n712\t// This includes all rotated addresses that have been merged to this canonical device.\n713\tfunc (h *Handler) getDeviceAliases(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n714\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n715\t\n716\t\t// First check if this is an alias - resolve to canonical if so\n717\t\tcanonicalAddr := h.registry.ResolveAlias(mac)\n718\t\n719\t\t// Get aliases for the canonical address\n720\t\taliases, err := h.registry.GetAliases(canonicalAddr)\n721\t\tif err != nil {\n722\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n723\t\t\treturn\n724\t\t}\n725\t\n726\t\t// Build response with device info\n727\t\tdevice, _ := h.registry.GetDevice(canonicalAddr)\n728\t\n729\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n730\t\t\t\"canonical_addr\": canonicalAddr,\n731\t\t\t\"device\":         device,\n732\t\t\t\"aliases\":        aliases,\n733\t\t\t\"alias_count\":    len(aliases),\n734\t\t\t\"note\":           \"Devices with auto-rotating addresses (phones) may have multiple historical addresses. Trackers typically have stable addresses.\",\n735\t\t})\n736\t}\n737\t\n738\ttype splitDeviceRequest struct {\n739\t\tCanonicalAddr string `json:\"canonical_addr\"` // The canonical device address\n740\t\tAliasAddr     string `json:\"alias_addr\"`      // The alias to split off\n741\t\tNewPersonID   string `json:\"new_person_id\"`   // Optional: assign to different person\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t// splitDevice splits an alias from its canonical device, creating a separate device entry.\n745\t// Use this when a rotation merge was incorrect.\n746\tfunc (h *Handler) splitDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n747\t\tvar req splitDeviceRequest\n748\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.CanonicalAddr == \"\" || req.AliasAddr == \"\" {\n749\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n750\t\t\treturn\n751\t\t}\n752\t\n753\t\tif req.CanonicalAddr == req.AliasAddr {\n754\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"cannot split device from itself\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n755\t\t\treturn\n756\t\t}\n757\t\n758\t\t// Verify canonical device exists\n759\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.CanonicalAddr); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n760\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"canonical device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n761\t\t\treturn\n762\t\t}\n763\t\n764\t\t// Remove the alias relationship\n765\t\tif err := h.registry.RemoveAlias(req.AliasAddr); err != nil {\n766\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error removing alias\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n767\t\t\treturn\n768\t\t}\n769\t\n770\t\t// If the alias has observations in ble_devices, update it\n771\t\t// Create a proper device entry from the alias\n772\t\tnow := time.Now().UnixNano()\n773\t\t_, err := h.registry.db.Exec(`\n774\t\t\tUPDATE ble_devices SET\n775\t\t\t\tperson_id = ?,\n776\t\t\t\tlast_seen_at = ?\n777\t\t\tWHERE mac = ?\n778\t\t`, req.NewPersonID, now, req.AliasAddr)\n779\t\tif err != nil {\n780\t\t\t// Alias might not exist in ble_devices yet, which is fine\n781\t\t\t// Create a new device entry\n782\t\t\t_, err2 := h.registry.db.Exec(`\n783\t\t\t\tINSERT INTO ble_devices (mac, person_id, last_seen_at, first_seen_at, enabled)\n784\t\t\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1)\n785\t\t\t`, req.AliasAddr, req.NewPersonID, now, now)\n786\t\t\tif err2 != nil {\n787\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error creating device\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n788\t\t\t\treturn\n789\t\t\t}\n790\t\t}\n791\t\n792\t\t// Get the updated canonical device\n793\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.CanonicalAddr)\n794\t\tif err != nil {\n795\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n796\t\t\treturn\n797\t\t}\n798\t\n799\t\t// Get the split device\n800\t\tsplitDevice, _ := h.registry.GetDevice(req.AliasAddr)\n801\t\n802\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n803\t\t\t\"canonical_device\": device,\n804\t\t\t\"split_device\":     splitDevice,\n805\t\t\t\"message\":          \"Successfully split \" + req.AliasAddr + \" from \" + req.CanonicalAddr,\n806\t\t})\n807\t}\n808\t\n809\t// ── Utility endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n810\t\n811\t// ArchiveStaleHandler triggers archival of devices not seen for > 7 days.\n812\tfunc (h *Handler) ArchiveStaleHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n813\t\tdaysStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"days\")\n814\t\tdays := 7\n815\t\tif daysStr != \"\" {\n816\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(daysStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n817\t\t\t\tdays = n\n818\t\t\t}\n819\t\t}\n820\t\n821\t\tcount, err := h.registry.ArchiveStale(time.Duration(days) * 24 * time.Hour)\n822\t\tif err != nil {\n823\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n824\t\t\treturn\n825\t\t}\n826\t\n827\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n828\t\t\t\"archived_count\": count,\n829\t\t\t\"message\":        \"Archived \" + strconv.FormatInt(count, 10) + \" devices not seen in \" + strconv.Itoa(days) + \" days.\",\n830\t\t})\n831\t}\n832\t\n833\tfunc writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, v interface{}) {\n834\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n835\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v) fields:\n52\t//   - id: integer trigger ID (auto-assigned)\n53\t//   - name: user-defined trigger name\n54\t//   - shape: 3D volume geometry (box or cylinder)\n55\t//   - condition: trigger condition nil {\n325\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"trigger not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n326\t\t\treturn\n327\t\t}\n328\t\n329\t\tresp := h.toResponse(trigger, time.Now())\n330\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, resp)\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// volumeCreateTriggerRequest is the request body for POST /api/triggers.\n334\ttype volumeCreateTriggerRequest struct {\n335\t\tName            string                   `json:\"name\"`\n336\t\tShape           volume.ShapeJSON         `json:\"shape\"`\n337\t\tCondition       string                   `json:\"condition\"`\n338\t\tConditionParams volume.ConditionParams `json:\"condition_params,omitempty\"`\n339\t\tTimeConstraint  *volume.TimeConstraint   `json:\"time_constraint,omitempty\"`\n340\t\tActions         []volume.Action          `json:\"actions\"`\n341\t\tEnabled         *bool                    `json:\"enabled,omitempty\"`\n342\t}\n343\t\n344\t// createTrigger handles POST /api/triggers.\n345\t//\n346\t// Creates a new automation trigger with 3D volume geometry. The request body\n347\t// must include name, shape, and condition. Actions default to an empty array\n348\t// if omitted. Enabled defaults to true.\n349\t//\n350\t// Request body (application/json):\n351\t//\n352\t//\t{\n353\t//\t  \"name\": \"Couch Dwell\",\n354\t//\t  \"shape\": {\"type\": \"box\", \"x\": 1, \"y\": 2, \"z\": 0, \"w\": 1, \"d\": 1, \"h\": 1.5},\n355\t//\t  \"condition\": \"dwell\",\n356\t//\t  \"condition_params\": {\"duration_s\": 30},\n357\t//\t  \"time_constraint\": {\"from\": \"22:00\", \"to\": \"06:00\"},\n358\t//\t  \"actions\": [{\"type\": \"webhook\", \"url\": \"http://example.com/hook\"}],\n359\t//\t  \"enabled\": true\n360\t//\t}\n361\t//\n362\t// Response 201 (application/json): the created trigger object.\n363\t// Response 400: missing required fields, invalid shape geometry, or invalid condition value.\n364\t// Response 500: database error.\n365\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) createTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n366\t\tvar req volumeCreateTriggerRequest\n367\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n368\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n369\t\t\treturn\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\tif req.Name == \"\" {\n373\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"name is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n374\t\t\treturn\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\t// Validate shape\n378\t\tif !h.isValidShape(&req.Shape) {\n379\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid shape geometry\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n380\t\t\treturn\n381\t\t}\n382\t\n383\t\t// Validate condition\n384\t\tvalidConditions := map[string]bool{\n385\t\t\t\"enter\":  true,\n386\t\t\t\"leave\":  true,\n387\t\t\t\"dwell\":  true,\n388\t\t\t\"vacant\": true,\n389\t\t\t\"count\":  true,\n390\t\t}\n391\t\tif !validConditions[req.Condition] {\n392\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"condition must be one of: enter, leave, dwell, vacant, count\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n393\t\t\treturn\n394\t\t}\n395\t\n396\t\t// Set default enabled if not specified\n397\t\tenabled := true\n398\t\tif req.Enabled != nil {\n399\t\t\tenabled = *req.Enabled\n400\t\t}\n401\t\n402\t\ttrigger := &volume.Trigger{\n403\t\t\tName:            req.Name,\n404\t\t\tShape:           req.Shape,\n405\t\t\tCondition:       req.Condition,\n406\t\t\tConditionParams: req.ConditionParams,\n407\t\t\tTimeConstraint:  req.TimeConstraint,\n408\t\t\tActions:         req.Actions,\n409\t\t\tEnabled:         enabled,\n410\t\t}\n411\t\n412\t\tid, err := h.store.Create(trigger)\n413\t\tif err != nil {\n414\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to create trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n415\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to create trigger: %v\", err)\n416\t\t\treturn\n417\t\t}\n418\t\n419\t\t// Fetch the created trigger to get the full object\n420\t\tcreated, err := h.store.Get(id)\n421\t\tif err != nil {\n422\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to retrieve created trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n423\t\t\treturn\n424\t\t}\n425\t\n426\t\tresp := h.toResponse(created, time.Now())\n427\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, resp)\n428\t}\n429\t\n430\t// volumeUpdateTriggerRequest is the request body for PUT /api/triggers/{id}.\n431\t// Only non-nil fields are updated.\n432\ttype volumeUpdateTriggerRequest struct {\n433\t\tName            *string                  `json:\"name,omitempty\"`\n434\t\tShape           *volume.ShapeJSON        `json:\"shape,omitempty\"`\n435\t\tCondition       *string                  `json:\"condition,omitempty\"`\n436\t\tConditionParams *volume.ConditionParams `json:\"condition_params,omitempty\"`\n437\t\tTimeConstraint  *volume.TimeConstraint   `json:\"time_constraint,omitempty\"`\n438\t\tActions         *[]volume.Action         `json:\"actions,omitempty\"`\n439\t\tEnabled         *bool                    `json:\"enabled,omitempty\"`\n440\t}\n441\t\n442\t// updateTrigger handles PUT /api/triggers/{id}.\n443\t//\n444\t// Updates an existing trigger. Only fields present in the request body are\n445\t// modified; omitted fields retain their current values. Shape geometry is\n446\t// validated on update.\n447\t//\n448\t// Request body (application/json): partial trigger object with fields to update.\n449\t//\n450\t// Response 200 (application/json): the updated trigger object.\n451\t// Response 400: invalid request body or invalid shape geometry.\n452\t// Response 404: trigger not found.\n453\t// Response 500: database error.\n454\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) updateTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n455\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n456\t\n457\t\ttrigger, err := h.store.Get(id)\n458\t\tif err != nil {\n459\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"trigger not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n460\t\t\treturn\n461\t\t}\n462\t\n463\t\tvar req volumeUpdateTriggerRequest\n464\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n465\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n466\t\t\treturn\n467\t\t}\n468\t\n469\t\t// Apply updates\n470\t\tif req.Name != nil {\n471\t\t\ttrigger.Name = *req.Name\n472\t\t}\n473\t\tif req.Shape != nil {\n474\t\t\tif !h.isValidShape(req.Shape) {\n475\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid shape geometry\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n476\t\t\t\treturn\n477\t\t\t}\n478\t\t\ttrigger.Shape = *req.Shape\n479\t\t}\n480\t\tif req.Condition != nil {\n481\t\t\ttrigger.Condition = *req.Condition\n482\t\t}\n483\t\tif req.ConditionParams != nil {\n484\t\t\ttrigger.ConditionParams = *req.ConditionParams\n485\t\t}\n486\t\tif req.TimeConstraint != nil {\n487\t\t\ttrigger.TimeConstraint = req.TimeConstraint\n488\t\t}\n489\t\tif req.Actions != nil {\n490\t\t\ttrigger.Actions = *req.Actions\n491\t\t}\n492\t\tif req.Enabled != nil {\n493\t\t\ttrigger.Enabled = *req.Enabled\n494\t\t}\n495\t\n496\t\tif err := h.store.Update(trigger); err != nil {\n497\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to update trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n498\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to update trigger: %v\", err)\n499\t\t\treturn\n500\t\t}\n501\t\n502\t\tresp := h.toResponse(trigger, time.Now())\n503\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, resp)\n504\t}\n505\t\n506\t// deleteTrigger handles DELETE /api/triggers/{id}.\n507\t//\n508\t// Removes a trigger by ID and all associated state (trigger state, webhook log entries).\n509\t//\n510\t// Response 204: trigger deleted.\n511\t// Response 500: database error.\n512\tfunc (h *VolumeTriggersHandler) deleteTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n513\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n514\t\n515\t\tif err := h.store.Delete(id);
