listDevices handles GET /api/ble/devices.\n224\t//\n225\t// Returns a list of all BLE devices seen by the system. Devices can be filtered\n226\t// by registration status (registered/discovered), time window (hours parameter),\n227\t// and archival status.\n228\t//\n229\t// Query parameters:\n230\t//   - registered: \"true\" to return only devices assigned to a person\n231\t//   - discovered: \"true\" to return only unassigned devices\n232\t//   - archived: \"true\" to include archived (soft-deleted) devices\n233\t//   - hours: time window in hours (default: 24)\n234\t//\n235\t// Response: JSON object with \"devices\" array and \"privacy_notice\" string.\n236\t// Each device includes: mac, name, label, manufacturer, device_type, device_name,\n237\t// person_id, person_name, rssi_min, rssi_max, rssi_avg, first_seen_at, last_seen_at,\n238\t// last_seen_node, is_archived, is_wearable, enabled, last_location.\n239\t//\n240\t// Status codes:\n241\t//   - 200: Success\n242\t//   - 500: Internal error\n243\tfunc (h *Handler) listDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n244\t\tincludeArchived := r.URL.Query().Get(\"archived\") == \"true\"\n245\t\tregistered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"registered\")\n246\t\tdiscovered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"discovered\")\n247\t\n248\t\t// Parse hours parameter (default: 24 hours)\n249\t\thoursStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"hours\")\n250\t\thours := 24 // Default to last 24 hours\n251\t\tif hoursStr != \"\" {\n252\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(hoursStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n253\t\t\t\thours = n\n254\t\t\t}\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\tvar devices []DeviceRecord\n258\t\tvar err error\n259\t\n260\t\t// Filter by registration status and time window\n261\t\tif registered == \"true\" {\n262\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n263\t\t\t// Filter to only registered devices (has person_id)\n264\t\t\tvar registeredDevices []DeviceRecord\n265\t\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n266\t\t\t\tif d.PersonID != \"\" {\n267\t\t\t\t\tregisteredDevices = append(registeredDevices, d)\n268\t\t\t\t}\n269\t\t\t}\n270\t\t\tdevices = registeredDevices\n271\t\t} else if discovered == \"true\" {\n272\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n273\t\t\t// Filter to only unregistered devices (no person_id)\n274\t\t\tvar unregisteredDevices []DeviceRecord\n275\t\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n276\t\t\t\tif d.PersonID == \"\" {\n277\t\t\t\t\tunregisteredDevices = append(unregisteredDevices, d)\n278\t\t\t\t}\n279\t\t\t}\n280\t\t\tdevices = unregisteredDevices\n281\t\t} else {\n282\t\t\t// Get all devices seen in the time window\n283\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n284\t\t}\n285\t\n286\t\tif err != nil {\n287\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n288\t\t\treturn\n289\t\t}\n290\t\tif devices == nil {\n291\t\t\tdevices = []DeviceRecord{}\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\t// Add privacy notice in response header\n295\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Privacy-Notice\", \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\")\n296\t\n297\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n298\t\t\t\"devices\":       devices,\n299\t\t\t\"privacy_notice\": \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\",\n300\t\t})\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\t// getDevice handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n304\t//\n305\t// Returns detailed information about a single BLE device by its MAC address.\n306\t// The MAC address should be in uppercase colon-separated hex format (e.g., \"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\").\n307\t//\n308\t// URL parameters:\n309\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n310\t//\n311\t// Response: JSON device object with all fields including location history.\n312\t//\n313\t// Status codes:\n314\t//   - 200: Success, device found\n315\t//   - 404: Device not found\n316\t//   - 500: Internal error\n317\tfunc (h *Handler) getDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n318\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n319\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n320\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n321\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n322\t\t\treturn\n323\t\t}\n324\t\tif err != nil {\n325\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n326\t\t\treturn\n327\t\t}\n328\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// getDeviceHistory handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}/history.\n332\t//\n333\t// Returns the sighting history for a specific BLE device. This includes\n334\t// RSSI observations from nodes that have detected this device over time.\n335\t//\n336\t// URL parameters:\n337\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n338\t//\n339\t// Query parameters:\n340\t//   - limit: maximum number of history entries to return (default: 100, max: 1000)\n341\t//\n342\t// Response: JSON object with \"mac\", \"history\" (array of sighting entries),\n343\t// and \"limit\" fields. Each history entry includes timestamp, rssi_dbm, and node_mac.\n344\t//\n345\t// Status codes:\n346\t//   - 200: Success\n347\t//   - 404: Device not found\n348\t//   - 500: Internal error\n349\tfunc (h *Handler) getDeviceHistory(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n350\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n351\t\n352\t\t// Parse limit parameter\n353\t\tlimitStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\")\n354\t\tlimit := 100\n355\t\tif limitStr != \"\" {\n356\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(limitStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n357\t\t\t\tlimit = n\n358\t\t\t}\n359\t\t}\n360\t\n361\t\t// Check that the device exists first\n362\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); err != nil {\n363\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n364\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n365\t\t\t\treturn\n366\t\t\t}\n367\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n368\t\t\treturn\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\thistory, err := h.registry.GetDeviceSightingHistory(mac, limit)\n372\t\tif err != nil {\n373\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n374\t\t\treturn\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n378\t\t\t\"mac\":     mac,\n379\t\t\t\"history\": history,\n380\t\t\t\"limit\":   limit,\n381\t\t})\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\ttype updateDeviceRequest struct {\n385\t\tLabel      string `json:\"label\"`       // User-assigned display label\n386\t\tDeviceType string `json:\"device_type\"` // Device type (apple_phone, apple_watch, tile, etc.)\n387\t\tPersonID   string `json:\"person_id\"`   // Person ID to assign device to\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// updateDevice handles PUT /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n391\t//\n392\t// Updates a BLE device's properties. This endpoint is used to set a human-readable\n393\t// label for a device and/or assign it to a person for identity tracking.\n394\t//\n395\t// URL parameters:\n396\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address (uppercase colon-separated hex)\n397\t//\n398\t// Request body: JSON object with optional fields:\n399\t//   - label: User-assigned display label (e.g., \"Alice's iPhone\")\n400\t//   - device_type: Device type identifier (e.g., \"apple_phone\", \"tile\", \"fitbit\")\n401\t//   - person_id: UUID of person to assign this device to (must exist)\n402\t//\n403\t// Response: Updated device object as JSON.\n404\t//\n405\t// Status codes:\n406\t//   - 200: Success, device updated\n407\t//   - 400: Invalid request body or person_id not found\n408\t//   - 404: Device not found\n409\t//   - 500: Internal error\n410\tfunc (h *Handler) updateDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n411\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n412\t\n413\t\t// Verify device exists\n414\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n415\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n416\t\t\treturn\n417\t\t} else if err != nil {\n418\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n419\t\t\treturn\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\tvar req updateDeviceRequest\n423\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n424\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n425\t\t\treturn\n426\t\t}\n427\t\n428\t\tupdates := make(map[string]interface{})\n429\t\n430\t\tif req.Label != \"\" {\n431\t\t\tupdates[\"label\"] = req.Label\n432\t\t}\n433\t\tif req.DeviceType != \"\" {\n434\t\t\tupdates[\"device_type\"] = req.DeviceType\n435\t\t}\n436\t\tif req.PersonID != \"\" {\n437\t\t\t// Verify person exists\n438\t\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(req.PersonID); err != nil {\n439\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n440\t\t\t\treturn\n441\t\t\t}\n442\t\t\tupdates[\"person_id\"] = req.PersonID\n443\t\t}\n444\t\n445\t\tif len(updates) > 0 {\n446\t\t\tif err := h.registry.UpdateDevice(mac, updates); err != nil {\n447\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n448\t\t\t\treturn\n449\t\t\t}\n450\t\t}\n451\t\n452\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n453\t\tif err != nil {\n454\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n455\t\t\treturn\n456\t\t}\n457\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\tfunc (h *Handler) archiveDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n461\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n462\t\n463\t\t// Verify device exists\n464\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n465\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n466\t\t\treturn\n467\t\t} else if err != nil {\n468\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n469\t\t\treturn\n470\t\t}\n471\t\n472\t\tif err := h.registry.ArchiveDevice(mac); err != nil {\n473\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n474\t\t\treturn\n475\t\t}\n476\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n477\t}\n478\t\n479\t// ── Duplicate detection endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n480\t\n481\tfunc (h *Handler) listDuplicates(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n482\t\tduplicates, err := h.registry.DetectPossibleDuplicates()\n483\t\tif err != nil {\n484\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n485\t\t\treturn\n486\t\t}\n487\t\tif duplicates == nil {\n488\t\t\tduplicates = []PossibleDuplicate{}\n489\t\t}\n490\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n491\t\t\t\"duplicates\": duplicates,\n492\t\t\t\"message\":    \"These devices may be the same device with a rotated MAC address. Review and merge if appropriate.\",\n493\t\t})\n494\t}\n495\t\n496\ttype mergeDevicesRequest struct {\n497\t\tMAC1 string `json:\"mac1\"`\n498\t\tMAC2 string `json:\"mac2\"`\n499\t}\n500\t\n501\tfunc (h *Handler) mergeDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n502\t\tvar req mergeDevicesRequest\n503\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.MAC1 == \"\" || req.MAC2 == \"\" {\n504\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n505\t\t\treturn\n506\t\t}\n507\t\n508\t\tif req.MAC1 == req.MAC2 {\n509\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"cannot merge device with itself\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n510\t\t\treturn\n511\t\t}\n512\t\n513\t\t// Verify both devices exist\n514\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC1); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n515\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device 1 not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n516\t\t\treturn\n517\t\t}\n518\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC2); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n519\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device 2 not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n520\t\t\treturn\n521\t\t}\n522\t\n523\t\tif err := h.registry.MergeDevices(req.MAC1, req.MAC2); err != nil {\n524\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n525\t\t\treturn\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC1)\n529\t\tif err != nil {\n530\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n531\t\t\treturn\n532\t\t}\n533\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n534\t\t\t\"merged\": device,\n535\t\t\t\"message\": \"Devices merged successfully. \" + req.MAC2 + \" has been removed.\",\n536\t\t})\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\t// ── People endpoints ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n540\t\n541\tfunc (h *Handler) listPeople(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n542\t\tpeople, err := `json:\"color\"`\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\tfunc (h *Handler) createPerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n559\t\tvar req createPersonRequest\n560\t\tif err := {\n572\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n573\t\t\treturn\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)\n577\t\twriteJSON(w, person)\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\tfunc (h *Handler) getPerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n581\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n582\t\n583\t\tperson, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id)\n584\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n585\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n586\t\t\treturn\n587\t\t}\n588\t\tif err != nil {\n589\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n590\t\t\treturn\n591\t\t}\n592\t\n593\t\t// Get devices for this person\n594\t\tdevices, err := h.registry.GetPersonDevices(id)\n595\t\tif err != nil {\n596\t\t\tdevices = nil\n597\t\t}\n598\t\n599\t\t// Find most recent last_seen among devices\n600\t\tvar lastSeen time.Time\n601\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n602\t\t\tif d.LastSeenAt.After(lastSeen) {\n603\t\t\t\tlastSeen = d.LastSeenAt\n604\t\t\t}\n605\t\t}\n606\t\n607\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n608\t\t\t\"id\":           person.ID,\n609\t\t\t\"name\":         person.Name,\n610\t\t\t\"color\":        person.Color,\n611\t\t\t\"created_at\":   person.CreatedAt,\n612\t\t\t\"device_count\": len(devices),\n613\t\t\t\"devices\":      devices,\n614\t\t\t\"last_seen\":    lastSeen,\n615\t\t})\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\ttype updatePersonRequest struct {\n619\t\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n620\t\tColor string `json:\"color\"`\n621\t}\n622\t\n623\tfunc (h *Handler) updatePerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n624\t\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n625\t\n626\t\t// Verify person exists\n627\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n628\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n654\t\t\treturn\n655\t\t}\n656\t\twriteJSON(w, person)\n657\t}\n658\t\n659\tfunc (h *Handler) deletePerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n660\t\tid := ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n679\t\n680\ttype preregisterDeviceRequest struct {\n681\t\tMAC   string `json:\"mac\"`\n682\t\tLabel string `json:\"label\"`\n683\t}\n684\t\n685\t// preregisterDevice manually creates a device entry for a known MAC address.\n686\t// This is useful for pre-registering tracker tags that haven't been seen yet.\n687\tfunc (h *Handler) preregisterDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n688\t\tvar req preregisterDeviceRequest\n689\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.MAC == \"\" {\n690\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body: mac is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n691\t\t\treturn\n692\t\t}\n693\t\n694\t\t// Default label to MAC if not provided\n695\t\tif req.Label == \"\" {\n696\t\t\treq.Label = req.MAC\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.PreregisterDevice(req.MAC, req.Label)\n700\t\tif err != nil {\n701\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n702\t\t\treturn\n703\t\t}\n704\t\n705\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)\n706\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t// ── Alias endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n710\t\n711\t// getDeviceAliases returns the alias history for a device.\n712\t// This includes all rotated addresses that have been merged to this canonical device.\n713\tfunc (h *Handler) getDeviceAliases(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n714\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n715\t\n716\t\t// First check if this is an alias - resolve to canonical if so\n717\t\tcanonicalAddr := h.registry.ResolveAlias(mac)\n718\t\n719\t\t// Get aliases for the canonical address\n720\t\taliases, err := h.registry.GetAliases(canonicalAddr)\n721\t\tif err != nil {\n722\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n723\t\t\treturn\n724\t\t}\n725\t\n726\t\t// Build response with device info\n727\t\tdevice, _ := h.registry.GetDevice(canonicalAddr)\n728\t\n729\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n730\t\t\t\"canonical_addr\": canonicalAddr,\n731\t\t\t\"device\":         device,\n732\t\t\t\"aliases\":        aliases,\n733\t\t\t\"alias_count\":    len(aliases),\n734\t\t\t\"note\":           \"Devices with auto-rotating addresses (phones) may have multiple historical addresses. Trackers typically have stable addresses.\",\n735\t\t})\n736\t}\n737\t\n738\ttype splitDeviceRequest struct {\n739\t\tCanonicalAddr string `json:\"canonical_addr\"` // The canonical device address\n740\t\tAliasAddr     string `json:\"alias_addr\"`      // The alias to split off\n741\t\tNewPersonID   string `json:\"new_person_id\"`   // Optional: assign to different person\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t// splitDevice splits an alias from its canonical device, creating a separate device entry.\n745\t// Use this when a rotation merge was incorrect.\n746\tfunc (h *Handler) splitDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n747\t\tvar req splitDeviceRequest\n748\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.CanonicalAddr == \"\" || req.AliasAddr == \"\" {\n749\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n750\t\t\treturn\n751\t\t}\n752\t\n753\t\tif req.CanonicalAddr == req.AliasAddr {\n754\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"cannot split device from itself\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n755\t\t\treturn\n756\t\t}\n757\t\n758\t\t// Verify canonical device exists\n759\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.CanonicalAddr); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n760\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"canonical device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n761\t\t\treturn\n762\t\t}\n763\t\n764\t\t// Remove the alias relationship\n765\t\tif err := h.registry.RemoveAlias(req.AliasAddr); err != nil {\n766\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error removing alias\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n767\t\t\treturn\n768\t\t}\n769\t\n770\t\t// If the alias has observations in ble_devices, update it\n771\t\t// Create a proper device entry from the alias\n772\t\tnow := time.Now().UnixNano()\n773\t\t_, err := h.registry.db.Exec(`\n774\t\t\tUPDATE ble_devices SET\n775\t\t\t\tperson_id = ?,\n776\t\t\t\tlast_seen_at = ?\n777\t\t\tWHERE mac = ?\n778\t\t`, req.NewPersonID, now, req.AliasAddr)\n779\t\tif err != nil {\n780\t\t\t// Alias might not exist in ble_devices yet, which is fine\n781\t\t\t// Create a new device entry\n782\t\t\t_, err2 := h.registry.db.Exec(`\n783\t\t\t\tINSERT INTO ble_devices (mac, person_id, last_seen_at, first_seen_at, enabled)\n784\t\t\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1)\n785\t\t\t`, req.AliasAddr, req.NewPersonID, now, now)\n786\t\t\tif err2 != nil {\n787\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error creating device\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n788\t\t\t\treturn\n789\t\t\t}\n790\t\t}\n791\t\n792\t\t// Get the updated canonical device\n793\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.CanonicalAddr)\n794\t\tif err != nil {\n795\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n796\t\t\treturn\n797\t\t}\n798\t\n799\t\t// Get the split device\n800\t\tsplitDevice, _ := h.registry.GetDevice(req.AliasAddr)\n801\t\n802\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n803\t\t\t\"canonical_device\": device,\n804\t\t\t\"split_device\":     splitDevice,\n805\t\t\t\"message\":          \"Successfully split \" + req.AliasAddr + \" from \" + req.CanonicalAddr,\n806\t\t})\n807\t}\n808\t\n809\t// ── Utility endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n810\t\n811\t// ArchiveStaleHandler triggers archival of devices not seen for > 7 days.\n812\tfunc (h *Handler) ArchiveStaleHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n813\t\tdaysStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"days\")\n814\t\tdays := 7\n815\t\tif daysStr != \"\" {\n816\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(daysStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n817\t\t\t\tdays = n\n818\t\t\t}\n819\t\t}\n820\t\n821\t\tcount, err := h.registry.ArchiveStale(time.Duration(days) * 24 * time.Hour)\n822\t\tif err != nil {\n823\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n824\t\t\treturn\n825\t\t}\n826\t\n827\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n828\t\t\t\"archived_count\": count,\n829\t\t\t\"message\":        \"Archived \" + strconv.FormatInt(count, 10) + \" devices not seen in \" + strconv.Itoa(days) + \" days.\",\n830\t\t})\n831\t}\n832\t\n833\tfunc writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, v interface{}) {\n834\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n835\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v) //nolint:errcheck\n836\t}\n837\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e969917b-904e-4516-80e4-c0016537e6d7","uuid":"52b7ff26-f3af-4ae3-8caa-5060b3f2c21b","timestamp":"2026-04-25T12:50:27.236Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/ble/handler.go","content":"package found\"\n//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/people/{id} [delete]\nfunc (h *Handler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n\t// Device endpoints\n\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices\", h.listDevices)\n\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.getDevice)\n\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}/history\", h.getDeviceHistory)\n\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}/aliases\", h.getDeviceAliases)\n\tr.Put(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.updateDevice)\n\tr.Delete(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.archiveDevice)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/devices/preregister\", h.preregisterDevice)\n\n\t// Duplicate detection\n\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/duplicates\", h.listDuplicates)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/merge\", h.mergeDevices)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/split\", h.splitDevice)\n\n\t// People endpoints\n\tr.Get(\"/api/people\", h.listPeople)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/people\", h.createPerson)\n\tr.Get(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.getPerson)\n\tr.Put(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.updatePerson)\n\tr.Delete(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.deletePerson)\n}\n\n// ── Device endpoints ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// listDevices handles GET /api/ble/devices.\n//\n// Returns a list of all BLE devices seen by the system. Devices can be filtered\n// by registration status (registered/discovered), time window (hours parameter),\n// and archival status.\n//\n// Query parameters:\n//   - registered: \"true\" to return only devices assigned to a person\n//   - discovered: \"true\" to return only unassigned devices\n//   - archived: \"true\" to include archived (soft-deleted) devices\n//   - hours: time window in hours (default: 24)\n//\n// Response: JSON object with \"devices\" array and \"privacy_notice\" string.\n// Each device includes: mac, name, label, manufacturer, device_type, device_name,\n// person_id, person_name, rssi_min, rssi_max, rssi_avg, first_seen_at, last_seen_at,\n// last_seen_node, is_archived, is_wearable, enabled, last_location.\n//\n// Status codes:\n//   - 200: Success\n//   - 500: Internal error\nfunc (h *Handler) listDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tincludeArchived := r.URL.Query().Get(\"archived\") == \"true\"\n\tregistered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"registered\")\n\tdiscovered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"discovered\")\n\n\t// Parse hours parameter (default: 24 hours)\n\thoursStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"hours\")\n\thours := 24 // Default to last 24 hours\n\tif hoursStr != \"\" {\n\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(hoursStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n\t\t\thours = n\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar devices []DeviceRecord\n\tvar err error\n\n\t// Filter by registration status and time window\n\tif registered == \"true\" {\n\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n\t\t// Filter to only registered devices (has person_id)\n\t\tvar registeredDevices []DeviceRecord\n\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n\t\t\tif d.PersonID != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tregisteredDevices = append(registeredDevices, d)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tdevices = registeredDevices\n\t} else if discovered == \"true\" {\n\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n\t\t// Filter to only unregistered devices (no person_id)\n\t\tvar unregisteredDevices []DeviceRecord\n\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n\t\t\tif d.PersonID == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tunregisteredDevices = append(unregisteredDevices, d)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tdevices = unregisteredDevices\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Get all devices seen in the time window\n\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n\t}\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif devices == nil {\n\t\tdevices = []DeviceRecord{}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add privacy notice in response header\n\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Privacy-Notice\", \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\")\n\n\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"devices\":       devices,\n\t\t\"privacy_notice\": \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// getDevice handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n//\n// Returns detailed information about a single BLE device by its MAC address.\n// The MAC address should be in uppercase colon-separated hex format (e.g., \"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\").\n//\n// URL parameters:\n//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n//\n// Response: JSON device object with all fields including location history.\n//\n// Status codes:\n//   - 200: Success, device found\n//   - 404: Device not found\n//   - 500: Internal error\nfunc (h *Handler) getDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\twriteJSON(w, device)\n}\n\n// getDeviceHistory handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}/history.\n//\n// Returns the sighting history for a specific BLE device. This includes\n// RSSI observations from nodes that have detected this device over time.\n//\n// URL parameters:\n//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n//\n// Query parameters:\n//   - limit: maximum number of history entries to return (default: 100, max: 1000)\n//\n// Response: JSON object with \"mac\", \"history\" (array of sighting entries),\n// and \"limit\" fields. Each history entry includes timestamp, rssi_dbm, and node_mac.\n//\n// Status codes:\n//   - 200: Success\n//   - 404: Device not found\n//   - 500: Internal error\nfunc (h *Handler) getDeviceHistory(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n\n\t// Parse limit parameter\n\tlimitStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\")\n\tlimit := 100\n\tif limitStr != \"\" {\n\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(limitStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n\t\t\tlimit = n\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check that the device exists first\n\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\thistory, err := h.registry.GetDeviceSightingHistory(mac, limit)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"mac\":     mac,\n\t\t\"history\": history,\n\t\t\"limit\":   limit,\n\t})\n}\n\ntype updateDeviceRequest struct {\n\tLabel      string `json:\"label\"`       // User-assigned display label\n\tDeviceType string `json:\"device_type\"` // Device type (apple_phone, apple_watch, tile, etc.)\n\tPersonID   string `json:\"person_id\"`   // Person ID to assign device to\n}\n\n// updateDevice handles PUT /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n//\n// Updates a BLE device's properties. This endpoint is used to set a human-readable\n// label for a device and/or assign it to a person for identity tracking.\n//\n// URL parameters:\n//   - mac: BLE device MAC address (uppercase colon-separated hex)\n//\n// Request body: JSON object with optional fields:\n//   - label: User-assigned display label (e.g., \"Alice's iPhone\")\n//   - device_type: Device type identifier (e.g., \"apple_phone\", \"tile\", \"fitbit\")\n//   - person_id: UUID of person to assign this device to (must exist)\n//\n// Response: Updated device object as JSON.\n//\n// Status codes:\n//   - 200: Success, device updated\n//   - 400: Invalid request body or person_id not found\n//   - 404: Device not found\n//   - 500: Internal error\nfunc (h *Handler) updateDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n\n\t// Verify device exists\n\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t} else if err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar req updateDeviceRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tupdates := make(map[string]interface{})\n\n\tif req.Label != \"\" {\n\t\tupdates[\"label\"] = req.Label\n\t}\n\tif req.DeviceType != \"\" {\n\t\tupdates[\"device_type\"] = req.DeviceType\n\t}\n\tif req.PersonID != \"\" {\n\t\t// Verify person exists\n\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(req.PersonID); err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tupdates[\"person_id\"] = req.PersonID\n\t}\n\n\tif len(updates) > 0 {\n\t\tif err := h.registry.UpdateDevice(mac, updates); err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\twriteJSON(w, device)\n}\n\nfunc (h *Handler) archiveDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n\n\t// Verify device exists\n\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t} else if err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.registry.ArchiveDevice(mac); Review and merge if appropriate.\",\n\t})\n}\n\ntype mergeDevicesRequest struct {\n\tMAC1 string `json:\"mac1\"`\n\tMAC2 string `json:\"mac2\"`\n}\n\nfunc (h *Handler) mergeDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req mergeDevicesRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.MAC1 == \"\" || req.MAC2 == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif req.MAC1 == req.MAC2 {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"cannot merge device with itself\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify both devices exist\n\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC1); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device 1 not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC2); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device 2 not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.registry.MergeDevices(req.MAC1, req.MAC2); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.MAC1)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"merged\": device,\n\t\t\"message\": \"Devices merged successfully. \" + req.MAC2 + \" has been removed.\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// ── People endpoints ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfunc (h *Handler) listPeople(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tpeople, err := h.registry.GetPeopleWithDevices()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif people == nil {\n\t\tpeople = []map[string]interface{}{}\n\t}\n\twriteJSON(w, people)\n}\n\ntype createPersonRequest struct {\n\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n\tColor string `json:\"color\"`\n}\n\nfunc (h *Handler) createPerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req createPersonRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.Name == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Default color if not provided\n\tif req.Color == \"\" {\n\t\treq.Color = \"#3b82f6\"\n\t}\n\n\tperson, err := h.registry.CreatePerson(req.Name, req.Color)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)\n\twriteJSON(w, person)\n}\n\nfunc (h *Handler) getPerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\tperson, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id)\n\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get devices for this person\n\tdevices, err := h.registry.GetPersonDevices(id)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdevices = nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Find most recent last_seen among devices\n\tvar lastSeen time.Time\n\tfor _, d := range devices {\n\t\tif d.LastSeenAt.After(lastSeen) {\n\t\t\tlastSeen = d.LastSeenAt\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"id\":           person.ID,\n\t\t\"name\":         person.Name,\n\t\t\"color\":        person.Color,\n\t\t\"created_at\":   person.CreatedAt,\n\t\t\"device_count\": len(devices),\n\t\t\"devices\":      devices,\n\t\t\"last_seen\":    lastSeen,\n\t})\n}\n\ntype updatePersonRequest struct {\n\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n\tColor string `json:\"color\"`\n}\n\nfunc (h *Handler) updatePerson(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\t// Verify person exists\n\tif _, err := h.registry.GetPerson(id); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"person not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t} else if err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.registry.DeletePerson(id); err != nil || req.MAC == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body: mac is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Default label to MAC if not provided\n\tif req.Label == \"\" {\n\t\treq.Label = req.MAC\n\t}\n\n\tdevice, err := h.registry.PreregisterDevice(req.MAC, req.Label)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)\n\twriteJSON(w, device)\n}\n\n// ── Alias endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// getDeviceAliases returns the alias history for a device.\n// This includes all rotated addresses that have been merged to this canonical device.\nfunc (h *Handler) getDeviceAliases(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n\n\t// First check if this is an alias - resolve to canonical if so\n\tcanonicalAddr := h.registry.ResolveAlias(mac)\n\n\t// Get aliases for the canonical address\n\taliases, err := h.registry.GetAliases(canonicalAddr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build response with device info\n\tdevice, _ := h.registry.GetDevice(canonicalAddr)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"canonical_addr\": canonicalAddr,\n\t\t\"device\":         device,\n\t\t\"aliases\":        aliases,\n\t\t\"alias_count\":    len(aliases),\n\t\t\"note\":           \"Devices with auto-rotating addresses (phones) may have multiple historical addresses. Trackers typically have stable addresses.\",\n\t})\n}\n\ntype splitDeviceRequest struct {\n\tCanonicalAddr string `json:\"canonical_addr\"` // The canonical device address\n\tAliasAddr     string `json:\"alias_addr\"`      // The alias to split off\n\tNewPersonID   string `json:\"new_person_id\"`   // Optional: assign to different person\n}\n\n// splitDevice splits an alias from its canonical device, creating a separate device entry.\n// Use this when a rotation merge was incorrect.\nfunc (h *Handler) splitDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req splitDeviceRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil || req.CanonicalAddr == \"\" || req.AliasAddr == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"invalid request body\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif req.CanonicalAddr == req.AliasAddr {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"cannot split device from itself\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify canonical device exists\n\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.CanonicalAddr); errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"canonical device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove the alias relationship\n\tif err := h.registry.RemoveAlias(req.AliasAddr); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error removing alias\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// If the alias has observations in ble_devices, update it\n\t// Create a proper device entry from the alias\n\tnow := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\t_, err := h.registry.db.Exec(`\n\t\tUPDATE ble_devices SET\n\t\t\tperson_id = ?,\n\t\t\tlast_seen_at = ?\n\t\tWHERE mac = ?\n\t`, req.NewPersonID, now, req.AliasAddr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Alias might not exist in ble_devices yet, which is fine\n\t\t// Create a new device entry\n\t\t_, err2 := h.registry.db.Exec(`\n\t\t\tINSERT INTO ble_devices (mac, person_id, last_seen_at, first_seen_at, enabled)\n\t\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1)\n\t\t`, req.AliasAddr, req.NewPersonID, now, now)\n\t\tif err2 != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error creating device\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the updated canonical device\n\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(req.CanonicalAddr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the split device\n\tsplitDevice, _ := h.registry.GetDevice(req.AliasAddr)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"canonical_device\": device,\n\t\t\"split_device\":     splitDevice,\n\t\t\"message\":          \"Successfully split \" + req.AliasAddr + \" from \" + req.CanonicalAddr,\n\t})\n}\n\n// ── Utility endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// ArchiveStaleHandler triggers archival of devices not seen for > 7 days.\nfunc (h *Handler) ArchiveStaleHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tdaysStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"days\")\n\tdays := 7\n\tif daysStr != \"\" {\n\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(daysStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n\t\t\tdays = n\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tcount, err := h.registry.ArchiveStale(time.Duration(days) * 24 * time.Hour)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"archived_count\": count,\n\t\t\"message\":        \"Archived \" + strconv.FormatInt(count, 10) + \" devices not seen in \" + strconv.Itoa(days) + \" days.\",\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, v interface{}) {\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v) //nolint:errcheck\n}\n","numLines":837,"startLine":1,"totalLines":837}}}

Package api provides REST API handlers for Spaxel zones and portals.\npackage api\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5\"\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/dashboard\"\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/zones\"\n)\n\n// ZonesHandler manages zones and portals via the zones.Manager.\n// Changes to zones and portals are immediately broadcast to dashboard clients\n// via the ZoneChangeBroadcaster, and also reflected in the next delta tick.\ntype ZonesHandler struct {\n\tmu  sync.RWMutex\n\tmgr *zones.Manager\n\tbc  dashboard.ZoneChangeBroadcaster\n}\n\n// zoneWithOcc extends a zone with current occupancy and people list for API responses.\ntype zoneWithOcc struct {\n\tID        string    `json:\"id\"`\n\tName      string    `json:\"name\"`\n\tColor     string    `json:\"color,omitempty\"`\n\tMinX      float64   `json:\"x\"`\n\tMinY      float64   `json:\"y\"`\n\tMinZ      float64   `json:\"z\"`\n\tMaxX      float64   `json:\"max_x\"`\n\tMaxY      float64   `json:\"max_y\"`\n\tMaxZ      float64   `json:\"max_z\"`\n\tWidth     float64   `json:\"w\"`\n\tDepth     float64   `json:\"d\"`\n\tHeight    float64   `json:\"h\"`\n\tEnabled   bool      `json:\"enabled\"`\n\tZoneType  string    `json:\"zone_type\"`\n\tOccupancy int       `json:\"occupancy\"`\n\tPeople    []string  `json:\"people\"`\n\tCreatedAt time.Time `json:\"created_at\"`\n}\n\n// portalWithZones extends a portal with resolved zone names for API responses.\ntype portalWithZones struct {\n\tID        string    `json:\"id\"`\n\tName      string    `json:\"name\"`\n\tZoneA     string    `json:\"zone_a\"`\n\tZoneB     string    `json:\"zone_b\"`\n\tP1X       float64   `json:\"p1_x\"`\n\tP1Y       float64   `json:\"p1_y\"`\n\tP1Z       float64   `json:\"p1_z\"`\n\tP2X       float64   `json:\"p2_x\"`\n\tP2Y       float64   `json:\"p2_y\"`\n\tP2Z       float64   `json:\"p2_z\"`\n\tP3X       float64   `json:\"p3_x\"`\n\tP3Y       float64   `json:\"p3_y\"`\n\tP3Z       float64   `json:\"p3_z\"`\n\tNX     `json:\"enabled\"`\n\tCreatedAt time.Time `json:\"created_at\"`\n}\n\n// crossingResponse is a single crossing event as returned by the API.\ntype crossingResponse struct {\n\tID        int64     `json:\"id\"`\n\tPortalID  string    `json:\"portal_id\"`\n\tBlobID    int       `json:\"blob_id\"`\n\tDirection string    `json:\"direction\"`\n\tFromZone  string    `json:\"from_zone\"`\n\tToZone    string    `json:\"to_zone\"`\n\tTimestamp time.Time `json:\"timestamp\"`\n\tPerson    string    `json:\"person,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// NewZonesHandler creates a new zones handler backed by a zones.Manager.\nfunc NewZonesHandler(mgr *zones.Manager) *ZonesHandler {\n\treturn &ZonesHandler{mgr: mgr}\n}\n\n// SetZoneChangeBroadcaster sets the broadcaster for immediate WebSocket\n// notifications when zones or portals are modified.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) SetZoneChangeBroadcaster(bc dashboard.ZoneChangeBroadcaster) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\th.bc = bc\n}\n\n// notifyZoneChange broadcasts a zone change event if a broadcaster is set.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) notifyZoneChange(action string, z *zones.Zone) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tbc := h.bc\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\tif bc == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tocc := h.mgr.GetZoneOccupancy(z.ID)\n\tcount := 0\n\tif occ != nil {\n\t\tcount = occ.Count\n\t}\n\tbc.BroadcastZoneChange(action, dashboard.ZoneSnapshot{\n\t\tID:    z.ID,\n\t\tName:  z.Name,\n\t\tCount: count,\n\t\tMinX:  z.MinX,\n\t\tMinY:  z.MinY,\n\t\tMinZ:  z.MinZ,\n\t\tSizeX: z.MaxX - z.MinX,\n\t\tSizeY: z.MaxY - z.MinY,\n\t\tSizeZ: z.MaxZ - z.MinZ,\n\t})\n}\n\n// notifyPortalChange broadcasts a portal change event if a broadcaster is set.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) notifyPortalChange(action string, p *zones.Portal) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tbc := h.bc\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\tif bc == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tbc.BroadcastPortalChange(action, dashboard.PortalSnapshot{\n\t\tID:      p.ID,\n\t\tName:    p.Name,\n\t\tZoneA:   p.ZoneAID,\n\t\tZoneB:   p.ZoneBID,\n\t\tP1X:     p.P1X,\n\t\tP1Y:     p.P1Y,\n\t\tP1Z:     p.P1Z,\n\t\tP2X:     p.P2X,\n\t\tP2Y:     p.P2Y,\n\t\tP2Z:     p.P2Z,\n\t\tP3X:     p.P3X,\n\t\tP3Y:     p.P3Y,\n\t\tP3Z:     p.P3Z,\n\t\tNX:      p.NX,\n\t\tNY:      p.NY,\n\t\tNZ:      p.NZ,\n\t\tWidth:   p.Width,\n\t\tHeight:  p.Height,\n\t\tEnabled: p.Enabled,\n\t})\n}\n\n// Close closes the underlying manager.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) Close() error {\n\treturn h.mgr.Close()\n}\n\n// RegisterRoutes registers zones and portals endpoints.\n//\n// Zones:\n//\n//\tGET [get]\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n\t// Zones\n\tr.Get(\"/api/zones\", h.listZones)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/zones\", h.createZone)\n\tr.Put(\"/api/zones/{id}\", h.mgr.GetZoneOccupancy(z.ID)\n\tcount := 0\n\tif occ != nil {\n\t\tcount = occ.Count\n\t}\n\treturn zoneWithOcc{\n\t\tID:        z.ID,\n\t\tName:      z.Name,\n\t\tColor:     z.Color,\n\t\tMinX:      z.MinX,\n\t\tMinY:      z.MinY,\n\t\tMinZ:      z.MinZ,\n\t\tMaxX:      z.MaxX,\n\t\tMaxY:      z.MaxY,\n\t\tMaxZ:      z.MaxZ,\n\t\tWidth:     z.MaxX - z.MinX,\n\t\tDepth:     z.MaxY - z.MinY,\n\t\tHeight:    z.MaxZ - z.MinZ,\n\t\tEnabled:   z.Enabled,\n\t\tZoneType:  string(z.ZoneType),\n\t\tOccupancy: count,\n\t\tPeople:    []string{},\n\t\tCreatedAt: z.CreatedAt,\n\t}\n}\n\n// toPortalResponse converts a zones.Portal to the API response format.\nfunc toPortalResponse(p *zones.Portal) portalWithZones {\n\treturn portalWithZones{\n\t\tID:        p.ID,\n\t\tName: current occupancy.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) listZones(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tallZones := h.mgr.GetAllZones()\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tresponse := err\n297\t\t}\n298\t\tdefer rows.Close()\n299\t\n300\t\tfor rows.Next() {\n301\t\t\tvar enabled int\n302\t\t\tvar createdAt int64\n303\t\t\tp := &Portal{}\n304\t\t\tif err := rows.Scan(&p.ID, &p.Name, &p.ZoneAID, &p.ZoneBID, &p.P1X, &p.P1Y, &p.P1Z, &p.P2X, &p.P2Y, &p.P2Z, &p.P3X, &p.P3Y, &p.P3Z, &p.NX, &p.NY, &p.NZ, &p.Width, &p.Height, &enabled, &createdAt); err != nil {\n305\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to scan portal: %v\", err)\n306\t\t\t\tcontinue\n307\t\t\t}\n308\t\t\tp.Enabled = enabled != 0\n309\t\t\tif createdAt > 0 {\n310\t\t\t\tp.CreatedAt = time.Unix(0, createdAt)\n311\t\t\t}\n312\t\t\tm.portals[p.ID] = p\n313\t\t}\n314\t\treturn rows.Err()\n315\t}\n316\t\n317\t// Close closes the database.\n318\tfunc (m *Manager) Close() error {\n319\t\treturn m.db.Close()\n320\t}\n321\t\n322\t// SetOnCrossing sets the callback for crossing events.\n323\tfunc (m *Manager) SetOnCrossing(cb func(CrossingEvent)) {\n324\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n325\t\tm.onCrossing = cb\n326\t\tm.mu.Unlock()\n327\t}\n328\t\n329\t// SetOnZoneEntry sets the callback for zone entry events.\n330\tfunc (m *Manager) SetOnZoneEntry(cb func(ZoneTransitionEvent)) {\n331\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n332\t\tm.onZoneEntry = cb\n333\t\tm.mu.Unlock()\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// SetOnZoneExit sets the callback for zone exit events.\n337\tfunc (m *Manager) SetOnZoneExit(cb func(ZoneTransitionEvent)) {\n338\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n339\t\tm.onZoneExit = cb\n340\t\tm.mu.Unlock()\n341\t}\n342\t\n343\t// CreateZone creates a new zone.\n344\tfunc (m *Manager) CreateZone(zone *Zone) error {\n345\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n346\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n347\t\n348\t\t// Set default zone type if not specified\n349\t\tif zone.ZoneType == \"\" {\n350\t\t\tzone.ZoneType = ZoneTypeNormal\n351\t\t}\n352\t\n353\t\tnow := time.Now().UnixNano()\n354\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`\n355\t\t\tINSERT INTO zones (id, name, color, min_x, min_y, min_z, max_x, max_y, max_z, enabled, zone_type, is_children_zone, created_at)\n356\t\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n357\t\t`, zone.ID, zone.Name, zone.Color, zone.MinX, zone.MinY, zone.MinZ, zone.MaxX, zone.MaxY, zone.MaxZ, zone.Enabled, string(zone.ZoneType), zone.IsChildrenZone, now)\n358\t\tif err != nil {\n359\t\t\treturn err\n360\t\t}\n361\t\n362\t\tzone.CreatedAt = time.Unix(0, now)\n363\t\tm.zones[zone.ID] = zone\n364\t\treturn nil\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// UpdateZone updates an existing zone.\n368\tfunc (m *Manager) UpdateZone(zone *Zone) error {\n369\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n370\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n371\t\n372\t\t// Set default zone type if not specified\n373\t\tif zone.ZoneType == \"\" {\n374\t\t\tzone.ZoneType = ZoneTypeNormal\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`\n378\t\t\tUPDATE zones SET name=?, color=?, min_x=?, min_y=?, min_z=?, max_x=?, max_y=?, max_z=?, enabled=?, zone_type=?, is_children_zone=?\n379\t\t\tWHERE id=?\n380\t\t`, zone.Name, zone.Color, zone.MinX, zone.MinY, zone.MinZ, zone.MaxX, zone.MaxY, zone.MaxZ, zone.Enabled, string(zone.ZoneType), zone.IsChildrenZone, zone.ID)\n381\t\tif err != nil {\n382\t\t\treturn err\n383\t\t}\n384\t\n385\t\tm.zones[zone.ID] = zone\n386\t\treturn nil\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// DeleteZone deletes a zone.\n390\tfunc (m *Manager) DeleteZone(id string) error {\n391\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n392\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n393\t\n394\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`DELETE FROM zones WHERE id=?`, id)\n395\t\tif err != nil {\n396\t\t\treturn err\n397\t\t}\n398\t\n399\t\tdelete(m.zones, id)\n400\t\tdelete(m.occupancy, id)\n401\t\treturn nil\n402\t}\n403\t\n404\t// GetZone returns a zone by ID.\n405\tfunc (m *Manager) GetZone(id string) *Zone {\n406\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n407\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n408\t\treturn m.zones[id]\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\t// GetAllZones returns all zones.\n412\tfunc (m *Manager) GetAllZones() []*Zone {\n413\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n414\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n415\t\n416\t\tzones := make([]*Zone, 0, len(m.zones))\n417\t\tfor _, z := range m.zones {\n418\t\t\tzones = append(zones, z)\n419\t\t}\n420\t\treturn zones\n421\t}\n422\t\n423\t// CreatePortal creates a new portal.\n424\tfunc (m *Manager) CreatePortal(portal *Portal) error {\n425\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n426\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n427\t\n428\t\t// Compute normal vector from three points\n429\t\tportal.NX, portal.NY, portal.NZ = computeNormal(portal)\n430\t\n431\t\tnow := time.Now().UnixNano()\n432\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`\n433\t\t\tINSERT INTO portals (id, name, zone_a_id, zone_b_id, p1_x, p1_y, p1_z, p2_x, p2_y, p2_z, p3_x, p3_y, p3_z, n_x, n_y, n_z, width, height, enabled, created_at)\n434\t\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n435\t\t`, portal.ID, portal.Name, portal.ZoneAID, portal.ZoneBID,\n436\t\t\tportal.P1X, portal.P1Y, portal.P1Z, portal.P2X, portal.P2Y, portal.P2Z,\n437\t\t\tportal.P3X, portal.P3Y, portal.P3Z, portal.NX, portal.NY, portal.NZ,\n438\t\t\tportal.Width, portal.Height, portal.Enabled, now)\n439\t\tif err != nil {\n440\t\t\treturn err\n441\t\t}\n442\t\n443\t\tportal.CreatedAt = time.Unix(0, now)\n444\t\tm.portals[portal.ID] = portal\n445\t\treturn nil\n446\t}\n447\t\n448\t// UpdatePortal updates an existing portal.\n449\tfunc (m *Manager) UpdatePortal(portal *Portal) error {\n450\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n451\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n452\t\n453\t\t// Compute normal vector from three points\n454\t\tportal.NX, portal.NY, portal.NZ = computeNormal(portal)\n455\t\n456\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`\n457\t\t\tUPDATE portals SET name=?, zone_a_id=?, zone_b_id=?, p1_x=?, p1_y=?, p1_z=?, p2_x=?, p2_y=?, p2_z=?, p3_x=?, p3_y=?, p3_z=?, n_x=?, n_y=?, n_z=?, width=?, height=?, enabled=?\n458\t\t\tWHERE id=?\n459\t\t`, portal.Name, portal.ZoneAID, portal.ZoneBID,\n460\t\t\tportal.P1X, portal.P1Y, portal.P1Z, portal.P2X, portal.P2Y, portal.P2Z,\n461\t\t\tportal.P3X, portal.P3Y, portal.P3Z, portal.NX, portal.NY, portal.NZ,\n462\t\t\tportal.Width, portal.Height, portal.Enabled, portal.ID)\n463\t\tif err != nil {\n464\t\t\treturn err\n465\t\t}\n466\t\n467\t\tm.portals[portal.ID] = portal\n468\t\treturn nil\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t// DeletePortal deletes a portal.\n472\tfunc (m *Manager) DeletePortal(id string) error {\n473\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n474\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n475\t\n476\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`DELETE FROM portals WHERE id=?`, id)\n477\t\tif err != nil {\n478\t\t\treturn err\n479\t\t}\n480\t\n481\t\tdelete(m.portals, id)\n482\t\treturn nil\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// GetPortal returns a portal by ID.\n486\tfunc (m *Manager) GetPortal(id string) *Portal {\n487\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n488\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n489\t\treturn m.portals[id]\n490\t}\n491\t\n492\t// GetAllPortals returns all portals.\n493\tfunc (m *Manager) GetAllPortals() []*Portal {\n494\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n495\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n496\t\n497\t\tportals := make([]*Portal, 0, len(m.portals))\n498\t\tfor _, p := range m.portals {\n499\t\t\tportals = append(portals, p)\n500\t\t}\n501\t\treturn portals\n502\t}\n503\t\n504\t// pendingCrossing holds a crossing event and associated zone transition events to fire after lock release.\n505\ttype pendingCrossing struct {\n506\t\tcrossing CrossingEvent\n507\t\texit     *ZoneTransitionEvent\n508\t\tentry    *ZoneTransitionEvent\n509\t}\n510\t\n511\t// UpdateBlobPositions updates blob positions and detects portal crossings.\n512\t// Callbacks are fired synchronously after the lock is released to avoid deadlock.\n513\tfunc (m *Manager) UpdateBlobPositions(blobs []struct {\n514\t\tID     int\n515\t\tX, Y, Z float64\n516\t}) {\n517\t\tnow := time.Now()\n518\t\n519\t\t// Collect pending events while holding the lock.\n520\t\tvar pending []pendingCrossing\n521\t\n522\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n523\t\n524\t\tfor _, blob := range blobs {\n525\t\t\t// Get previous position\n526\t\t\tprev, existed := m.blobPositions[blob.ID]\n527\t\n528\t\t\t// Determine which zone the blob is in\n529\t\t\tzoneID := m.findZoneForPosition(blob.X, blob.Y, blob.Z)\n530\t\n531\t\t\t// Update position\n532\t\t\tm.blobPositions[blob.ID] = struct {\n533\t\t\t\tX, Y, Z     float64\n534\t\t\t\tZoneID      string\n535\t\t\t\tLastUpdated time.Time\n536\t\t\t}{blob.X, blob.Y, blob.Z, zoneID, now}\n537\t\n538\t\t\tif existed && prev.ZoneID != zoneID {\n539\t\t\t\t// Remove blob from old zone occupancy\n540\t\t\t\tif prev.ZoneID != \"\" {\n541\t\t\t\t\tm.removeFromOccupancy(prev.ZoneID, blob.ID)\n542\t\t\t\t}\n543\t\t\t}\n544\t\n545\t\t\t// Add to new zone occupancy\n546\t\t\tif zoneID != \"\" {\n547\t\t\t\tm.updateOccupancy(zoneID, blob.ID)\n548\t\t\t}\n549\t\n550\t\t\t// Detect portal crossings and collect zone transition events\n551\t\t\tif existed && prev.ZoneID != zoneID {\n552\t\t\t\tcrossings := m.collectCrossings(blob.ID, prev.X, prev.Y, prev.Z, blob.X, blob.Y, blob.Z, zoneID)\n553\t\n554\t\t\t\t// Build zone exit event\n555\t\t\t\tvar exitEvt *ZoneTransitionEvent\n556\t\t\t\tif prev.ZoneID != \"\" {\n557\t\t\t\t\tprevName := \"\"\n558\t\t\t\t\tif z, ok := m.zones[prev.ZoneID]; ok {\n559\t\t\t\t\t\tprevName = z.Name\n560\t\t\t\t\t}\n561\t\t\t\t\texitEvt = &ZoneTransitionEvent{\n562\t\t\t\t\t\tBlobID:    blob.ID,\n563\t\t\t\t\t\tZoneID:    prev.ZoneID,\n564\t\t\t\t\t\tZoneName:  prevName,\n565\t\t\t\t\t\tKind:      \"zone_exit\",\n566\t\t\t\t\t\tTimestamp: now,\n567\t\t\t\t\t}\n568\t\t\t\t}\n569\t\n570\t\t\t\t// Build zone entry event\n571\t\t\t\tvar entryEvt *ZoneTransitionEvent\n572\t\t\t\tif zoneID != \"\" {\n573\t\t\t\t\tnewName := \"\"\n574\t\t\t\t\tif z, ok := m.zones[zoneID]; ok {\n575\t\t\t\t\t\tnewName = z.Name\n576\t\t\t\t\t}\n577\t\t\t\t\tentryEvt = &ZoneTransitionEvent{\n578\t\t\t\t\t\tBlobID:    blob.ID,\n579\t\t\t\t\t\tZoneID:    zoneID,\n580\t\t\t\t\t\tZoneName:  newName,\n581\t\t\t\t\t\tKind:      \"zone_entry\",\n582\t\t\t\t\t\tTimestamp: now,\n583\t\t\t\t\t}\n584\t\t\t\t}\n585\t\n586\t\t\t\tfor _, c := range crossings {\n587\t\t\t\t\tpending = append(pending, pendingCrossing{crossing: c, exit: exitEvt, entry: entryEvt})\n588\t\t\t\t\t// Only attach zone events to the first crossing\n589\t\t\t\t\texitEvt = nil\n590\t\t\t\t\tentryEvt = nil\n591\t\t\t\t}\n592\t\n593\t\t\t\t// If no portal crossings but zone changed, still fire zone events\n594\t\t\t\tif len(crossings) == 0 {\n595\t\t\t\t\tpending = append(pending, pendingCrossing{exit: exitEvt, entry: entryEvt})\n596\t\t\t\t}\n597\t\t\t}\n598\t\t}\n599\t\n600\t\t// Clean up old blob positions (not seen in 10 seconds)\n601\t\tfor id, pos := range m.blobPositions {\n602\t\t\tif now.Sub(pos.LastUpdated) > 10*time.Second {\n603\t\t\t\tdelete(m.blobPositions, id)\n604\t\t\t\t// Also remove from occupancy and persist\n605\t\t\t\tfor zoneID, occ := range m.occupancy {\n606\t\t\t\t\tnewBlobIDs := make([]int, 0)\n607\t\t\t\t\tfor _, bid := range occ.BlobIDs {\n608\t\t\t\t\t\tif bid != id {\n609\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnewBlobIDs = append(newBlobIDs, bid)\n610\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n611\t\t\t\t\t}\n612\t\t\t\t\tif len(newBlobIDs) != len(occ.BlobIDs) {\n613\t\t\t\t\t\tocc.BlobIDs = newBlobIDs\n614\t\t\t\t\t\tocc.Count = len(occ.BlobIDs)\n615\t\t\t\t\t\tm.persistOccupancyCount(zoneID, occ.Count)\n616\t\t\t\t\t}\n617\t\t\t\t}\n618\t\t\t}\n619\t\t}\n620\t\n621\t\tonCrossing := m.onCrossing\n622\t\tonZoneEntry := m.onZoneEntry\n623\t\tonZoneExit := m.onZoneExit\n624\t\n625\t\tm.mu.Unlock()\n626\t\n627\t\t// Fire callbacks synchronously after releasing the lock.\n628\t\tfor _, p := range pending {\n629\t\t\tif p.crossing.PortalID != \"\" && onCrossing != nil {\n630\t\t\t\tonCrossing(p.crossing)\n631\t\t\t}\n632\t\t\tif p.exit != nil && onZoneExit != nil {\n633\t\t\t\tonZoneExit(*p.exit)\n634\t\t\t}\n635\t\t\tif p.entry != nil && onZoneEntry != nil {\n636\t\t\t\tonZoneEntry(*p.entry)\n637\t\t\t}\n638\t\t}\n639\t}\n640\t\n641\t// findZoneForPosition returns the zone ID containing the position.\n642\tfunc (m *Manager) findZoneForPosition(x, y, z float64) string {\n643\t\tfor id, zone := range m.zones {\n644\t\t\tif !zone.Enabled {\n645\t\t\t\tcontinue\n646\t\t\t}\n647\t\t\tif x >= zone.MinX && x <= zone.MaxX &&\n648\t\t\t\ty >= zone.MinY && y <= zone.MaxY &&\n649\t\t\t\tz >= zone.MinZ && z <= zone.MaxZ {\n650\t\t\t\treturn id\n651\t\t\t}\n652\t\t}\n653\t\treturn \"\"\n654\t}\n655\t\n656\t// updateOccupancy updates the occupancy count for a zone.\n657\t// Persists the new count to SQLite for restart recovery.\n658\tfunc (m *Manager) updateOccupancy(zoneID string, blobID int) {\n659\t\tocc, exists := m.occupancy[zoneID]\n660\t\tif !exists {\n661\t\t\tocc = &ZoneOccupancy{\n662\t\t\t\tZoneID:  zoneID,\n663\t\t\t\tBlobIDs: []int{blobID},\n664\t\t\t\tCount:   1,\n665\t\t\t}\n666\t\t\tm.occupancy[zoneID] = occ\n667\t\t\tm.persistOccupancyCount(zoneID, 1)\n668\t\t\treturn\n669\t\t}\n670\t\n671\t\t// Check if blob already in zone\n672\t\tfor _, id := range occ.BlobIDs {\n673\t\t\tif id == blobID {\n674\t\t\t\treturn\n675\t\t\t}\n676\t\t}\n677\t\n678\t\tocc.BlobIDs = append(occ.BlobIDs, blobID)\n679\t\tocc.Count = len(occ.BlobIDs)\n680\t\tm.persistOccupancyCount(zoneID, occ.Count)\n681\t}\n682\t\n683\t// removeFromOccupancy removes a blob from a zone's occupancy tracking.\n684\t// Caller must hold m.mu write lock.\n685\tfunc (m *Manager) removeFromOccupancy(zoneID string, blobID int) {\n686\t\tocc, exists := m.occupancy[zoneID]\n687\t\tif !exists {\n688\t\t\treturn\n689\t\t}\n690\t\tnewBlobIDs := make([]int, 0, len(occ.BlobIDs))\n691\t\tfor _, id := range occ.BlobIDs {\n692\t\t\tif id != blobID {\n693\t\t\t\tnewBlobIDs = append(newBlobIDs, id)\n694\t\t\t}\n695\t\t}\n696\t\tif len(newBlobIDs) == len(occ.BlobIDs) {\n697\t\t\treturn // blob was not in this zone\n698\t\t}\n699\t\tocc.BlobIDs = newBlobIDs\n700\t\tocc.Count = len(occ.BlobIDs)\n701\t\tm.persistOccupancyCount(zoneID, occ.Count)\n702\t}\n703\t\n704\t// persistOccupancyCount writes a single zone's occupancy to SQLite.\n705\t// Caller must hold m.mu write lock.\n706\tfunc (m *Manager) persistOccupancyCount(zoneID string, count int) {\n707\t\tnowMs := time.Now().UnixMilli()\n708\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`\n709\t\t\tUPDATE zones SET last_known_occupancy = ?, occupancy_updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?\n710\t\t`, count, nowMs, zoneID)\n711\t\tif err != nil {\n712\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to persist occupancy for zone %s: %v\", zoneID, err)\n713\t\t}\n714\t}\n715\t\n716\t// collectCrossings detects portal crossings and persists them, returning the events.\n717\t// Caller must hold m.mu. Callbacks are NOT fired here — caller fires them after releasing the lock.\n718\tfunc (m *Manager) collectCrossings(blobID int, prevX, prevY, prevZ, currX, currY, currZ float64, newZoneID string) []CrossingEvent {\n719\t\tvar events []CrossingEvent\n720\t\n721\t\tfor _, portal := range m.portals {\n722\t\t\tif !portal.Enabled {\n723\t\t\t\tcontinue\n724\t\t\t}\n725\t\n726\t\t\t// Check if portal connects to the new zone\n727\t\t\tif portal.ZoneAID != newZoneID && portal.ZoneBID != newZoneID {\n728\t\t\t\tcontinue\n729\t\t\t}\n730\t\n731\t\t\t// Compute signed distance from portal plane\n732\t\t\tprevSide := pointPlaneSide(prevX, prevY, prevZ, portal.P1X, portal.P1Y, portal.P1Z, portal.NX, portal.NY, portal.NZ)\n733\t\t\tcurrSide := pointPlaneSide(currX, currY, currZ, portal.P1X, portal.P1Y, portal.P1Z, portal.NX, portal.NY, portal.NZ)\n734\t\n735\t\t\t// Check if crossed (signs are different)\n736\t\t\tif prevSide*currSide < 0 {\n737\t\t\t\t// Determine direction based on where the blob came FROM (prevSide),\n738\t\t\t\t// not where it is now. prevSide > 0 means it was on the A side\n739\t\t\t\t// (positive half-space), so it crossed from A to B.\n740\t\t\t\tvar direction int\n741\t\t\t\tvar fromZone, toZone string\n742\t\t\t\tif prevSide > 0 {\n743\t\t\t\t\tdirection = 1 // A->B (was on A side, now on B side)\n744\t\t\t\t\tfromZone = portal.ZoneAID\n745\t\t\t\t\ttoZone = portal.ZoneBID\n746\t\t\t\t} else {\n747\t\t\t\t\tdirection = -1 // B->A (was on B side, now on A side)\n748\t\t\t\t\tfromZone = portal.ZoneBID\n749\t\t\t\t\ttoZone = portal.ZoneAID\n750\t\t\t\t}\n751\t\n752\t\t\t\tevent := CrossingEvent{\n753\t\t\t\t\tPortalID:  portal.ID,\n754\t\t\t\t\tBlobID:    blobID,\n755\t\t\t\t\tDirection: direction,\n756\t\t\t\t\tFromZone:  fromZone,\n757\t\t\t\t\tToZone:    toZone,\n758\t\t\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n759\t\t\t\t}\n760\t\n761\t\t\t\t// Persist event\n762\t\t\t\tm.recordCrossing(event)\n763\t\n764\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Portal crossing: blob %d crossed %s (direction: %d)\", blobID, portal.Name, direction)\n765\t\n766\t\t\t\tevents = append(events, event)\n767\t\t\t}\n768\t\t}\n769\t\n770\t\treturn events\n771\t}\n772\t\n773\t// pointPlaneSide returns which side of a plane a point is on (>0 or <0).\n774\tfunc pointPlaneSide(px, py, pz, p1x, p1y, p1z, nx, ny, nz float64) float64 {\n775\t\t// Vector from plane point to point\n776\t\tdx := px - p1x\n777\t\tdy := py - p1y\n778\t\tdz := pz - p1z\n779\t\n780\t\t// Dot product with normal\n781\t\treturn dx*nx + dy*ny + dz*nz\n782\t}\n783\t\n784\t// computeNormal computes the normal vector from three points.\n785\tfunc computeNormal(p *Portal) (nx, ny, nz float64) {\n786\t\t// Vectors from P1 to P2 and P1 to P3\n787\t\tv1x := p.P2X - p.P1X\n788\t\tv1y := p.P2Y - p.P1Y\n789\t\tv1z := p.P2Z - p.P1Z\n790\t\n791\t\tv2x := p.P3X - p.P1X\n792\t\tv2y := p.P3Y - p.P1Y\n793\t\tv2z := p.P3Z - p.P1Z\n794\t\n795\t\t// Cross product\n796\t\tnx = v1y*v2z - v1z*v2y\n797\t\tny = v1z*v2x - v1x*v2z\n798\t\tnz = v1x*v2y - v1y*v2x\n799\t\n800\t\t// Normalize\n801\t\tlength := math.Sqrt(nx*nx + ny*ny + nz*nz)\n802\t\tif length > 0 {\n803\t\t\tnx /= length\n804\t\t\tny /= length\n805\t\t\tnz /= length\n806\t\t}\n807\t\n808\t\treturn nx, ny, nz\n809\t}\n810\t\n811\t// recordCrossing persists a crossing event.\n812\tfunc (m *Manager) recordCrossing(event CrossingEvent) {\n813\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`\n814\t\t\tINSERT INTO crossing_events (portal_id, blob_id, direction, from_zone, to_zone, timestamp, identity)\n815\t\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n816\t\t`, event.PortalID, event.BlobID, event.Direction, event.FromZone, event.ToZone, event.Timestamp.UnixMilli(), event.Identity)\n817\t\tif err != nil {\n818\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to record crossing event: %v\", err)\n819\t\t}\n820\t}\n821\t\n822\t// GetOccupancy returns current occupancy for all zones.\n823\tfunc (m *Manager) GetOccupancy() map[string]*ZoneOccupancy {\n824\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n825\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n826\t\n827\t\tresult := make(map[string]*ZoneOccupancy)\n828\t\tfor k, v := range m.occupancy {\n829\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n830\t\t}\n831\t\treturn result\n832\t}\n833\t\n834\t// GetZoneOccupancy returns occupancy for a specific zone.\n835\tfunc (m *Manager) GetZoneOccupancy(zoneID string) *ZoneOccupancy {\n836\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n837\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n838\t\treturn m.occupancy[zoneID]\n839\t}\n840\t\n841\t// GetBlobZone returns the zone ID that a blob is currently in.\n842\tfunc (m *Manager) GetBlobZone(blobID int) string {\n843\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n844\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n845\t\n846\t\tpos, exists := m.blobPositions[blobID]\n847\t\tif !exists {\n848\t\t\treturn \"\"\n849\t\t}\n850\t\treturn pos.ZoneID\n851\t}\n852\t\n853\t// UpdateBlobPosition updates a single blob's position (convenience method).\n854\tfunc (m *Manager) UpdateBlobPosition(blobID int, x, y, z float64) {\n855\t\tm.UpdateBlobPositions([]struct {\n856\t\t\tID     int\n857\t\t\tX, Y, Z float64\n858\t\t}{{ID: blobID, X: x, Y: y, Z: z}})\n859\t}\n860\t\n861\t// GetRecentCrossings returns recent crossing events.\n862\tfunc (m *Manager) GetRecentCrossings(limit int) []CrossingEvent {\n863\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n864\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n865\t\n866\t\trows, err := m.db.Query(`\n867\t\t\tSELECT portal_id, blob_id, direction, from_zone, to_zone, timestamp, identity\n868\t\t\tFROM crossing_events\n869\t\t\tORDER BY timestamp DESC\n870\t\t\tLIMIT ?\n871\t\t`, limit)\n872\t\tif err != nil {\n873\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to query crossings: %v\", err)\n874\t\t\treturn nil\n875\t\t}\n876\t\tdefer rows.Close()\n877\t\n878\t\tvar events []CrossingEvent\n879\t\tfor rows.Next() {\n880\t\t\tvar event CrossingEvent\n881\t\t\tvar ts int64\n882\t\t\tif err := rows.Scan(&event.PortalID, &event.BlobID, &event.Direction, &event.FromZone, &event.ToZone, &ts, &event.Identity); err != nil {\n883\t\t\t\tcontinue\n884\t\t\t}\n885\t\t\tevent.Timestamp = time.UnixMilli(ts)\n886\t\t\tevents = append(events, event)\n887\t\t}\n888\t\treturn events\n889\t}\n890\t\n891\t// GetBlobDwellTime returns how long a blob has been in a specific zone.\n892\tfunc (m *Manager) GetBlobDwellTime(blobID int, zoneID string) (time.Duration, bool) {\n893\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n894\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n895\t\n896\t\t// Find the most recent crossing event where this blob entered this zone\n897\t\tvar enterTime int64\n898\t\terr := m.db.QueryRow(`\n899\t\t\tSELECT timestamp FROM crossing_events\n900\t\t\tWHERE blob_id = ? AND to_zone = ?\n901\t\t\tORDER BY timestamp DESC\n902\t\t\tLIMIT 1\n903\t\t`, blobID, zoneID).Scan(&enterTime)\n904\t\n905\t\tif err != nil {\n906\t\t\t// No crossing event found - use last position update time\n907\t\t\tpos, exists := m.blobPositions[blobID]\n908\t\t\tif !exists || pos.ZoneID != zoneID {\n909\t\t\t\treturn 0, false\n910\t\t\t}\n911\t\t\treturn time.Since(pos.LastUpdated), true\n912\t\t}\n913\t\n914\t\t// Calculate dwell time since entering the zone\n915\t\tdwellTime := time.Since(time.UnixMilli(enterTime))\n916\t\treturn dwellTime, true\n917\t}\n918\t\n919\t// true if the zone is a children zone.\n925\tfunc (z *Zone) IsChildrenZoneType() bool {\n926\t\treturn z.ZoneType == ZoneTypeChildren || z.IsChildrenZone\n927\t}\n928\t\n929\t// GetBedroomZones returns all zones configured as bedrooms.\n930\tfunc (m *Manager) GetBedroomZones() []*Zone {\n931\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n932\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n933\t\n934\t\tvar bedrooms []*Zone\n935\t\tfor _, zone := range m.zones {\n936\t\t\tif zone.Enabled && zone.IsBedroomZone() {\n937\t\t\t\tbedrooms = append(bedrooms, zone)\n938\t\t\t}\n939\t\t}\n940\t\treturn bedrooms\n941\t}\n942\t\n943\t// GetZoneByPosition returns the zone containing the given position.\n944\tfunc (m *Manager) GetZoneByPosition(x, y, z float64) *Zone {\n945\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n946\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n947\t\n948\t\tfor _, zone := range m.zones {\n949\t\t\tif !zone.Enabled {\n950\t\t\t\tcontinue\n951\t\t\t}\n952\t\t\tif x >= zone.MinX && x <= zone.MaxX &&\n953\t\t\t\ty >= zone.MinY && y <= zone.MaxY &&\n954\t\t\t\tz >= zone.MinZ && z <= zone.MaxZ {\n955\t\t\t\treturn zone\n956\t\t\t}\n957\t\t}\n958\t\treturn nil\n959\t}\n960\t\n961\t// ─── Occupancy Reconciliation ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n962\t\n963\t// reconcileOccupancy restores zone occupancy counts from persisted values\n964\t// plus net portal crossings since midnight. Called once on startup.\n965\tfunc (m *Manager) reconcileOccupancy() {\n966\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n967\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n968\t\n969\t\tnow := time.Now().In(m.tz)\n970\t\tmidnight := time.Date(now.Year(), now.Month(), now.Day(), 0, 0, 0, 0, m.tz)\n971\t\tmidnightMs := midnight.UnixMilli()\n972\t\n973\t\t// Step 1: Load last_known_occupancy per zone\n974\t\trows, err := m.db.Query(`SELECT id, last_known_occupancy FROM zones`)\n975\t\tif err != nil {\n976\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to load persisted occupancy: %v\", err)\n977\t\t\treturn\n978\t\t}\n979\t\ttype persisted struct {\n980\t\t\tzoneID string\n981\t\t\tcount  int\n982\t\t}\n983\t\tvar persistedOcc []persisted\n984\t\tfor rows.Next() {\n985\t\t\tvar p persisted\n986\t\t\tif err := rows.Scan(&p.zoneID, &p.count); err != nil {\n987\t\t\t\tcontinue\n988\t\t\t}\n989\t\t\tpersistedOcc = append(persistedOcc, p)\n990\t\t}\n991\t\trows.Close()\n992\t\n993\t\t// Step 2: Compute net portal crossings since midnight\n994\t\tcrossRows, err := m.db.Query(`\n995\t\t\tSELECT from_zone, to_zone, timestamp\n996\t\t\tFROM crossing_events\n997\t\t\tWHERE timestamp >= ?\n998\t\t`, midnightMs)\n999\t\tif err != nil {\n1000\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to query portal crossings since midnight: %v\", err)\n1001\t\t\treturn\n1002\t\t}\n1003\t\tdefer crossRows.Close()\n1004\t\n1005\t\tnetPerZone := make(map[string]int)\n1006\t\tfor crossRows.Next() {\n1007\t\t\tvar fromZone, toZone string\n1008\t\t\tvar tsMs int64\n1009\t\t\tif err := crossRows.Scan(&fromZone, &toZone, &tsMs); err != nil {\n1010\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1011\t\t\t}\n1012\t\t\t// Each crossing: from_zone loses one, to_zone gains one\n1013\t\t\tnetPerZone[fromZone]--\n1014\t\t\tnetPerZone[toZone]++\n1015\t\t}\n1016\t\n1017\t\t// Step 3: Apply net crossings to loaded occupancy\n1018\t\tanyRestored := false\n1019\t\tfor _, p := range persistedOcc {\n1020\t\t\tif _, exists := m.zones[p.zoneID]; !exists {\n1021\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1022\t\t\t}\n1023\t\t\treconciled := p.count + netPerZone[p.zoneID]\n1024\t\t\tif reconciled < 0 {\n1025\t\t\t\treconciled = 0\n1026\t\t\t}\n1027\t\t\tm.occupancy[p.zoneID] = &ZoneOccupancy{\n1028\t\t\t\tZoneID:      p.zoneID,\n1029\t\t\t\tCount:       reconciled,\n1030\t\t\t\tBlobIDs:     nil,\n1031\t\t\t\tLastUpdated: now,\n1032\t\t\t\tStatus:      OccupancyUncertain,\n1033\t\t\t}\n1034\t\t\tif reconciled > 0 {\n1035\t\t\t\tanyRestored = true\n1036\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Zone %s: restored occupancy %d (persisted %d + net crossings %+d)\",\n1037\t\t\t\t\tp.zoneID, reconciled, p.count, netPerZone[p.zoneID])\n1038\t\t\t}\n1039\t\t}\n1040\t\n1041\t\tif anyRestored {\n1042\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Occupancy restored from persisted values (uncertain until verified)\")\n1043\t\t} else {\n1044\t\t\tm.reconciled = true\n1045\t\t}\n1046\t}\n1047\t\n1048\t// ReconcileTick should be called every ~30s for the first 60s of operation.\n1049\t// It compares portal-based occupancy against live blob counts per zone.\n1050\t// If they differ by >1 for 2 consecutive checks, blob count wins.\n1051\t// After 60s of live operation, marks all occupancies as reconciled.\n1052\tfunc (m *Manager) ReconcileTick() {\n1053\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n1054\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n1055\t\n1056\t\telapsed := time.Since(m.startedAt)\n1057\t\n1058\t\t// Count blobs per zone from live positions\n1059\t\tblobCounts := make(map[string]int)\n1060\t\tfor _, pos := range m.blobPositions {\n1061\t\t\tif pos.ZoneID != \"\" {\n1062\t\t\t\tblobCounts[pos.ZoneID]++\n1063\t\t\t}\n1064\t\t}\n1065\t\n1066\t\tfor zoneID, occ := range m.occupancy {\n1067\t\t\tif occ.Status == OccupancyReconciled {\n1068\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1069\t\t\t}\n1070\t\t\tblobCount := blobCounts[zoneID]\n1071\t\t\tdiff := occ.Count - blobCount\n1072\t\t\tif diff < 0 {\n1073\t\t\t\tdiff = -diff\n1074\t\t\t}\n1075\t\n1076\t\t\tif diff > 1 {\n1077\t\t\t\tm.reconDiscrep++\n1078\t\t\t\tm.reconChecks = 0\n1079\t\t\t\tif m.reconDiscrep >= 2 {\n1080\t\t\t\t\toldCount := occ.Count\n1081\t\t\t\t\tocc.Count = blobCount\n1082\t\t\t\t\tocc.BlobIDs = nil\n1083\t\t\t\t\tocc.LastUpdated = time.Now()\n1084\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Zone %s: reconciling occupancy %d -> %d (blob count ground truth)\",\n1085\t\t\t\t\t\tzoneID, oldCount, blobCount)\n1086\t\t\t\t\tm.reconDiscrep = 0\n1087\t\t\t\t}\n1088\t\t\t} else if diff == 0 {\n1089\t\t\t\t// Exact match — mark reconciled after 2 consecutive checks\n1090\t\t\t\tm.reconChecks++\n1091\t\t\t\tm.reconDiscrep = 0\n1092\t\t\t\tif m.reconChecks >= 2 {\n1093\t\t\t\t\tocc.Status = OccupancyReconciled\n1094\t\t\t\t}\n1095\t\t\t} else {\n1096\t\t\t\t// diff == 1: close but not exact, stay uncertain\n1097\t\t\t\tm.reconChecks = 0\n1098\t\t\t\tm.reconDiscrep = 0\n1099\t\t\t}\n1100\t\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t\t// Also mark zones with no occupancy entry as reconciled\n1103\t\tfor zoneID := range m.zones {\n1104\t\t\tif _, exists := m.occupancy[zoneID]; !exists {\n1105\t\t\t\tm.occupancy[zoneID] = &ZoneOccupancy{\n1106\t\t\t\t\tZoneID:      zoneID,\n1107\t\t\t\t\tCount:       0,\n1108\t\t\t\t\tBlobIDs:     nil,\n1109\t\t\t\t\tLastUpdated: time.Now(),\n1110\t\t\t\t\tStatus:      OccupancyReconciled,\n1111\t\t\t\t}\n1112\t\t\t}\n1113\t\t}\n1114\t\n1115\t\t// After 60s, force-reconcile everything\n1116\t\tif elapsed >= 60*time.Second {\n1117\t\t\tfor _, occ := range m.occupancy {\n1118\t\t\t\tif occ.Status == OccupancyUncertain {\n1119\t\t\t\t\tocc.Status = OccupancyReconciled\n1120\t\t\t\t\tocc.Count = blobCounts[occ.ZoneID]\n1121\t\t\t\t\tocc.BlobIDs = nil\n1122\t\t\t\t\tocc.LastUpdated = time.Now()\n1123\t\t\t\t}\n1124\t\t\t}\n1125\t\t\tif !m.reconciled {\n1126\t\t\t\tm.reconciled = true\n1127\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Occupancy reconciliation complete (60s elapsed)\")\n1128\t\t\t}\n1129\t\t\treturn\n1130\t\t}\n1131\t\n1132\t\tif !m.reconciled {\n1133\t\t\tallReconciled := true\n1134\t\t\tfor _, occ := range m.occupancy {\n1135\t\t\t\tif occ.Status != OccupancyReconciled {\n1136\t\t\t\t\tallReconciled = false\n1137\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1138\t\t\t\t}\n1139\t\t\t}\n1140\t\t\tif allReconciled && len(m.occupancy) > 0 {\n1141\t\t\t\tm.reconciled = true\n1142\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Occupancy reconciliation complete (all zones verified)\")\n1143\t\t\t}\n1144\t\t}\n1145\t}\n1146\t\n1147\t// PersistOccupancy writes current occupancy counts to SQLite for restart recovery.\n1148\t// Should be called on graceful shutdown and periodically.\n1149\tfunc (m *Manager) PersistOccupancy() error {\n1150\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n1151\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n1152\t\n1153\t\tnowMs := time.Now().UnixMilli()\n1154\t\tfor zoneID, occ := range m.occupancy {\n1155\t\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`\n1156\t\t\t\tUPDATE zones SET last_known_occupancy = ?, occupancy_updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?\n1157\t\t\t`, occ.Count, nowMs, zoneID)\n1158\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1159\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"persist occupancy for zone %s: %w\", zoneID, err)\n1160\t\t\t}\n1161\t\t}\n1162\t\treturn nil\n1163\t}\n1164\t\n1165\t// PersistZoneOccupancy updates the persisted occupancy for a single zone.\n1166\tfunc (m *Manager) PersistZoneOccupancy(zoneID string) error {\n1167\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n1168\t\tocc, exists := m.occupancy[zoneID]\n1169\t\tm.mu.RUnlock()\n1170\t\n1171\t\tif !exists {\n1172\t\t\treturn nil\n1173\t\t}\n1174\t\n1175\t\tnowMs := time.Now().UnixMilli()\n1176\t\t_, err := m.db.Exec(`\n1177\t\t\tUPDATE zones SET last_known_occupancy = ?, occupancy_updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?\n1178\t\t`, occ.Count, nowMs, zoneID)\n1179\t\treturn err\n1180\t}\n1181\t\n1182\t// IsReconciled returns whether the initial occupancy reconciliation is complete.\n1183\tfunc (m *Manager) IsReconciled() bool {\n1184\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n1185\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n1186\t\treturn m.reconciled\n1187\t}\n1188\t\n1189\t// GetOccupancyStatus returns the status map for all zones.\n1190\tfunc (m *Manager) GetOccupancyStatus() map[string]OccupancyStatus {\n1191\t\tm.mu.RLock()\n1192\t\tdefer m.mu.RUnlock()\n1193\t\n1194\t\tresult := make(map[string]OccupancyStatus,
